- READY dka_hhs_v1: DKA/HHS or hyperglycemic crisis
  - readiness: Complete validated workup
  - safety 8, history 10, core exam 8, conditional exam 0, tests 10, red flags 6, management 7, evidence/LR 53, catalog gaps 0, retrieved 53, suppressed 5
  - module: Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS (hyperglycemia_possible_dka_v1); sources: ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026
  - issues: none
  - review notes: none
  - safety labels: Measure blood pressure; Measure heart rate; Measure respiratory rate; Measure oxygen saturation; Measure temperature; Measure current weight if available; Assess mental status; Check ability to protect airway
  - core exam labels: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration; Check skin turgor; Observe Kussmaul breathing; Check capillary refill; Palpate peripheral pulses; Check distal extremity warmth; Palpate abdomen for tenderness; Palpate for abdominal guarding
  - tests/reference thresholds: Point-of-care glucose now; Blood beta-hydroxybutyrate preferred; urine ketones only if blood ketones are unavailable; Basic metabolic panel/electrolytes with sodium, potassium, chloride, bicarbonate, BUN, creatinine, glucose, anion gap, and corrected sodium; Venous pH/blood gas for acidosis severity; arterial gas only when oxygenation/ventilation concern or local protocol requires it; Calculate effective osmolality and measure serum osmolality when HHS/mixed crisis, severe dehydration, altered mental status, or very high glucose is possible; ECG and potassium-focused monitoring if potassium abnormality, weakness, renal failure, or insulin therapy is expected; ...
  - management-changing findings: Suspected DKA/HHS is not routine outpatient hyperglycemia; use urgent evaluation with protocolized fluids, insulin, electrolyte replacement, and precipitant treatment.; ICU/high-acuity care is appropriate for severe DKA, HHS, shock, altered mental status, critical precipitating illness, severe electrolyte disturbance, or inability to monitor safely.; Uncomplicated mild/moderate DKA may be managed in ED observation or step-down with close nursing, serial labs, and IV or protocolized subcutaneous rapid-acting insulin; severe/complicated DKA and HHS need higher acuity.; Do not start or continue insulin infusion without potassium-aware protocol monitoring; if K <3.5 mmol/L, replace potassium and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L.; For HHS, avoid overly rapid glucose, sodium, or osmolality correction; monitor neurologic status and osmolality closely.; Transition off IV insulin only after biochemical resolution and clinical stability; use basal-bolus insulin with basal overlap 1-2 h before stopping IV insulin, and do not use anion gap alone as the resolution criterion.; ...

  - tests/reference thresholds: IGF-1 age/sex-adjusted.; Oral glucose GH suppression test: failure to suppress below assay cutoff, often <1 ng/mL or lower with modern assays.; Pituitary MRI after biochemical evidence.; A1c/BP/OSA/echo/colon risk screening.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; GH-excess safety tests: check age-adjusted IGF-1, oral-glucose GH suppression when needed, pituitary MRI after biochemical confirmation, and glucose/A1c, sleep apnea, BP/cardiac, colon, and thyroid risk screening.; ...
  - management-changing findings: Elevated IGF-1 plus inadequate GH suppression changes to pituitary MRI/surgical pathway.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency.; Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway.

  - tests/reference thresholds: First confirm endogenous Cushing with UFC, late-night salivary cortisol, or 1-mg DST cortisol >1.8 ug/dL.; ACTH: normal/high supports ACTH-dependent disease.; Pituitary MRI.; Inferior petrosal sinus sampling when MRI equivocal/discordant.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; Cushing safety tests: use late-night salivary cortisol, 1-mg dexamethasone suppression, or 24-hour urinary free cortisol after excluding exogenous steroids, and assess glucose, BP, potassium, infection/VTE, and bone risk to determine severity and treatment urgency.; ...
  - management-changing findings: ACTH-dependent confirmed Cushing plus pituitary localization changes to pituitary surgery pathway; discordance triggers IPSS.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency.; Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway.

  - readiness: Complete validated workup

  - core exam labels: Inspect skin for bruising; Test chair-rise strength; Inspect abdomen for purple striae; Inspect supraclavicular fullness
  - tests/reference thresholds: Exclude exogenous steroids.; 1-mg overnight DST: cortisol >1.8 ug/dL abnormal/sensitive cutoff.; Late-night salivary cortisol, often two samples.; 24-hour urinary free cortisol, at least two collections.; ACTH after confirmed hypercortisolism.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; ...
  - management-changing findings: Abnormal screening plus convincing phenotype triggers confirmatory/endocrine pathway; ACTH directs adrenal vs ACTH-dependent workup.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable.; Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning.

  - management-changing findings: Hypernatremia or impaired water access changes to urgent monitored fluid/desmopressin pathway.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency.; Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway.

  - management-changing findings: >3x ULN metanephrines changes to localization/genetic/preoperative alpha-blockade pathway.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable.; Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning.

## Structured Workups

### dka_hhs_v1: DKA/HHS or hyperglycemic crisis

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: dka_hhs
Installed module: Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS (hyperglycemia_possible_dka_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (dka_measure_bp) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Blood pressure anchors hemodynamic safety and helps identify shock, hypertensive urgency, orthostasis, or medication-risk context before focused diagnosis-specific decisions.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (dka_measure_hr) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Heart rate anchors physiologic severity and can reveal adrenergic state, hypovolemia, arrhythmia, infection severity, medication effect, or decompensation.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure respiratory rate (dka_measure_rr) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Respiratory rate and visible breathing effort are early bedside markers of respiratory failure, metabolic stress, sepsis physiology, or endocrine crisis severity.; options ___/min / Normal effort / Tachypneic/labored / Bradypneic/tiring / Not available; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure oxygen saturation (dka_measure_spo2) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Oxygenation separates stable outpatient-style assessment from possible respiratory failure, pulmonary source, shock physiology, or need for urgent support.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen / Hypoxemic / Escalating oxygen need / Not available; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Measure temperature (dka_measure_temperature) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Temperature confirms fever or hypothermia and helps frame infection, inflammatory illness, endocrine crisis, or treatment urgency.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
6. Measure current weight if available (dka_measure_weight) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Anthropometrics support cardiometabolic risk, medication dosing or safety, and longitudinal disease-severity interpretation.; options ___/min / Normal effort / Tachypneic/labored / Bradypneic/tiring / Not available; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
7. Assess mental status (dka_assess_mental_status) - encephalopathy, airway-protection risk, or high-acuity disposition need This basic acuity check helps identify severity, airway/safety risk, escalation needs, and safe interpretation of the selected validated workup.; options Baseline/alert / Confused / Somnolent / Agitated / Unable to assess; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
8. Check ability to protect airway (dka_check_airway_protection) - Airway protection is an acuity and safety assessment, not a core diagnostic maneuver; abnormal findings change respiratory support, aspiration precautions, and monitored-care disposition.; options Protecting airway / Questionable protection / Not protecting airway / Unable to assess; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Known diabetes type, duration, insulin regimen, pump/CGM use, and last insulin dose? - Clarifies diabetes context, hyperglycemic-crisis probability, severity, and treatment safety for DKA/HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024
2. Recent glucose values, beta-hydroxybutyrate/urine ketones, anion gap, bicarbonate, pH, potassium, creatinine, and osmolality if known? - Clarifies DKA/HHS diagnostic criteria, crisis severity, electrolyte safety, and whether current treatment matches guideline-based monitoring needs.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; details Ask specifically about Recent glucose values. | Ask specifically about beta-hydroxybutyrate/urine ketones. | Ask specifically about anion gap. | Ask specifically about bicarbonate. | Ask specifically about potassium. | Ask specifically about creatinine. | Ask specifically about osmolality if known.
3. Missed or reduced insulin, pump/infusion-set failure, empty reservoir, expired insulin, medication access issue, or recent dose change? - Identifies the precipitant or recurrence risk that changes acute correction, infection search, education, and discharge safety planning.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; details Ask specifically about Missed. | Ask specifically about reduced insulin. | Ask specifically about pump/infusion-set failure. | Ask specifically about empty reservoir. | Ask specifically about expired insulin. | Ask specifically about medication access issue. | Ask specifically about recent dose change.
4. Polyuria, polydipsia, weight loss, dehydration, or very dry mouth? - Screens for hyperglycemic-crisis severity, euglycemic DKA risk, dehydration, HHS features, and special-population safety constraints.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024
5. Vomiting, abdominal pain, poor oral intake, or inability to keep fluids down? - Screens for hyperglycemic-crisis severity, euglycemic DKA risk, dehydration, HHS features, and special-population safety constraints.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024
6. Fever, dysuria, cough, wound, line infection, dental/skin infection, chest pain, stroke symptoms, pancreatitis symptoms, steroids, or other trigger? - Identifies the precipitant or recurrence risk that changes acute correction, infection search, education, and discharge safety planning.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; details Ask specifically about Fever. | Ask specifically about dysuria. | Ask specifically about cough. | Ask specifically about wound. | Ask specifically about line infection. | Ask specifically about dental/skin infection. | Ask specifically about chest pain. | Ask specifically about stroke symptoms.
7. Any confusion, sleepiness, seizure, severe weakness, or inability to participate in care? - Screens for hyperglycemic-crisis severity, euglycemic DKA risk, dehydration, HHS features, and special-population safety constraints.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; details Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about sleepiness. | Ask specifically about seizure. | Ask specifically about severe weakness. | Ask specifically about inability to participate in care.
8. SGLT2 inhibitor use, pregnancy/postpartum state, fasting/low-carb intake, alcohol, toxin exposure, or recent surgery? - Screens for hyperglycemic-crisis severity, euglycemic DKA risk, dehydration, HHS features, and special-population safety constraints.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
9. Heart failure, CKD/ESKD, frailty, cirrhosis, pregnancy, or other reason to use smaller fluid boluses and closer electrolyte monitoring? - Screens for hyperglycemic-crisis severity, euglycemic DKA risk, dehydration, HHS features, and special-population safety constraints.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; details Ask specifically about Heart failure. | Ask specifically about CKD/ESKD. | Ask specifically about frailty. | Ask specifically about cirrhosis. | Ask specifically about pregnancy. | Ask specifically about other reason to use smaller fluid boluses. | Ask specifically about closer electrolyte monitoring.
10. What has already been given: fluids, insulin route/rate, potassium/phosphate, dextrose, bicarbonate, antibiotics, and current monitoring level? - Clarifies DKA/HHS diagnostic criteria, crisis severity, electrolyte safety, and whether current treatment matches guideline-based monitoring needs.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; details Ask specifically about What has already been given: fluids. | Ask specifically about insulin route/rate. | Ask specifically about potassium/phosphate. | Ask specifically about dextrose. | Ask specifically about bicarbonate. | Ask specifically about antibiotics. | Ask specifically about current monitoring level.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration (dka_inspect_mucous_membranes) - Document dry mucous membranes because this changes fluid deficit assessment and monitoring urgency.; options Moist / Dry / Very dry / Unable to assess; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Check skin turgor (dka_assess_skin_turgor) - Document poor skin turgor because this supports clinically important volume depletion and fluid replacement urgency.; options Normal recoil / Reduced recoil / Unable to assess; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe Kussmaul breathing (dka_assess_kussmaul_breathing) - Document deep labored respirations because this changes acidosis severity assessment and urgency of crisis management.; options Normal effort / Increased work / Kussmaul/deep respirations / Unable to assess; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Check capillary refill (dka_check_capillary_refill) - Document delayed refill because this changes shock assessment, fluid urgency, and monitored-care level.; options Normal/warm / Delayed/cool / Mottled/poor perfusion / Unable to assess; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Palpate peripheral pulses (dka_palpate_peripheral_pulses) - Document weak pulses because this changes perfusion assessment and shock concern.; options Symmetric/normal / Diminished / Absent / Asymmetric / Unable to assess; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Check distal extremity warmth (dka_assess_extremity_temperature) - Document cool extremities because this changes shock assessment and perfusion monitoring.; options Normal/warm / Delayed/cool / Mottled/poor perfusion / Unable to assess; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Palpate abdomen for tenderness (dka_palpate_abdomen) - Document focal tenderness because this changes concern for pancreatitis, surgical abdomen, infection, or alternate precipitant.; options Nontender / Tender / Guarding / Rebound/peritoneal concern / Unable to assess; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
8. Palpate for abdominal guarding (dka_assess_abdominal_guarding) - Document guarding because this changes surgical mimic concern and escalation pathway.; options Nontender / Tender / Guarding / Rebound/peritoneal concern / Unable to assess; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Suspected DKA/HHS is not routine outpatient hyperglycemia; use urgent evaluation with protocolized fluids, insulin, electrolyte replacement, and precipitant treatment. (dka_emergency) - This distinction changes disposition and timing because DKA/HHS management depends on serial labs, potassium-safe insulin, fluid resuscitation, and precipitant control.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. ICU/high-acuity care is appropriate for severe DKA, HHS, shock, altered mental status, critical precipitating illness, severe electrolyte disturbance, or inability to monitor safely. (dka_high_acuity) - These features change level of care because deterioration, electrolyte shifts, neurologic risk, and treatment intensity require close nursing and serial reassessment.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Uncomplicated mild/moderate DKA may be managed in ED observation or step-down with close nursing, serial labs, and IV or protocolized subcutaneous rapid-acting insulin; severe/complicated DKA and HHS need higher acuity. (dka_mild_moderate_dka) - Severity and monitoring capacity change whether treatment can be safely delivered outside ICU versus needing high-acuity care.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Do not start or continue insulin infusion without potassium-aware protocol monitoring; if K <3.5 mmol/L, replace potassium and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L. (dka_potassium_rule) - Potassium status changes immediate insulin safety because insulin can worsen hypokalemia and precipitate dangerous arrhythmias.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. For HHS, avoid overly rapid glucose, sodium, or osmolality correction; monitor neurologic status and osmolality closely. (dka_hhs_correction) - Correction rate changes neurologic safety and fluid/insulin strategy in HHS, especially when mental status or hyperosmolarity is prominent.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Transition off IV insulin only after biochemical resolution and clinical stability; use basal-bolus insulin with basal overlap 1-2 h before stopping IV insulin, and do not use anion gap alone as the resolution criterion. (dka_resolution_transition) - Resolution criteria and basal overlap change recurrence risk, hypoglycemia risk, and safe transfer/discharge readiness.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
7. Before discharge, confirm insulin and diabetes supplies, ketone testing plan, sick-day rules, glucagon when indicated, medication affordability, education, and close follow-up; address recurrent DKA or social/mental-health barriers. (dka_discharge_prevention) - Discharge readiness changes recurrence prevention, medication safety, and follow-up urgency after hyperglycemic crisis.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (dka_measure_bp_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (dka_measure_hr_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure respiratory rate (dka_measure_rr_limitation) - Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure oxygen saturation (dka_measure_spo2_limitation) - Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Measure temperature (dka_measure_temperature_limitation) - Temperature depends on measurement site, antipyretics, timing, immune status, and device accuracy; absence of fever does not exclude serious infection or endocrine crisis.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
6. Measure current weight if available (dka_measure_weight_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
7. Assess mental status (dka_assess_mental_status_limitation) - Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
8. Check ability to protect airway (dka_check_airway_protection_limitation) - Do not use provocative unsafe maneuvers; interpret with mental status, respiratory pattern, secretion handling, aspiration risk, local airway protocols, and clinical trajectory.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

21. 22. ECG and potassium-focused monitoring if potassium abnormality, weakness, renal failure, or insulin therapy is expected (dka_ecg_potassium_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS test or threshold.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. CBC with differential plus magnesium and phosphate when crisis is suspected or insulin therapy is expected (dka_cbc_phos_mg_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS test or threshold.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Precipitant workup guided by presentation: urinalysis/culture, blood cultures, chest imaging, viral testing, pregnancy test, lipase, troponin/ECG, neuroimaging, or other source evaluation (dka_trigger_tests_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS test or threshold.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Hemoglobin A1c if no reliable result in the prior 3 months or if baseline control is unclear (dka_a1c_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS test or threshold.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. If treating DKA/HHS: glucose every 1-2 h; electrolytes, creatinine, phosphate, beta-hydroxybutyrate, and venous pH about every 4 h until DKA resolves; osmolality every 4 h in HHS (dka_serial_monitoring_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS test or threshold.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Suspected DKA/HHS is not routine outpatient hyperglycemia; use urgent evaluation with protocolized fluids, insulin, electrolyte replacement, and precipitant treatment. (dka_emergency_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
28. ICU/high-acuity care is appropriate for severe DKA, HHS, shock, altered mental status, critical precipitating illness, severe electrolyte disturbance, or inability to monitor safely. (dka_high_acuity_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
29. Uncomplicated mild/moderate DKA may be managed in ED observation or step-down with close nursing, serial labs, and IV or protocolized subcutaneous rapid-acting insulin; severe/complicated DKA and HHS need higher acuity. (dka_mild_moderate_dka_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Do not start or continue insulin infusion without potassium-aware protocol monitoring; if K <3.5 mmol/L, replace potassium and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L. (dka_potassium_rule_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. For HHS, avoid overly rapid glucose, sodium, or osmolality correction; monitor neurologic status and osmolality closely. (dka_hhs_correction_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Transition off IV insulin only after biochemical resolution and clinical stability; use basal-bolus insulin with basal overlap 1-2 h before stopping IV insulin, and do not use anion gap alone as the resolution criterion. (dka_resolution_transition_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Before discharge, confirm insulin and diabetes supplies, ketone testing plan, sick-day rules, glucagon when indicated, medication affordability, education, and close follow-up; address recurrent DKA or social/mental-health barriers. (dka_discharge_prevention_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. 37. SGLT2 inhibitor, pregnancy, fasting, or lower glucose with ketotic symptoms (dka_euglycemic_risk_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

39. Pregnancy, frailty/older adult, heart failure, advanced kidney disease, or major comorbidity affecting fluids/insulin (dka_special_population_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
40. DKA: diabetes/prior diabetes or glucose >=200 mg/dL, beta-hydroxybutyrate >=3.0 mmol/L or urine ketones >=2+, and pH <7.3 and/or bicarbonate <18 mmol/L; can be euglycemic with SGLT2, pregnancy, fasting, or poor intake (dka_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
41. HHS: glucose >=600 mg/dL, effective osmolality >300 mOsm/kg or total osmolality >320 mOsm/kg, absent significant ketonemia, and no significant acidosis; assess for mixed DKA/HHS (hhs_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
42. Precipitating illness or barrier: infection, insulin omission/access issue, pump failure, MI/stroke, pancreatitis, glucocorticoids/other medications, pregnancy, alcohol, or other physiologic stress (trigger_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
43. Hyperglycemia without crisis: persistent inpatient glucose >=180 mg/dL generally needs insulin initiation/intensification, while mild stable T2DM may be managed less intensively per inpatient protocol (noncrisis_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Visual acuity (dka_hhs_v1_suppress_visual_acuity_3) - Visual acuity is outside the validated scope for DKA/HHS or hyperglycemic crisis unless patient modifiers or another validated intent explicitly support that organ-system context.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; authorization validated_clinical_intent_suppress_rule
4. Ophthalmoscopic exam (dka_hhs_v1_suppress_ophthalmoscopic_exam_4) - Ophthalmoscopic exam is outside the validated scope for DKA/HHS or hyperglycemic crisis unless patient modifiers or another validated intent explicitly support that organ-system context.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; authorization validated_clinical_intent_suppress_rule
5. Pronator drift (dka_hhs_v1_suppress_pronator_drift_5) - Pronator drift is outside the validated scope for DKA/HHS or hyperglycemic crisis unless patient modifiers or another validated intent explicitly support that organ-system context.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; authorization validated_clinical_intent_suppress_rule

### suspected_pe_v1: Suspected pulmonary embolism

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: suspected_pe; chest_pain

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Heart rate (SAFETY-validated-heart-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Respiratory rate (SAFETY-validated-respiratory-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Normal / Tachypneic / Bradypneic / Labored / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Oxygen saturation / support (SAFETY-validated-oxygen-saturation) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen ___ L/min / Hypoxemic / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any sudden shortness of breath, pleuritic chest pain, hemoptysis, syncope, prior VTE, recent surgery/immobility/travel, estrogen or pregnancy/postpartum state, cancer, unilateral leg swelling or pain, or bleeding risk? - PE/DVT probability and treatment safety: cardiopulmonary symptoms, VTE risk factors, prior clot, DVT symptoms, and anticoagulation or thrombolysis contraindications.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
2. Any sudden dyspnea, pleuritic pain, hemoptysis, syncope, escalating oxygen need, or inability to speak full sentences? - Respiratory severity in suspected PE: tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, inability to speak, cyanosis, fatigue, or impending respiratory failure.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ATS_CAP_2025; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask specifically about sudden dyspnea. | Ask specifically about pleuritic pain. | Ask specifically about hemoptysis. | Ask specifically about syncope. | Ask specifically about escalating oxygen need. | Ask specifically about inability to speak full sentences.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Auscultate posterior lung fields (EXAM-092-posterior-lung-sounds) - Checks work of breathing and alternate pulmonary diagnoses while PE remains on the table.; options Clear / Crackles / Wheezes / Rhonchi / Diminished; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - Assesses rhythm, murmur, heart failure/strain, and perfusion clues.; options Normal / Murmur / Rub / Gallop / Other ___; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - Assesses rhythm, murmur, heart failure/strain, and perfusion clues.; options Not elevated / Elevated / Unable to assess; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
4. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - Assesses rhythm, murmur, heart failure/strain, and perfusion clues.; options Equal / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Observe work of breathing (REQ-pe-work-of-breathing) - Suspected PE workups need a direct respiratory-severity check before lower-yield pulmonary maneuvers, because distress and hypoxemia change acuity even before imaging confirms PE.; options Comfortable / Tachypneic / Labored / Accessory muscle use / Unable to speak full sentences / Cyanotic / Unable; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ATS_CAP_2025; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect unilateral leg swelling (REQ-pe-unilateral-leg-swelling-inspection) - Suspected PE workups should compare legs for DVT-compatible findings instead of relying only on history, because a proximal DVT clue changes VTE probability and imaging/treatment framing.; options Symmetric / Right more swollen / Left more swollen / Calf tenderness / Erythema-warmth / Unable; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Auscultate posterior lung fields (EXAM-092-posterior-lung-sounds) - Respiratory distress, wheeze, crackles, focal diminished sounds, or other findings that point to PE severity or competing lung diagnoses.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - murmur, abnormal heart sound, rhythm irregularity, or S3/S4 clue; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - elevated right-sided filling pressure or venous congestion; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
4. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - perfusion deficit, vascular disease, or limb ischemia clue; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Observe work of breathing (REQ-pe-work-of-breathing) - Respiratory severity in suspected PE: tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, inability to speak, cyanosis, fatigue, or impending respiratory failure.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ATS_CAP_2025; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect unilateral leg swelling (REQ-pe-unilateral-leg-swelling-inspection) - DVT-compatible bedside clue: asymmetric calf or leg swelling, focal calf tenderness, erythema/warmth, or an alternate limb process.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. PE probability, D-dimer, and imaging pathway (REQ-pe-probability-d-dimer-imaging) - Diagnostic pathway selection: low/intermediate probability with D-dimer rule-out potential versus high probability or unstable PE requiring imaging/escalation.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
8. DVT and right-heart strain evaluation (REQ-pe-dvt-rv-strain-workup) - VTE confirmation and PE severity: proximal DVT evidence, right ventricular strain, myocardial injury, hemodynamic compromise, and oxygenation trend.; source ESC_PE_2019; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
9. High-risk PE escalation cues (REQ-pe-hemodynamic-escalation-cues) - High-risk PE physiology: shock, hypotension, syncope, severe hypoxemia, altered mentation, persistent tachycardia/tachypnea, RV strain, or arrest.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
10. Anticoagulation and thrombolysis safety cues (REQ-pe-anticoagulation-safety-cues) - Treatment-risk context: bleeding risk, recent surgery/trauma, intracranial risk, severe hypertension, pregnancy/postpartum, renal/contrast limitation, fall risk, or pending procedure.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

Suppressed/not-recommended items
1. Respiratory rate (EXAM-087-respiratory-rate) - Replaced by the validated respiratory rate safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.; source Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; vital-sign trend interpreted in clinical context; authorization validated_clinical_intent
2. Auscultate lateral lung fields (EXAM-093-lateral-lung-sounds) - Not recommended separately because Auscultate posterior lung fields covers the same bedside exam family; add a patient-specific modifier before using this lower-priority variant.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent
3. Aortic area (EXAM-109-aortic-area) - Individual valve-area auscultation cards are not recommended separately unless murmur, valvular disease, endocarditis, chest pain, heart failure, or a focused cardiac-auscultation modifier is present; use the broader heart-sounds item first.; source Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; authorization validated_clinical_intent
4. Pulmonic area (EXAM-110-pulmonic-area) - Individual valve-area auscultation cards are not recommended separately unless murmur, valvular disease, endocarditis, chest pain, heart failure, or a focused cardiac-auscultation modifier is present; use the broader heart-sounds item first.; source Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; authorization validated_clinical_intent
5. 6. Mitral area (EXAM-112-mitral-area) - Individual valve-area auscultation cards are not recommended separately unless murmur, valvular disease, endocarditis, chest pain, heart failure, or a focused cardiac-auscultation modifier is present; use the broader heart-sounds item first.; source Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; authorization validated_clinical_intent
7. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-086-heart-rate) - Replaced by the validated heart rate safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.; source Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; vital-sign trend interpreted in clinical context; authorization validated_clinical_intent
8. Palpate point of maximal impulse (EXAM-107-pmi) - PMI/apical impulse is reserved for cardiac, heart-failure, or volume-overload contexts.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac exam resources; authorization validated_clinical_intent
9. Blood pressure (EXAM-088-blood-pressure) - Replaced by the validated blood pressure safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.; source Stanford Medicine 25 blood pressure measurement techniques; Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; authorization validated_clinical_intent
10. Inspect posterior thorax (EXAM-089-posterior-thorax-inspection) - Not promoted because this maneuver lacks a direct syndrome, organ-system, management, or feasibility match for the selected validated workup; use only after adding a specific patient trigger or secondary validated intent.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent

12. Tactile fremitus (EXAM-090-tactile-fremitus) - Not promoted because the selected workup already uses the highest-yield respiratory maneuvers; extra lung-field, percussion, or fremitus rows are conditional add-ons for focal consolidation, effusion, or unclear pulmonary source.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent
13. Percuss Anterior lung (EXAM-095-anterior-lung-percussion) - Not promoted because the selected workup already uses the highest-yield respiratory maneuvers; extra lung-field, percussion, or fremitus rows are conditional add-ons for focal consolidation, effusion, or unclear pulmonary source.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent
14. Stethoscope cleaned (EXAM-085-stethoscope-cleaned) - Technique/setup rows are audit metadata, not a patient-specific recommended exam item.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent
15. Auscultate anterior lung fields (EXAM-096-anterior-lung-sounds) - Not recommended separately because Auscultate posterior lung fields covers the same bedside exam family; add a patient-specific modifier before using this lower-priority variant.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent
16. Test Knee flexion strength (EXAM-026-knee-flexion-strength) - Neuro, gait, strength, sensory, reflex, or coordination maneuvers need focal neurologic deficit, weakness, sensory loss, ataxia/gait concern, cord/radicular symptoms, neuropathy, or diabetic-foot context.; source JAMA_RCE; authorization validated_clinical_intent

18. 23. Vascular exam setup (EXAM-097-vascular-exam-setup) - Technique/setup rows are audit metadata, not a patient-specific recommended exam item.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; authorization validated_clinical_intent
24. Cardiac exam setup (EXAM-105-cardiac-exam-setup) - Technique/setup rows are audit metadata, not a patient-specific recommended exam item.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac exam resources; authorization validated_clinical_intent

### chest_pain_acs_v1: Chest pain / ACS-style presentation

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: chest_pain
Installed module: Chest pain (chest_pain_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (cp_measure_bp) - Chest pain: Blood pressure anchors hemodynamic safety and helps identify shock, hypertensive urgency, orthostasis, or medication-risk context before focused diagnosis-specific decisions.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (cp_measure_hr) - Chest pain: Heart rate anchors physiologic severity and can reveal adrenergic state, hypovolemia, arrhythmia, infection severity, medication effect, or decompensation.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure respiratory rate (cp_measure_rr) - Chest pain: Respiratory rate and visible breathing effort are early bedside markers of respiratory failure, metabolic stress, sepsis physiology, or endocrine crisis severity.; options ___/min / Normal effort / Tachypneic/labored / Bradypneic/tiring / Not available; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure oxygen saturation (cp_measure_spo2) - Chest pain: Oxygenation separates stable outpatient-style assessment from possible respiratory failure, pulmonary source, shock physiology, or need for urgent support.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen / Hypoxemic / Escalating oxygen need / Not available; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Assess general appearance (cp_assess_general_appearance) - Chest pain: Global acuity and perfusion findings identify patients who need urgent escalation before a routine focused workup is completed.; options Well-appearing / Ill-appearing / Toxic/unstable appearing / Unable to assess; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. When did the chest discomfort start, and is it ongoing? - Clarifies ischemic probability, symptom tempo, risk pathway selection, and urgent ECG/troponin testing needs for chest pain.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021
2. What does it feel like, where is it, and does it radiate to arm, jaw, back, or shoulder? - Clarifies ischemic probability, symptom tempo, risk pathway selection, and urgent ECG/troponin testing needs for chest pain.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; details Ask specifically about What does it feel like. | Ask specifically about where is it. | Ask specifically about does it radiate to arm. | Ask specifically about back. | Ask specifically about shoulder.
3. Is it exertional, relieved by rest, or similar to prior angina? - Clarifies ischemic probability, symptom tempo, risk pathway selection, and urgent ECG/troponin testing needs for chest pain.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021
4. Any shortness of breath, pleuritic pain, cough, hemoptysis, or oxygen requirement? - Screens for PE, pneumothorax, pneumonia, heart failure, and other cardiopulmonary mimics that change testing pathway and disposition.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; details Ask specifically about shortness of breath. | Ask specifically about pleuritic pain. | Ask specifically about cough. | Ask specifically about hemoptysis. | Ask specifically about oxygen requirement.
5. Any diaphoresis, nausea/vomiting, palpitations, or marked weakness? - Clarifies associated autonomic or cardiopulmonary symptoms that increase concern for ACS or another high-acuity chest-pain cause.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; details Ask specifically about diaphoresis. | Ask specifically about nausea/vomiting. | Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about marked weakness.
6. Any syncope, presyncope, neurologic symptoms, or sudden maximal pain? - Screens for high-risk red flags such as syncope, neurologic symptoms, sudden maximal pain, or aortic-syndrome features that require urgent escalation.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; details Ask specifically about syncope. | Ask specifically about presyncope. | Ask specifically about neurologic symptoms. | Ask specifically about sudden maximal pain.
7. Known CAD, prior MI/stent/CABG, diabetes, CKD, smoking, hypertension, or strong family history? - Clarifies ischemic probability, symptom tempo, risk pathway selection, and urgent ECG/troponin testing needs for chest pain.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; details Ask specifically about Known CAD. | Ask specifically about prior MI/stent/CABG. | Ask specifically about diabetes. | Ask specifically about smoking. | Ask specifically about hypertension. | Ask specifically about strong family history.
8. Recent cocaine/stimulant use, anticoagulants, estrogen therapy, or medication changes? - Identifies stimulant, anticoagulant, estrogen, or medication exposures that change ACS, PE, bleeding, and treatment-safety assessment.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Auscultate heart rhythm (cp_auscultate_heart_rhythm) - Document irregular rhythm because atrial fibrillation or other arrhythmia changes ECG interpretation, monitoring, and management.; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Auscultate for cardiac murmurs (cp_auscultate_murmurs) - Document a new murmur because it changes concern for valvular disease, mechanical complication, or aortic pathology.; options No murmur / Systolic murmur / Diastolic murmur / New/changed murmur / Unable to assess; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate for gallop (cp_auscultate_gallop) - Document an S3 or S4 when present because it changes heart failure or ischemic risk framing.; options No gallop / S3 / S4 / Unable to assess; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe work of breathing (cp_assess_work_of_breathing) - Document increased work of breathing because it changes respiratory support needs and cardiopulmonary emergency concern.; options Normal effort / Increased work / Kussmaul/deep respirations / Unable to assess; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Auscultate lung sounds (cp_auscultate_lung_sounds) - Document wheeze, crackles, or asymmetric breath sounds because these change heart failure, obstructive lung disease, pneumonia, or pneumothorax concern.; options Clear / Crackles / Wheeze / Diminished/asymmetric / Unable to assess; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Palpate radial pulses (cp_palpate_radial_pulses) - Document diminished or asymmetric pulses because this changes concern for shock or acute aortic syndrome.; options Symmetric/normal / Diminished / Absent / Asymmetric / Unable to assess; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Inspect and palpate extremity perfusion (cp_assess_extremity_perfusion) - Document cool extremities, delayed capillary refill, or poor perfusion because these change shock assessment and escalation.; options Normal/warm / Delayed/cool / Mottled/poor perfusion / Unable to assess; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Use a structured chest pain clinical decision pathway and risk stratification rather than gestalt alone. (cp_structured_pathway) - Structured pathways improve diagnostic safety and determine safe discharge, observation, admission, imaging, or cardiology consultation decisions.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (cp_measure_bp_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (cp_measure_hr_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure respiratory rate (cp_measure_rr_limitation) - Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure oxygen saturation (cp_measure_spo2_limitation) - Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Assess general appearance (cp_assess_general_appearance_limitation) - Global appearance and perfusion are subjective and affected by baseline function, pain, medications, temperature, and examiner experience; pair with objective vitals and reassessment.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
6. Auscultate heart rhythm (cp_auscultate_heart_rhythm_limitation) - Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
