### dyspnea_hf_v1: Dyspnea / heart failure or volume overload

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: dyspnea_hf

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; JAMA_RCE; SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Heart rate (SAFETY-validated-heart-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; JAMA_RCE; SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Respiratory rate (SAFETY-validated-respiratory-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Normal / Tachypneic / Bradypneic / Labored / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; JAMA_RCE; SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Oxygen saturation / support (SAFETY-validated-oxygen-saturation) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen ___ L/min / Hypoxemic / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; JAMA_RCE; SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; JAMA_RCE; SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. What changed with breathing: onset, exertional dyspnea, orthopnea or PND, cough/wheeze, chest pain, leg swelling, weight change, oxygen need, or missed diuretics? - Dyspnea/heart-failure source and severity: pulmonary congestion, obstructive/infectious symptoms, ischemic symptoms, oxygen escalation, or volume change.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; JAMA_RCE; SM25; details Ask specifically about What changed with breathing: onset. | Ask specifically about exertional dyspnea. | Ask specifically about orthopnea. | Ask specifically about cough/wheeze. | Ask specifically about chest pain. | Ask specifically about leg swelling. | Ask specifically about weight change. | Ask specifically about oxygen need.
2. Is breathing worse at rest, with exertion, lying flat, during sleep, or with chest pain, cough, wheeze, fever, leg swelling, or higher oxygen need? - Respiratory severity: tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, inability to speak, fatigue, cyanosis, or impending respiratory failure.; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask specifically about Is breathing worse at rest. | Ask specifically about with exertion. | Ask specifically about lying flat. | Ask specifically about during sleep. | Ask specifically about with chest pain. | Ask specifically about cough. | Ask specifically about wheeze. | Ask specifically about fever.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Auscultate posterior lung fields (EXAM-092-posterior-lung-sounds) - Differentiates congestion, wheeze, consolidation, effusion, or other pulmonary causes.; options Clear / Crackles / Wheezes / Rhonchi / Diminished; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.; options Normal / Murmur / Rub / Gallop / Other ___; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.; options Not elevated / Elevated / Unable to assess; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
4. Inspect and press lower extremity edema (EXAM-104-lower-extremity-edema) - Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.; options None / Trace / 1+ / 2+ / 3+; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.; options Equal / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect posterior thorax (EXAM-089-posterior-thorax-inspection) - Assesses respiratory effort and distress before deciding whether bedside support or escalation is needed.; options Normal / Increased work / Asymmetric / Deformity; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Observe work of breathing (REQ-dyspnea-work-of-breathing) - Dyspnea workups must start with respiratory effort because distress changes oxygen, ventilatory support, monitoring, and disposition before the etiology is fully proven.; options Comfortable / Tachypneic / Labored / Accessory muscle use / Unable to speak full sentences / Fatigued / Unable; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
1. Palpate point of maximal impulse (EXAM-107-pmi) - Adds apical impulse only when dyspnea overlaps with heart failure, cardiomyopathy, pulmonary hypertension, or volume-overload context.; options Normal / Displaced / Diffuse / Unable; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Management-changing findings
1. Auscultate posterior lung fields (EXAM-092-posterior-lung-sounds) - Lung findings: crackles, wheeze, rhonchi, diminished sounds, asymmetry, or focal ventilation abnormality.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - Heart-failure/volume clue: elevated JVP, edema, S3/gallop or murmur, irregular rhythm, or perfusion deficit.; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - Heart-failure/volume clue: elevated JVP, edema, S3/gallop or murmur, irregular rhythm, or perfusion deficit.; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
4. Inspect and press lower extremity edema (EXAM-104-lower-extremity-edema) - Heart-failure/volume clue: elevated JVP, edema, S3/gallop or murmur, irregular rhythm, or perfusion deficit.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - Heart-failure/volume clue: elevated JVP, edema, S3/gallop or murmur, irregular rhythm, or perfusion deficit.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect posterior thorax (EXAM-089-posterior-thorax-inspection) - Work of breathing: accessory muscle use, retractions, asymmetry, fatigue, or distress.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Observe work of breathing (REQ-dyspnea-work-of-breathing) - Respiratory severity: tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, inability to speak, fatigue, cyanosis, or impending respiratory failure.; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
8. Dyspnea/HF initial tests and reference thresholds (REQ-dyspnea-hf-initial-tests) - HF/dyspnea etiology and safety screen: congestion/cardiomegaly or alternative disease on CXR, rhythm/ischemia on ECG, natriuretic peptide support or exclusion of HF, renal/electrolyte safety, anemia/infection, and myocardial injury.; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
9. Diuresis and respiratory-support safety review (REQ-dyspnea-diuresis-safety-tests) - Treatment-safety data: oxygen trajectory, renal function, potassium/magnesium/sodium abnormalities, acid-base or ventilatory failure, and response to diuresis or respiratory support.; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
10. Respiratory failure escalation cues (REQ-dyspnea-respiratory-failure-cues) - Impending or established respiratory failure: severe hypoxemia, escalating oxygen, fatigue, cyanosis, altered mentation, hypercapnia, or acidemia.; source ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
11. Shock or acute heart-failure escalation cues (REQ-dyspnea-shock-acute-hf-cues) - High-risk cardiac dyspnea clue: shock, poor perfusion, oliguria, ischemia, dangerous arrhythmia, syncope, flash pulmonary edema, hypertensive emergency, or acute valvular complication.; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
12. Palpate point of maximal impulse (EXAM-107-pmi) - Precordial structural clue: displaced/diffuse apical impulse or visible heave in cardiomyopathy/volume-overload context.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

### sleep_apnea_snoring_v1: Snoring or suspected obstructive sleep apnea

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: sleep_apnea_airway

Focused history questions
1. Any drowsy driving or work-safety risk, resistant hypertension, atrial fibrillation, heart failure, opioid or sedative use, alcohol near bedtime, prior sleep study or CPAP use, CPAP intolerance, or oral-appliance interest? - OSA severity, safety risk, cardiometabolic comorbidity, medication/alcohol contributors, and treatment readiness.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AASM_OSA_DIAGNOSTIC_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; details Ask specifically about drowsy driving. | Ask specifically about work-safety risk. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about atrial fibrillation. | Ask specifically about heart failure. | Ask specifically about opioid. | Ask specifically about sedative use. | Ask specifically about alcohol near bedtime.
2. Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, waking choking, morning headache, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or drowsy driving? - OSA risk phenotype: enlarged neck circumference or upper-airway crowding in a patient with snoring, witnessed apneas, or sleepiness.; source AASM_OSA_DIAGNOSTIC_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about waking choking. | Ask specifically about morning headache. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about drowsy driving.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect oropharynx (EXAM-061-oropharynx) - Looks for visible oropharyngeal crowding or oral airway findings that support sleep-apnea risk assessment.; options Clear / Erythematous / Exudate / Swollen / Uvula deviated; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Measure neck circumference (REQ-osa-neck-circumference) - Neck circumference is a quick bedside OSA risk phenotype item and should be shown as an active exam step rather than a reviewer-only gap.; options Measured ___ cm / Enlarged by local threshold / Not measured / Unable; source AASM_OSA_DIAGNOSTIC_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Inspect oropharynx (EXAM-061-oropharynx) - Upper-airway risk clue: crowded oropharynx, large tonsils, macroglossia, or other visible obstruction pattern.; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

11. Inspect fundus with ophthalmoscope (EXAM-055-ophthalmoscopic-exam) - Eye/cranial-nerve maneuvers need vision change, eye pain/redness, diplopia, headache/papilledema, pituitary/sellar, or focal neurologic context.; source JAMA_RCE; authorization validated_clinical_intent

### abdominal_pain_cramping_v1: Abdominal pain or cramping

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: abdominal_gi

Temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; JAMA_RCE; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Where is the pain worst, what changed over time, and are there fever, persistent vomiting, blood or black stool, jaundice, urinary/flank symptoms, pregnancy possibility, syncope, severe/worsening focal pain, or inability to pass stool/gas? - Abdominal source and acuity: biliary, appendiceal, pancreatic, obstruction/ileus, GI bleed, GU/renal, pelvic/pregnancy-related, infectious/inflammatory, ischemic, or nonspecific self-limited pattern.; source AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask specifically about Where is the pain worst. | Ask specifically about what changed over time. | Ask specifically about are there fever. | Ask specifically about persistent vomiting. | Ask specifically about blood. | Ask specifically about black stool. | Ask specifically about jaundice. | Ask specifically about urinary/flank symptoms.
2. Where is the pain worst, and is it associated with vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, urinary symptoms, fever, or bleeding? - Abdominal pattern: focal tenderness, guarding, rebound/peritoneal irritation, distension, altered bowel sounds, mass, or ascites clue.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; details Ask specifically about Where is the pain worst. | Ask specifically about is it associated with vomiting. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about urinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about bleeding.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect abdomen (EXAM-073-abdominal-inspection) - Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.; options Flat / Distended / Scar / Lesion / Asymmetric; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Percuss abdomen (EXAM-075-abdominal-percussion) - Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.; options Tympanic / Dullness / Tender / Not done; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate bowel sounds (EXAM-074-bowel-sounds) - Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.; options Present / Hypoactive / Hyperactive / Absent; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Palpate abdomen (EXAM-076-abdominal-palpation) - Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.; options Nontender / Tender ___ / Guarding / Mass ___; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
1. Test rebound tenderness (EXAM-081-rebound-tenderness) - Makes peritoneal irritation explicit as a conditional abdominal-pain maneuver instead of hiding it until the user supplies perfect localization terms.; options No rebound or involuntary guarding / Rebound tenderness present / Rigidity or generalized peritonitis concern / Unable to assess; source MCGEE_EBPD; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test Murphy sign (EXAM-080-murphy-sign) - Keeps biliary disease on the abdominal-pain bedside differential; perform when pain localizes to RUQ/epigastrium or biliary features are present.; options Negative Murphy sign / RUQ inspiratory arrest or positive Murphy sign / Peritoneal or unstable exam concern / Unable to assess; source MCGEE_EBPD; SM25; WSES acute calculous cholecystitis guideline; LR+ 2.8, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.

Focused history questions
1. Could pregnancy be possible, and are there ectopic or PID danger features: missed period, positive pregnancy test, severe unilateral pelvic pain, syncope, shoulder pain, fever, purulent discharge, dyspareunia, or heavy bleeding? - Pregnancy-related emergency, ectopic-pattern symptoms, PID features, and bleeding severity.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACOG_ENDOMETRIOSIS_DIAGNOSIS_2026; CDC_STI_2021; ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; details Ask specifically about Could pregnancy be possible. | Ask specifically about are there ectopic. | Ask specifically about PID danger features: missed period. | Ask specifically about positive pregnancy test. | Ask specifically about severe unilateral pelvic pain. | Ask specifically about syncope. | Ask specifically about shoulder pain. | Ask specifically about fever.
2. Where is the pain, is it typical for your period, and are there urinary, GI, bleeding, pregnancy, fever, or discharge symptoms? - Lower-abdominal tenderness pattern, guarding, distension, or bowel abnormality that separates uncomplicated cramps from acute abdomen/GU/pelvic pathology.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; details Ask specifically about Where is the pain. | Ask specifically about is it typical for your period. | Ask specifically about are there urinary. | Ask specifically about bleeding. | Ask specifically about pregnancy. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about discharge symptoms.
3. Could you be pregnant, is the period late or abnormal, and is there severe one-sided pain, shoulder pain, syncope, or heavy bleeding? - Pregnancy or ectopic-risk context in pelvic, menstrual, or lower-abdominal pain.; source ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask specifically about Could you be pregnant. | Ask specifically about is the period late. | Ask specifically about abnormal. | Ask specifically about is there severe one-sided pain. | Ask specifically about shoulder pain. | Ask specifically about syncope. | Ask specifically about heavy bleeding.

Test psoas sign (EXAM-082-psoas-sign) - Abdominal, hepatobiliary, peritoneal, spleen, or CVA maneuvers need abdominal pain/localization, GI bleeding, jaundice, flank/GU symptoms, sepsis source concern, or another validated abdominal/GU intent.; source AAFP appendicitis review citing clinical likelihood ratios; JAMA Rational Clinical Examination PubMed record; authorization validated_clinical_intent
2. Test obturator sign (EXAM-083-obturator-sign) - Abdominal, hepatobiliary, peritoneal, spleen, or CVA maneuvers need abdominal pain/localization, GI bleeding, jaundice, flank/GU symptoms, sepsis source concern, or another validated abdominal/GU intent.; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination appendicitis PubMed record; open acute-abdomen review; authorization validated_clinical_intent
3. Test rebound tenderness (EXAM-081-rebound-tenderness) - Abdominal, hepatobiliary, peritoneal, spleen, or CVA maneuvers need abdominal pain/localization, GI bleeding, jaundice, flank/GU symptoms, sepsis source concern, or another validated abdominal/GU intent.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; authorization validated_clinical_intent
4. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-086-heart-rate) - Replaced by the validated heart rate safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.; source Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; vital-sign trend interpreted in clinical context; authorization validated_clinical_intent
5. Blood pressure (EXAM-088-blood-pressure) - Replaced by the validated blood pressure safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.; source Stanford Medicine 25 blood pressure measurement techniques; Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; authorization validated_clinical_intent
6. Percuss abdomen (EXAM-075-abdominal-percussion) - Abdominal percussion is a lower-yield add-on for uncomplicated menstrual/pelvic cramps unless distension, obstruction/ileus, or ascites is suspected.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; authorization validated_clinical_intent
7. Test Murphy sign (EXAM-080-murphy-sign) - Murphy sign needs RUQ, biliary, gallbladder, or jaundice context.; source Open review of abdominal eponyms; WSES acute calculous cholecystitis guideline; authorization validated_clinical_intent
8. Palpate spleen (EXAM-077-spleen-palpation) - Spleen palpation needs splenomegaly, hematologic, malignancy, or systemic lymphadenopathy context.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; authorization validated_clinical_intent
9. Respiratory rate (EXAM-087-respiratory-rate) - Replaced by the validated respiratory rate safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.; source Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; vital-sign trend interpreted in clinical context; authorization validated_clinical_intent
10. Percuss costovertebral angle tenderness (EXAM-084-cva-tenderness) - Abdominal, hepatobiliary, peritoneal, spleen, or CVA maneuvers need abdominal pain/localization, GI bleeding, jaundice, flank/GU symptoms, sepsis source concern, or another validated abdominal/GU intent.; source PubMed study of CVA tenderness in suspected ureteral stone; NCBI acute pyelonephritis review; authorization validated_clinical_intent
11. Palpate liver edge (EXAM-079-liver-edge) - Liver edge/span needs hepatobiliary, hepatomegaly, cirrhosis, ascites, or jaundice context.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; authorization validated_clinical_intent
12. Percuss liver span (EXAM-078-liver-span) - Liver edge/span needs hepatobiliary, hepatomegaly, cirrhosis, ascites, or jaundice context.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; authorization validated_clinical_intent
13. Inspect and press lower extremity edema (EXAM-104-lower-extremity-edema) - Edema exam needs volume, heart-failure, renal, liver/ascites, leg swelling, DVT, or endocrine-crisis context.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; authorization validated_clinical_intent
14. Test cremasteric reflex (EXAM-GU-CREMASTERIC-REFLEX) - Not promoted because this maneuver lacks a direct syndrome, organ-system, management, or feasibility match for the selected validated workup; use only after adding a specific patient trigger or secondary validated intent.; source EAU_ACUTE_SCROTUM; authorization validated_clinical_intent
15. Inspect and palpate scrotum (EXAM-GU-SCROTAL-EXAM) - Not promoted because this maneuver lacks a direct syndrome, organ-system, management, or feasibility match for the selected validated workup; use only after adding a specific patient trigger or secondary validated intent.; source AUA_ACUTE_SCROTUM; authorization validated_clinical_intent
16. Abdominal exam setup (EXAM-072-abdominal-exam-setup) - Technique/setup rows are audit metadata, not a patient-specific recommended exam item.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; authorization validated_clinical_intent
17. Test Neck stiffness (EXAM-NEURO-NECK-STIFFNESS) - Not promoted because this maneuver lacks a direct syndrome, organ-system, management, or feasibility match for the selected validated workup; use only after adding a specific patient trigger or secondary validated intent.; source NICE_MENINGITIS_2024; authorization validated_clinical_intent
18. Inspect mucosa for lesions (EXAM-DERM-MUCOSAL-LESIONS) - Not promoted because this maneuver lacks a direct syndrome, organ-system, management, or feasibility match for the selected validated workup; use only after adding a specific patient trigger or secondary validated intent.; source AAFP_PRURITUS_2022; authorization validated_clinical_intent

### gu_renal_dysuria_v1: Dysuria, flank pain, pyelonephritis, or AKI/hypovolemia

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: gu_renal

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

5. Temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Are symptoms limited to dysuria/frequency/urgency, or are there fever/chills, flank pain, nausea/vomiting, hematuria, low urine output, stone history, pregnancy possibility, catheter/procedure, immunocompromise, or prior resistant urine culture? - GU/renal source and severity: cystitis, pyelonephritis/systemic UTI, obstructed infected stone, renal colic, AKI/hypovolemia, catheter/procedure-associated infection, pregnancy, or resistant pathogen risk.; source IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask specifically about Are symptoms limited to dysuria/frequency/urgency. | Ask specifically about are there fever/chills. | Ask specifically about flank pain. | Ask specifically about nausea/vomiting. | Ask specifically about hematuria. | Ask specifically about low urine output. | Ask specifically about stone history. | Ask specifically about pregnancy possibility.
2. Any dysuria, frequency/urgency, hematuria, flank pain, fever or rigors, nausea/vomiting, decreased urine, poor intake, stone history, or kidney disease? - GU/renal source and severity: lower UTI, pyelonephritis, obstructing stone, AKI/hypovolemia, systemic infection, or urine-output change.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE; details Ask specifically about dysuria. | Ask specifically about frequency/urgency. | Ask specifically about hematuria. | Ask specifically about flank pain. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about rigors. | Ask specifically about nausea/vomiting. | Ask specifically about decreased urine.
3. Any pregnancy, catheter or urinary instrumentation, urinary retention, known stone, solitary or transplant kidney, immunocompromise, resistant organism history, recent antibiotics, or inability to keep fluids or medicines down? - Complicated UTI, obstructing stone, renal vulnerability, resistant infection risk, and inability to tolerate outpatient therapy.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE; details Ask specifically about pregnancy. | Ask specifically about catheter. | Ask specifically about urinary instrumentation. | Ask specifically about urinary retention. | Ask specifically about known stone. | Ask specifically about solitary. | Ask specifically about transplant kidney. | Ask specifically about immunocompromise.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Percuss costovertebral angle tenderness (EXAM-084-cva-tenderness) - Looks for pyelonephritis or renal colic when flank or urinary symptoms are present.; options Absent / Right / Left / Bilateral; source PubMed study of CVA tenderness in suspected ureteral stone; NCBI acute pyelonephritis review; LR+ 1.3, LR- 0.7; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
2. Inspect oral mucosa (EXAM-060-mouth-exam) - Checks dehydration and bedside perfusion in GU/renal illness without turning uncomplicated urinary symptoms into a heart-failure exam.; options Normal / Dry mucosa / Lesions / Poor dentition / Bleeding; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - Checks dehydration and bedside perfusion in GU/renal illness without turning uncomplicated urinary symptoms into a heart-failure exam.; options Equal / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Palpate abdomen (EXAM-076-abdominal-palpation) - Adds suprapubic or abdominal source assessment when urinary, flank, hematuria, nausea/vomiting, or abdominal symptoms coexist.; options Nontender / Tender ___ / Guarding / Mass ___; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Percuss costovertebral angle tenderness (EXAM-084-cva-tenderness) - renal colic or pyelonephritis-associated renal tenderness; source PubMed study of CVA tenderness in suspected ureteral stone; NCBI acute pyelonephritis review; LR+ 1.3, LR- 0.7; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
2. Inspect oral mucosa (EXAM-060-mouth-exam) - Volume/perfusion clue: dry mucosa, poor intake/dehydration, weak pulses, shock physiology, or systemic infection physiology.; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - Volume/perfusion clue: dry mucosa, poor intake/dehydration, weak pulses, shock physiology, or systemic infection physiology.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Palpate abdomen (EXAM-076-abdominal-palpation) - GU/abdominal source clue: suprapubic tenderness, abdominal tenderness, retention concern, or alternate abdominal process.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Urine and renal-function tests (REQ-gu-renal-urine-renal-tests) - Urine/renal diagnostic data: pyuria/bacteriuria/hematuria, culture/susceptibility, creatinine or electrolyte abnormality, pregnancy status, systemic infection, sepsis physiology, or medication-safety constraint.; source IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
6. Renal obstruction and stone imaging pathway (REQ-gu-renal-imaging-pathway) - Imaging target: hydronephrosis/obstruction, ureteral stone, infected obstructed system, renal abscess, urinary retention, or alternate abdominal/pelvic pathology.; source NICE_RENAL_STONES; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; IDSA_CUTI_2025; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
7. Urosepsis and pyelonephritis escalation cues (REQ-gu-renal-urosepsis-red-flags) - Systemic urinary infection clue: fever/rigors, flank/CVA symptoms, sepsis physiology, altered mentation, vomiting, pregnancy, immunocompromise, catheter/procedure association, resistance risk, or renal dysfunction.; source IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
8. Focused history questions
1. Any dysuria, urethral or vaginal discharge, genital ulcer or lesion, pelvic pain, testicular pain, fever, pregnancy possibility, new partners, STI exposure, HIV/syphilis risk, recent antibiotics, or persistent symptoms after treatment? - Local GU/STI syndrome and complications: urethritis, cervicitis, PID, epididymitis, genital ulcer disease, pregnancy-related safety, resistant infection, or persistent/recurrent infection.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; CDC_STI_2021; MCGEE_EBPD; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
2. Any dysuria urethral discharge genital sores testicular pain new partners STI exposure fever or pelvic/scrotal pain? - Urethral discharge genital lesion local inflammation or testicular tenderness; source CDC_STI_2021
3. Any groin lumps genital ulcers painful lesions fever STI exposure or lower limb skin infection? - Tender enlarged or fluctuant inguinal nodes suggesting local infection STI or malignancy clue; source CDC_STI_2021

### fever_sepsis_v1: Fever, infection, or sepsis

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: infection_sepsis
Installed module: Fever, infection, or sepsis (fever_infection_sepsis_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (fever_measure_bp) - Fever, infection, or sepsis: Blood pressure anchors hemodynamic safety and helps identify shock, hypertensive urgency, orthostasis, or medication-risk context before focused diagnosis-specific decisions.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (fever_measure_hr) - Fever, infection, or sepsis: Heart rate anchors physiologic severity and can reveal adrenergic state, hypovolemia, arrhythmia, infection severity, medication effect, or decompensation.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure respiratory rate (fever_measure_rr) - Fever, infection, or sepsis: Respiratory rate and visible breathing effort are early bedside markers of respiratory failure, metabolic stress, sepsis physiology, or endocrine crisis severity.; options ___/min / Normal effort / Tachypneic/labored / Bradypneic/tiring / Not available; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure oxygen saturation (fever_measure_spo2) - Fever, infection, or sepsis: Oxygenation separates stable outpatient-style assessment from possible respiratory failure, pulmonary source, shock physiology, or need for urgent support.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen / Hypoxemic / Escalating oxygen need / Not available; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Measure temperature (fever_measure_temperature) - Fever, infection, or sepsis: Temperature confirms fever or hypothermia and helps frame infection, inflammatory illness, endocrine crisis, or treatment urgency.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
6. Mental status (fever_assess_mental_status) - Sepsis severity or CNS/metabolic involvement: new altered mentation, unsafe cooperation, or deviation from baseline. This basic acuity check helps identify severity, airway/safety risk, escalation needs, and safe interpretation of the selected validated workup.; options Baseline / Confused / Somnolent / Agitated / Unable to assess; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Focused history questions
1. How high was the fever, how was it measured, when did it start, and what antipyretics, antibiotics, steroids, or immunosuppressants have you taken? - Establishes fever reliability, tempo, pretreatment, medication masking, and host-risk context.; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; details Record maximum temperature and how it was measured. | Clarify fever onset and trajectory. | Ask what antipyretics, antibiotics, steroids, or immunosuppressants were already taken.
2. What symptoms localize the fever source: cough, sputum, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, sore throat, ear or sinus pain, dental pain, dysuria, flank pain, abdominal pain, vomiting, diarrhea, rash, wound, line concern, headache, neck stiffness, hot joint, back pain, confusion, low urine output, fainting, or rapid worsening? - Localizes the most likely infectious source and identifies sepsis severity clues.; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; details Ask specifically about cough. | Ask specifically about sputum. | Ask specifically about dyspnea. | Ask specifically about pleuritic pain. | Ask specifically about sore throat. | Ask specifically about sinus pain. | Ask specifically about dental pain. | Ask specifically about dysuria.
3. Any poor intake, inability to keep fluids down, dehydration, dizziness, fainting, confusion, unusual sleepiness, low urine output, severe weakness, or rapid worsening with the fever? - Screens for sepsis physiology, dehydration, hypoperfusion, encephalopathy, and inability to maintain safe oral intake.; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; details Ask specifically about poor intake. | Ask specifically about inability to keep fluids down. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about dizziness. | Ask specifically about fainting. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about unusual sleepiness. | Ask specifically about low urine output.
4. Any cough, sputum, shortness of breath, pleuritic pain, wheeze, hypoxia, aspiration risk, or sick respiratory contacts? - Screens for pneumonia, viral respiratory infection, aspiration, obstructive lung disease, and respiratory failure.; source ATS_CAP_2025; details Ask specifically about cough. | Ask specifically about sputum. | Ask specifically about shortness of breath. | Ask specifically about pleuritic pain. | Ask specifically about wheeze. | Ask specifically about hypoxia. | Ask specifically about aspiration risk. | Ask specifically about sick respiratory contacts.
5. Any immunosuppression, pregnancy possibility, recent hospitalization or procedure, indwelling line or device, travel, outdoor bite exposure, animal exposure, food or water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, or new medication? - Identifies host-risk, exposure, healthcare-associated, travel/vector, animal/food/water, STI, injection-related, and drug-fever contexts.; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; details Ask specifically about immunosuppression. | Ask specifically about pregnancy possibility. | Ask specifically about recent hospitalization. | Ask specifically about procedure. | Ask specifically about indwelling line. | Ask specifically about device. | Ask specifically about travel. | Ask specifically about outdoor bite exposure.
6. Any dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, flank pain, catheter or recent urologic procedure, pregnancy possibility, prior resistant urine culture, nausea, vomiting, or reduced urine output? - Distinguishes lower UTI, pyelonephritis, infected obstruction, catheter-associated infection, and renal source risk.; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; details Ask specifically about dysuria. | Ask specifically about frequency. | Ask specifically about urgency. | Ask specifically about hematuria. | Ask specifically about flank pain. | Ask specifically about catheter. | Ask specifically about recent urologic procedure. | Ask specifically about pregnancy possibility.
7. Any abdominal pain, vomiting, diarrhea, jaundice, severe headache, neck stiffness, photophobia, confusion, rash, wound, line pain, hot swollen joint, severe back pain, or rapidly spreading skin pain? - Localizes non-respiratory and non-urinary fever sources and dangerous mimics.; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; details Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about jaundice. | Ask specifically about severe headache. | Ask specifically about neck stiffness. | Ask specifically about photophobia. | Ask specifically about confusion.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Observe work of breathing (fever_observe_work_of_breathing) - Document tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, speech limitation, or apparent respiratory distress.; options Normal / Tachypneic / Labored / Accessory muscle use / Unable; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Auscultate posterior lung fields (fever_auscultate_posterior_lungs) - Listen for focal crackles, wheeze, rhonchi, diminished sounds, or asymmetry suggesting pneumonia or another pulmonary source.; options Clear / Crackles / Wheezes / Rhonchi / Diminished / Asymmetric / Unable; source ATS_CAP_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect skin for infection source (fever_inspect_skin_source) - Look for rash, cellulitis, abscess, wound, drainage, ulcer, petechiae, purpura, line-site inflammation, bite, or rapidly spreading soft-tissue findings.; options No focal skin source / Rash / Cellulitis / Abscess / Wound or drainage / Line-site inflammation / Petechiae or purpura / Unable; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect oropharynx (fever_inspect_oropharynx) - Look for pharyngeal erythema, exudate, tonsillar asymmetry, oral ulcers, dental source, thrush, mucosal lesions, or dehydration clues.; options Normal / Erythema / Exudate / Dental concern / Oral lesion / Dry mucosa / Unable; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Palpate radial pulses (fever_palpate_radial_pulses) - Assess strength, regularity, equality, and whether pulses feel thready or bounding in the setting of fever.; options Strong and equal / Weak / Bounding / Irregular / Asymmetric / Unable; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Escalate unstable fever or suspected sepsis (fever_escalate_if_unstable) - These findings change fever from outpatient/source evaluation to urgent infection, resuscitation, respiratory support, and source-control workflow.; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Use outpatient follow-up only when low risk and safety net is clear (fever_outpatient_if_low_risk) - Low-risk fever management still needs explicit safety netting so serious infection, pneumonia, dehydration, and evolving focal sources are not missed.; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (fever_measure_bp_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (fever_measure_hr_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure respiratory rate (fever_measure_rr_limitation) - Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure oxygen saturation (fever_measure_spo2_limitation) - Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Measure temperature (fever_measure_temperature_limitation) - Temperature depends on measurement site, antipyretics, timing, immune status, and device accuracy; absence of fever does not exclude serious infection or endocrine crisis.; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
6. Mental status (fever_assess_mental_status_limitation) - Mental status is affected by baseline cognition, sedatives, intoxication, pain, language barriers, hypoxia, metabolic disease, and neurologic disease; interpret with glucose, oxygenation, vitals, labs, and collateral history.; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Observe work of breathing (fever_observe_work_of_breathing_limitation) - Work of breathing may be subtle in older adults, immunocompromised patients, neuromuscular disease, obesity, sedation, or early respiratory failure; pair with respiratory rate and oxygenation.; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
8. Auscultate posterior lung fields (fever_auscultate_posterior_lungs_limitation) - Normal auscultation does not exclude pneumonia or early lower respiratory infection; body habitus, poor inspiratory effort, ambient noise, positioning, and prior lung disease can reduce reliability.; source ATS_CAP_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
9. Inspect skin for infection source (fever_inspect_skin_source_limitation) - A limited exposed-skin exam can miss sacral, groin, foot, device, wound, or pressure-site sources; document areas deferred and inspect higher-risk sites when history supports it.; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
10. Inspect oropharynx (fever_inspect_oropharynx_limitation) - Oropharyngeal findings are nonspecific and can miss deep neck, sinus, otologic, or dental sources; airway symptoms, trismus, drooling, or muffled voice need escalation beyond routine inspection.; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
11. Palpate radial pulses (fever_palpate_radial_pulses_limitation) - Pulse quality is subjective and can be affected by vascular disease, arrhythmia, temperature, anxiety, examiner skill, and cuff timing; use with BP, mental status, capillary refill or perfusion exam, and labs.; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
12. Sepsis severity labs and reference thresholds (fever_sepsis_severity_labs_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Fever, infection, or sepsis test or threshold.; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

Use outpatient follow-up only when low risk and safety net is clear (fever_outpatient_if_low_risk_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Fever, infection, or sepsis.; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Sepsis or shock physiology (fever_sepsis_shock_physiology_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Fever, infection, or sepsis.; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

### stroke_focal_neuro_v1: Stroke or focal neurologic deficit

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup

Focused history questions
1. When was last known well, and what new focal symptoms occurred: face droop, speech trouble, arm/leg weakness, numbness, vision loss, diplopia, ataxia, seizure, or severe headache? - Focal neurologic localization and time-sensitive eligibility: cortical, brainstem, cranial nerve, motor/sensory, cerebellar, seizure, or hemorrhage-pattern clues.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; JAMA_RCE; details Ask specifically about When was last known well. | Ask specifically about what new focal symptoms occurred: face droop. | Ask specifically about speech trouble. | Ask specifically about arm/leg weakness. | Ask specifically about numbness. | Ask specifically about vision loss. | Ask specifically about diplopia. | Ask specifically about ataxia.
2. Any anticoagulant use, recent surgery or bleeding, prior intracranial hemorrhage, seizure at onset, hypoglycemia symptoms, migraine pattern, trauma, or rapidly resolving symptoms? - Thrombolysis and thrombectomy eligibility context, hemorrhage risk, stroke mimic clues, seizure pathway, and glucose-related mimic risk.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; JAMA_RCE; details Ask specifically about anticoagulant use. | Ask specifically about recent surgery. | Ask specifically about bleeding. | Ask specifically about prior intracranial hemorrhage. | Ask specifically about seizure at onset. | Ask specifically about hypoglycemia symptoms. | Ask specifically about migraine pattern. | Ask specifically about trauma.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test pronator drift (EXAM-018-pronator-drift) - Adds a high-yield upper motor neuron screen when facial weakness, postictal focal deficit, or stroke concern is present.; options Absent / Present right / Present left / Unable; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test visual fields (EXAM-002-visual-fields) - Localizes visual pathway, ocular motor, vestibular, brainstem, or postictal findings when vision, diplopia, focal deficits, vertigo, or ataxia are present.; options Full / Deficit right / Deficit left / Deficit bilateral / Unable; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Check pupils (EXAM-005-pupils) - Screens pupillary asymmetry or reactivity in headache, altered mental status, focal neurologic symptoms, seizure, or neuro-ophthalmic concern.; options Equal and reactive / Unequal / Sluggish / Nonreactive / Unable; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Test extraocular movements (EXAM-003-extraocular-movements) - Localizes visual pathway, ocular motor, vestibular, brainstem, or postictal findings when vision, diplopia, focal deficits, vertigo, or ataxia are present.; options Intact / Limited / Diplopia / Nystagmus / Unable; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect facial symmetry (EXAM-010-facial-symmetry) - Directly checks facial weakness and cranial nerve VII pattern.; options Symmetric / Droop right / Droop left / Unable; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Test eye closure strength (EXAM-009-eye-closure-strength) - Directly checks facial weakness and cranial nerve VII pattern.; options Symmetric / Weak right / Weak left / Unable; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
1. Test finger-to-nose (EXAM-040-finger-to-nose) - Adds bed-safe cerebellar/coordination localization for focal neurologic or stroke presentations.; options Normal / Impaired right / Impaired left / Impaired both / Unable; source Stanford Medicine 25 cerebellar exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test rapid alternating movements (EXAM-041-rapid-alternating-movements) - Adds bed-safe cerebellar/coordination localization for focal neurologic or stroke presentations.; options Normal / Slow right / Slow left / Impaired both / Unable; source Stanford Medicine 25 cerebellar exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test heel-to-shin (EXAM-042-heel-to-shin) - Adds bed-safe cerebellar/coordination localization for focal neurologic or stroke presentations.; options Normal / Impaired right / Impaired left / Impaired both / Unable; source Stanford Medicine 25 cerebellar exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Test facial light touch (LEGACY-neuro_cranial_nerves_cn_v_facial_light_touch) - Adds trigeminal or jaw localization when facial sensory symptoms, jaw weakness, or broader cranial nerve concern exists.; options Symmetric / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent / Unable; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Test facial sharp sensation (LEGACY-neuro_cranial_nerves_cn_v_facial_sharp_touch) - Adds trigeminal or jaw localization when facial sensory symptoms, jaw weakness, or broader cranial nerve concern exists.; options Symmetric / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent / Unable; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Test masseter strength (LEGACY-neuro_cranial_nerves_cn_v_masseter_strength) - Adds trigeminal or jaw localization when facial sensory symptoms, jaw weakness, or broader cranial nerve concern exists.; options Symmetric / Weak right / Weak left / Unable; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Management-changing findings
1. Test pronator drift (EXAM-018-pronator-drift) - focal motor weakness or upper motor neuron pattern; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test visual fields (EXAM-002-visual-fields) - cranial nerve II/III/IV/VI deficit or neuro-ophthalmic localization; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Catalog gaps needing review
- none

Suppressed/not-recommended items
1. Test visual acuity (EXAM-001-visual-acuity) - Eye/cranial-nerve maneuvers need vision change, eye pain/redness, diplopia, headache/papilledema, pituitary/sellar, or focal neurologic context.; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; authorization validated_clinical_intent
2. Test Tongue protrusion (EXAM-017-tongue-protrusion) - Oral/HEENT maneuvers need dehydration, sore throat, mucosal, jaundice, infection-source, or focused HEENT context.; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; authorization validated_clinical_intent
3. Observe gait (EXAM-043-gait) - Neuro, gait, strength, sensory, reflex, or coordination maneuvers need focal neurologic deficit, weakness, sensory loss, ataxia/gait concern, cord/radicular symptoms, neuropathy, or diabetic-foot context.; source Stanford Medicine 25 gait abnormalities guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent
4. Test Romberg (EXAM-047-romberg) - Neuro, gait, strength, sensory, reflex, or coordination maneuvers need focal neurologic deficit, weakness, sensory loss, ataxia/gait concern, cord/radicular symptoms, neuropathy, or diabetic-foot context.; source Stanford Medicine 25 cerebellar exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent
5. ### spine_cord_compression_v1: Back pain, incontinence, or cord compression red flags

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: spine_cord_compression

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. 3. 4. Oxygen saturation / support (SAFETY-validated-oxygen-saturation) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen ___ L/min / Hypoxemic / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any new leg weakness, numbness, saddle numbness, urinary retention/incontinence, severe back pain, fever, cancer history, or trauma? - Cord/cauda equina motor clue: leg weakness, asymmetric strength, foot drop, or motor level pattern.; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; details Ask specifically about new leg weakness. | Ask specifically about numbness. | Ask specifically about saddle numbness. | Ask specifically about urinary retention/incontinence. | Ask specifically about severe back pain. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about cancer history. | Ask specifically about trauma.
2. Any new numbness around the groin/saddle area, urinary retention, overflow incontinence, fecal incontinence, bilateral sciatica, or progressive leg weakness? - Cauda equina or cord compression clue: saddle sensory change, urinary retention/incontinence, bowel dysfunction, or progressive bilateral deficit.; source ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask specifically about new numbness around the groin/saddle area. | Ask specifically about urinary retention. | Ask specifically about overflow incontinence. | Ask specifically about fecal incontinence. | Ask specifically about bilateral sciatica. | Ask specifically about progressive leg weakness.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test extremity light touch (EXAM-030-extremity-light-touch) - Checks sensory level, dorsal-column pattern, and radicular or cauda-equina symptoms.; options Intact / Diminished / Absent / Sensory level ___; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test extremity pinprick (EXAM-031-extremity-pinprick) - Checks sensory level, dorsal-column pattern, and radicular or cauda-equina symptoms.; options Intact / Diminished / Absent / Sensory level ___; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test hip flexion strength (EXAM-024-hip-flexion-strength) - Localizes motor deficits when spinal cord, cauda equina, radiculopathy, or compressive neurologic disease is possible.; options 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
