### hypoglycemia_jittery_v1: Hypoglycemia-like, jittery, or adrenergic symptoms

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: adrenergic_jittery

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Mental status (REQ-hypoglycemia-mental-status) - Mental status separates adrenergic symptoms from neuroglycopenic hypoglycemia that needs immediate treatment and monitoring.; options Baseline / Anxious-jittery / Confused / Somnolent / Seizing / Unable; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Bedside glucose safety check (REQ-hypoglycemia-bedside-glucose) - Point-of-care glucose is immediately management-changing in hypoglycemia-like presentations.; options ___ mg/dL / <70 mg/dL / <54 mg/dL clinically significant / Normal / High / Not available; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Heart rate (SAFETY-validated-heart-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Respiratory rate (SAFETY-validated-respiratory-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Normal / Tachypneic / Bradypneic / Labored / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
6. Oxygen saturation / support (SAFETY-validated-oxygen-saturation) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen ___ L/min / Hypoxemic / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
7. Temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any confusion, sleepiness, seizure, trouble thinking, inability to eat/drink safely, or symptoms improving after carbohydrates? - Neuroglycopenia severity: confusion, somnolence, seizure, unsafe self-treatment, or inability to take oral carbohydrates.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about sleepiness. | Ask specifically about seizure. | Ask specifically about trouble thinking. | Ask specifically about inability to eat/drink safely. | Ask specifically about symptoms improving after carbohydrates.
2. What was the most recent glucose, when were insulin or secretagogues taken, when was the last meal, and did symptoms improve after carbohydrates? - Current glucose value and severity category in a patient with adrenergic or neuroglycopenic symptoms.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect diaphoresis (REQ-hypoglycemia-diaphoresis-inspection) - Visible diaphoresis is an atomic adrenergic sign and should not be hidden inside a broad endocrine exam.; options Absent / Present / Clammy / Unable; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Observe tremor (REQ-hypoglycemia-tremor-observation) - Tremor observation captures adrenergic physiology while keeping thyroid-specific tremor assessment conditional on thyroid features.; options Absent / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Unable; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Inspect diaphoresis (REQ-hypoglycemia-diaphoresis-inspection) - Adrenergic hypoglycemia clue: diaphoresis, clamminess, or visible autonomic activation.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Observe tremor (REQ-hypoglycemia-tremor-observation) - Adrenergic hypoglycemia clue: visible tremor or shakiness during symptoms.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Hypoglycemia confirmation and cause pathway (REQ-hypoglycemia-diagnostic-pathway) - True hypoglycemia, treatment response, medication-related hypoglycemia, critical illness, adrenal/pregnancy risk, or endogenous hyperinsulinism pattern.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
4. Hypoglycemia danger and disposition cues (REQ-hypoglycemia-red-flags) - Severe, persistent, recurrent, medication-related, pregnancy-related, or unsafe hypoglycemia pattern.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

Suppressed/not-recommended items
1. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-086-heart-rate) - Replaced by the validated heart rate safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.; source Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; vital-sign trend interpreted in clinical context; authorization validated_clinical_intent
2. Blood pressure (EXAM-088-blood-pressure) - Replaced by the validated blood pressure safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.; source Stanford Medicine 25 blood pressure measurement techniques; Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; authorization validated_clinical_intent
4. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - Adrenergic/jittery presentations need volume/perfusion add-ons only with cardiopulmonary, syncope, shock, arrhythmia, or vascular context.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; authorization validated_clinical_intent
5. Test pronator drift (EXAM-018-pronator-drift) - Broad neuro/eye maneuvers need neurologic, visual, sensory, or severe hypoglycemia context.; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; authorization validated_clinical_intent
6. Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - Heart sounds need palpitations, arrhythmia, chest pain, dyspnea, shock, murmur/endocarditis, thyroid, or another cardiopulmonary trigger.; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; authorization validated_clinical_intent
7. Inspect facial symmetry (EXAM-010-facial-symmetry) - Broad neuro/eye maneuvers need neurologic, visual, sensory, or severe hypoglycemia context.; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; authorization validated_clinical_intent
8. Inspect skin for thyroid phenotype (EXAM-ENDO-THYROID-SKIN-PHENOTYPE) - Not promoted because this maneuver lacks a direct syndrome, organ-system, management, or feasibility match for the selected validated workup; use only after adding a specific patient trigger or secondary validated intent.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; authorization validated_clinical_intent
9. Inspect and palpate thyroid (EXAM-071-thyroid-exam) - Thyroid exam needs thyroid disease, goiter/nodule, compressive neck symptoms, orbitopathy, thyroid crisis, or a selected thyroid validated intent.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; authorization validated_clinical_intent
10. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - Adrenergic/jittery presentations need volume/perfusion add-ons only with cardiopulmonary, syncope, shock, arrhythmia, or vascular context.; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; authorization validated_clinical_intent
11. Test Wrist flexion strength (EXAM-023-wrist-flexion-strength) - Neuro, gait, strength, sensory, reflex, or coordination maneuvers need focal neurologic deficit, weakness, sensory loss, ataxia/gait concern, cord/radicular symptoms, neuropathy, or diabetic-foot context.; source JAMA_RCE; authorization validated_clinical_intent
12. Test Knee flexion strength (EXAM-026-knee-flexion-strength) - Neuro, gait, strength, sensory, reflex, or coordination maneuvers need focal neurologic deficit, weakness, sensory loss, ataxia/gait concern, cord/radicular symptoms, neuropathy, or diabetic-foot context.; source JAMA_RCE; authorization validated_clinical_intent
13. Palpate point of maximal impulse (EXAM-107-pmi) - PMI/apical impulse is reserved for cardiac, heart-failure, or volume-overload contexts.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac exam resources; authorization validated_clinical_intent

### thyroid_crisis_v1: Thyroid storm or myxedema coma concern

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: thyroid_endocrine

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Mental status (REQ-thyroid-crisis-mental-status) - Mental status is a severity criterion in thyroid storm and myxedema coma and should be active safety data.; options Baseline / Agitated / Delirious / Somnolent / Comatose / Unable; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Heart rate (SAFETY-validated-heart-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Respiratory rate (SAFETY-validated-respiratory-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Normal / Tachypneic / Bradypneic / Labored / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Oxygen saturation / support (SAFETY-validated-oxygen-saturation) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen ___ L/min / Hypoxemic / Not available; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any antithyroid medication or levothyroxine change or nonadherence, iodine or amiodarone exposure, infection, recent surgery or trauma, pregnancy or postpartum state, beta-blocker use, steroid use, or adrenal insufficiency concern? - Thyroid emergency precipitant, medication exposure, pregnancy/postpartum context, beta-blocker or iodine safety, and adrenal-risk context.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
2. Any neck swelling, pain, hoarseness, dysphagia, recent iodine exposure, antithyroid medication change, or thyroid hormone change? - Thyroid/neck clue: goiter, tenderness, nodularity, asymmetry, fixation, or compressive finding.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; SM25; details Ask specifically about neck swelling. | Ask specifically about pain. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about dysphagia. | Ask specifically about recent iodine exposure. | Ask specifically about antithyroid medication change. | Ask specifically about thyroid hormone change.
3. Any heat intolerance sweating cold intolerance dry skin constipation tremor slowed thinking fever hypothermia or medication change? - Thyroid phenotype clue from warm moist skin in thyrotoxicosis or dry coarse skin in severe hypothyroidism; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016
4. Any new confusion, agitation, unusual sleepiness, hypothermia, fever, infection, missed thyroid medication, or iodine/amiodarone exposure? - Thyroid emergency severity: agitation, delirium, somnolence, coma, or inability to participate in care.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask specifically about new confusion. | Ask specifically about agitation. | Ask specifically about unusual sleepiness. | Ask specifically about hypothermia. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about infection. | Ask specifically about missed thyroid medication. | Ask specifically about iodine/amiodarone exposure.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - Links neck and cardiovascular findings to thyroid severity and escalation.; options Not elevated / Elevated / Unable to assess; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
2. Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - Links neck and cardiovascular findings to thyroid severity and escalation.; options Normal / Murmur / Rub / Gallop / Other ___; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Inspect and press lower extremity edema (EXAM-104-lower-extremity-edema) - Links neck and cardiovascular findings to thyroid severity and escalation.; options None / Trace / 1+ / 2+ / 3+; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect and palpate thyroid (EXAM-071-thyroid-exam) - Directly documents goiter, tenderness, nodularity, or neck mass findings in suspected thyroid emergency.; options Normal / Enlarged / Nodule / Tender / Asymmetric / Unable; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect skin for thyroid phenotype (EXAM-ENDO-THYROID-SKIN-PHENOTYPE) - Not promoted because this maneuver lacks a direct syndrome, organ-system, management, or feasibility match for the selected validated workup; use only after adding a specific patient trigger or secondary validated intent.; options Normal / Warm moist skin / Dry coarse skin / Unable; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - elevated right-sided filling pressure or venous congestion; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
2. Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - murmur, abnormal heart sound, rhythm irregularity, or S3/S4 clue; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Inspect and press lower extremity edema (EXAM-104-lower-extremity-edema) - peripheral edema pattern and volume overload clue; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect and palpate thyroid (EXAM-071-thyroid-exam) - Thyroid/neck clue: goiter, tenderness, nodularity, asymmetry, fixation, or compressive finding.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Thyroid crisis diagnostic and safety tests (REQ-thyroid-crisis-tests) - Thyroid hormone excess/deficiency severity, arrhythmia, electrolyte/glucose disturbance, renal/hepatic dysfunction, infection precipitant, or adrenal risk.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
6. Thyroid storm or myxedema escalation cues (REQ-thyroid-crisis-red-flags) - Thyroid emergency with neurologic, thermoregulatory, cardiovascular, respiratory, electrolyte, or precipitant danger.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
7. Inspect skin for thyroid phenotype (EXAM-ENDO-THYROID-SKIN-PHENOTYPE) - Thyroid phenotype clue from warm moist skin in thyrotoxicosis or dry coarse skin in severe hypothyroidism; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - elevated right-sided filling pressure or venous congestion; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.

Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - murmur, abnormal heart sound, rhythm irregularity, or S3/S4 clue; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Focused history questions
1. Any neck mass growth, dysphagia, dyspnea, hoarseness, radiation exposure, family thyroid cancer or MEN2, pregnancy or postpartum status, amiodarone, lithium or iodine exposure, biotin use, or thyroid medication adherence issue? - Structural thyroid danger symptoms, malignancy risk, orbit/cardiopulmonary risk context, medication or supplement confounding, and reproductive context.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
2. Any neck swelling, pain, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea when supine, radiation exposure, or family thyroid cancer/MEN2 history? - Thyroid/neck finding: diffuse goiter, nodularity, tenderness, asymmetry, fixation, or compressive clue.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about neck swelling. | Ask specifically about pain. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about dysphagia. | Ask specifically about dyspnea when supine. | Ask specifically about radiation exposure. | Ask specifically about family thyroid cancer/MEN2 history.
3. Any heat intolerance sweating cold intolerance dry skin constipation tremor slowed thinking fever hypothermia or medication change? - Thyroid phenotype clue from warm moist skin in thyrotoxicosis or dry coarse skin in severe hypothyroidism; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect and palpate thyroid (EXAM-071-thyroid-exam) - Directly evaluates goiter, nodules, tenderness, asymmetry, and compressive neck findings in thyroid disease.; options Normal / Enlarged / Nodule / Tender / Asymmetric; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Suppressed/not-recommended items
1. Blood pressure (EXAM-088-blood-pressure) - Replaced by the validated blood pressure safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.; source Stanford Medicine 25 blood pressure measurement techniques; Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; authorization validated_clinical_intent
2. Respiratory rate (EXAM-087-respiratory-rate) - Not promoted as a physical exam candidate because routine vitals/basic bedside data are modeled separately in the safety-check section.; source Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; vital-sign trend interpreted in clinical context; authorization validated_clinical_intent
3. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - Vascular, edema, JVP, or precordial add-ons need shock/perfusion, DVT/leg symptoms, heart-failure/volume overload, vascular disease, syncope, or cardiopulmonary context.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; authorization validated_clinical_intent
4. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - Routine thyroid disease does not need JVP, PMI, or edema checks unless there is volume overload, heart failure, dyspnea, myxedema, or shock context.; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; authorization validated_clinical_intent
5. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-086-heart-rate) - Replaced by the validated heart rate safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.; source Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; vital-sign trend interpreted in clinical context; authorization validated_clinical_intent
6. Palpate Carotids (EXAM-099-carotids) - Carotid exam needs syncope, focal neurologic, bruit, or vascular context.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; authorization validated_clinical_intent
7. Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - Not recommended separately because the selected workup already includes the highest-yield cardiopulmonary/perfusion item for this context; use focused heart sounds when murmur, arrhythmia, shock, or cardiac symptoms are explicit.; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; authorization validated_clinical_intent
8. Palpate point of maximal impulse (EXAM-107-pmi) - PMI/apical impulse is reserved for cardiac, heart-failure, or volume-overload contexts.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac exam resources; authorization validated_clinical_intent
9. Inspect sclerae and conjunctivae (EXAM-054-sclerae-and-conjunctivae) - Oral/HEENT maneuvers need dehydration, sore throat, mucosal, jaundice, infection-source, or focused HEENT context.; source SM25; authorization validated_clinical_intent
10. Inspect oral mucosa (EXAM-060-mouth-exam) - Oral/HEENT maneuvers need dehydration, sore throat, mucosal, jaundice, infection-source, or focused HEENT context.; source SM25; authorization validated_clinical_intent
11. Inspect oropharynx (EXAM-061-oropharynx) - Oral/HEENT maneuvers need dehydration, sore throat, mucosal, jaundice, infection-source, or focused HEENT context.; source SM25; authorization validated_clinical_intent
12. Test extraocular movements (EXAM-003-extraocular-movements) - Eye/cranial-nerve maneuvers need vision change, eye pain/redness, diplopia, headache/papilledema, pituitary/sellar, or focal neurologic context.; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; authorization validated_clinical_intent
13. Auscultate posterior lung fields (EXAM-092-posterior-lung-sounds) - Pulmonary auscultation/percussion maneuvers need dyspnea, cough, hypoxia, pneumonia, PE, heart-failure, or infection-source context.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent
14. Inspect external ears (EXAM-056-external-ears) - Ear, hearing, sinus, or nasal maneuvers need otalgia, hearing loss, ear drainage, vertigo with auditory symptoms, sinus pain, nasal obstruction/congestion, URI, or focused HEENT-source context.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; authorization validated_clinical_intent
15. Inspect posterior thorax (EXAM-089-posterior-thorax-inspection) - Not promoted because this maneuver lacks a direct syndrome, organ-system, management, or feasibility match for the selected validated workup; use only after adding a specific patient trigger or secondary validated intent.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent

17. Inspect fundus with ophthalmoscope (EXAM-055-ophthalmoscopic-exam) - Eye/cranial-nerve maneuvers need vision change, eye pain/redness, diplopia, headache/papilledema, pituitary/sellar, or focal neurologic context.; source JAMA_RCE; authorization validated_clinical_intent
18. Inspect scalp (EXAM-053-scalp-exam) - Scalp exam needs localized scalp symptoms, alopecia, rash, trauma, infestation/tick exposure, head wound, or dermatologic/skin-lesion context.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; authorization validated_clinical_intent
19. Palpate sinus tenderness (EXAM-059-sinus-tenderness) - Ear, hearing, sinus, or nasal maneuvers need otalgia, hearing loss, ear drainage, vertigo with auditory symptoms, sinus pain, nasal obstruction/congestion, URI, or focused HEENT-source context.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; authorization validated_clinical_intent
20. Tactile fremitus (EXAM-090-tactile-fremitus) - Pulmonary auscultation/percussion maneuvers need dyspnea, cough, hypoxia, pneumonia, PE, heart-failure, or infection-source context.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent
21. Percuss Posterior lung (EXAM-091-posterior-lung-percussion) - Pulmonary auscultation/percussion maneuvers need dyspnea, cough, hypoxia, pneumonia, PE, heart-failure, or infection-source context.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent
22. Percuss Anterior lung (EXAM-095-anterior-lung-percussion) - Pulmonary auscultation/percussion maneuvers need dyspnea, cough, hypoxia, pneumonia, PE, heart-failure, or infection-source context.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; authorization validated_clinical_intent

Focused history questions
1. Any foot ulcer, wound, drainage, redness, new pain, numbness/tingling, loss of protective sensation, falls, prior amputation, poor footwear, or inability to inspect feet? - Diabetes foot risk: ulcer/infection, neuropathy, poor protective sensation, falls, vascular risk, footwear mismatch, or self-care barrier.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ADA_FOOT_CARE_2026; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; details Ask specifically about foot ulcer. | Ask specifically about wound. | Ask specifically about drainage. | Ask specifically about redness. | Ask specifically about new pain. | Ask specifically about numbness/tingling. | Ask specifically about loss of protective sensation. | Ask specifically about falls.
2. Any spreading redness, drainage or odor, fever, new ischemic pain, rest pain, black tissue, loss of pulses, inability to offload, unsafe footwear, or lack of wound-care supplies or follow-up? - Foot infection, limb ischemia, ulcer severity, offloading feasibility, footwear risk, and discharge safety.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ADA_FOOT_CARE_2026; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; details Ask specifically about spreading redness. | Ask specifically about drainage. | Ask specifically about odor. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about new ischemic pain. | Ask specifically about rest pain. | Ask specifically about black tissue. | Ask specifically about loss of pulses.

Focused history questions
1. Any fever, mucosal lesions, facial or tongue swelling, trouble breathing, skin pain, blistering, purpura, rapidly spreading redness, new medication, tick or travel exposure, or immunocompromise? - Anaphylaxis, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, cellulitis or necrotizing infection, meningococcemia/purpura pattern, exposure-related rash, and host risk.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; details Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about mucosal lesions. | Ask specifically about facial. | Ask specifically about tongue swelling. | Ask specifically about trouble breathing. | Ask specifically about skin pain. | Ask specifically about blistering. | Ask specifically about purpura.
2. Any new medicine, fever, rapid spread, skin pain, mucosal sores, facial/lip/tongue swelling, wheeze, wound drainage, travel/bite exposure, or immunosuppression? - Morphology and distribution: urticaria, cellulitis, abscess, vesicles, purpura, mucosal disease, ulcer/wound, jaundice, infestation, or suspicious lesion.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; details Ask specifically about new medicine. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about rapid spread. | Ask specifically about skin pain. | Ask specifically about mucosal sores. | Ask specifically about facial/lip/tongue swelling. | Ask specifically about wheeze. | Ask specifically about wound drainage.
3. Any mouth sores eye pain genital sores blistering skin pain fever new drug or trouble breathing? - Mucosal involvement suggesting severe drug eruption infection autoimmune disease or STI clue; source AAFP_PRURITUS_2022

1. Focused history questions
1. Any vision loss, eye pain, photophobia, trauma, contact lens use, discharge, severe headache, diplopia, neurologic symptoms, or immunocompromise? - Vision-threatening or neurologic eye presentation: painful red eye, corneal/contact-lens risk, orbital/uveitic pattern, diplopia, headache, or focal neurologic clue.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAO_CONJUNCTIVITIS_PPP_2023; JAMA_RCE; details Ask specifically about vision loss. | Ask specifically about eye pain. | Ask specifically about photophobia. | Ask specifically about trauma. | Ask specifically about contact lens use. | Ask specifically about discharge. | Ask specifically about severe headache. | Ask specifically about diplopia.
2. Any severe headache, jaw claudication, scalp tenderness, neurologic deficit, halos or nausea, trauma or chemical exposure, shingles rash, immunosuppression, or inability to keep the eye open? - Giant-cell arteritis clues, acute angle-closure pattern, orbital or neurologic emergency, herpes zoster ophthalmicus, traumatic or chemical injury, and severe ocular inflammation.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAO_CONJUNCTIVITIS_PPP_2023; JAMA_RCE; details Ask specifically about severe headache. | Ask specifically about jaw claudication. | Ask specifically about scalp tenderness. | Ask specifically about neurologic deficit. | Ask specifically about halos. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about trauma. | Ask specifically about chemical exposure.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Use otoscope to inspect ears (EXAM-057-otoscope-exam) - Targets external ear, canal, and tympanic membrane findings when ear pain, hearing change, or otalgia is present.; options Normal / Cerumen / Erythema / Effusion / Abnormal TM / Unable; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

### bleeding_anemia_v1: Bleeding, bruising, pallor, anemia, or GI bleed

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: gi_bleed_alarm; heme_bleeding_anemia

Focused history questions
1. Any hematemesis, melena, hematochezia, easy bruising, petechiae, gum/nose bleeding, anticoagulant use, dizziness/syncope, dyspnea, chest pain, or heavy menses? - Bleeding/anemia source and severity: GI bleed, mucocutaneous bleeding, medication-related bleeding, hemodynamic symptoms, cardiopulmonary strain, or menstrual source.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACG_UGIB_2021; JAMA_RCE; details Ask specifically about hematemesis. | Ask specifically about melena. | Ask specifically about hematochezia. | Ask specifically about easy bruising. | Ask specifically about petechiae. | Ask specifically about gum/nose bleeding. | Ask specifically about anticoagulant use. | Ask specifically about dizziness/syncope.
2. Any anticoagulant or antiplatelet use, liver disease, kidney disease, bleeding disorder, recent procedure or trauma, syncope, chest pain, dyspnea at rest, pregnancy, or prior transfusion reaction? - Medication-related bleeding, coagulopathy risk, hemodynamic/cardiopulmonary instability, pregnancy context, and transfusion or reversal planning risk.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACG_UGIB_2021; JAMA_RCE; details Ask specifically about anticoagulant. | Ask specifically about antiplatelet use. | Ask specifically about liver disease. | Ask specifically about kidney disease. | Ask specifically about bleeding disorder. | Ask specifically about recent procedure. | Ask specifically about trauma. | Ask specifically about syncope.

Core physical exam maneuvers

13. Percuss liver span (EXAM-078-liver-span) - Liver edge/span needs hepatobiliary, hepatomegaly, cirrhosis, ascites, or jaundice context.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; authorization validated_clinical_intent

Focused history questions
1. Any trauma, inability to bear weight or use the limb, fever, hot swollen joint, immunosuppression, injection drug use, cancer history, neurologic deficit, bowel or bladder change, or anticoagulant use? - Fracture or dislocation risk, septic arthritis, spinal infection or cord compression, malignancy red flags, neurologic compromise, and bleeding risk.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; JAMA_RCE; details Ask specifically about trauma. | Ask specifically about inability to bear weight. | Ask specifically about use the limb. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about hot swollen joint. | Ask specifically about immunosuppression. | Ask specifically about injection drug use. | Ask specifically about cancer history.
2. Which exact joint or body area hurts most, and was there trauma, fever, swelling, redness, wound, or inability to bear weight? - Visible inflammation, deformity, wound, asymmetry, swelling, erythema, or trauma pattern at the symptomatic site.; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask specifically about Which exact joint. | Ask specifically about body area hurts most. | Ask specifically about was there trauma. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about swelling. | Ask specifically about redness. | Ask specifically about wound. | Ask specifically about inability to bear weight.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect painful site (REQ-msk-focused-site-inspection) - Every MSK workup needs an atomic inspection step for the symptomatic site before special tests are chosen.; options Normal / Swelling / Deformity / Erythema / Wound / Bruising / Unable; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate painful site (REQ-msk-focused-site-palpation) - Palpation must be separate from inspection and ROM so warmth, effusion, and bony tenderness remain visible.; options Nontender / Focal tenderness / Warmth / Effusion / Bony tenderness / Unable; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test painful-site range of motion (REQ-msk-focused-site-rom) - Active/passive motion is its own maneuver and should not be bundled with strength, gait, or special tests.; options Full / Limited active / Limited passive / Painful / Blocked / Unable; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

1. Inspect painful site (REQ-msk-focused-site-inspection) - Visible inflammation, deformity, wound, asymmetry, swelling, erythema, or trauma pattern at the symptomatic site.; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

### muscle_cramps_neuromuscular_v1: Muscle cramps or neuromuscular irritability

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: neuromuscular_cramps

Focused history questions
1. Are the cramps associated with true weakness, numbness, back pain, dark urine, or trouble walking? - Neuromuscular clue: focal weakness, reflex change, upper motor neuron pattern, or radicular deficit.; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources
2. Any severe muscle pain, weakness, swelling, dark urine, heavy exercise, heat exposure, statin or toxin exposure, vomiting/diarrhea, or diuretic use? - Muscle injury or inflammatory clue: focal tenderness, swelling, diffuse pain, severe tenderness, or compartment concern.; source MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; details Ask specifically about severe muscle pain. | Ask specifically about weakness. | Ask specifically about swelling. | Ask specifically about dark urine. | Ask specifically about heavy exercise. | Ask specifically about heat exposure. | Ask specifically about statin. | Ask specifically about toxin exposure.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test ankle dorsiflexion strength (EXAM-027-ankle-dorsiflexion-strength) - Checks for weakness or reflex abnormality when cramps may reflect electrolyte, radicular, neuromuscular, or myelopathic disease.; options 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test ankle plantarflexion strength (EXAM-028-ankle-plantarflexion-strength) - Checks for weakness or reflex abnormality when cramps may reflect electrolyte, radicular, neuromuscular, or myelopathic disease.; options 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test patellar reflex (EXAM-037-patellar-reflex) - Checks for weakness or reflex abnormality when cramps may reflect electrolyte, radicular, neuromuscular, or myelopathic disease.; options Absent / Diminished / Normal / Brisk / Clonus; source Stanford Medicine 25 deep tendon reflex guide; Merck Manual reflex exam; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Palpate painful site (REQ-cramps-focused-muscle-tenderness) - Cramps/myalgia need an atomic muscle tenderness assessment before broad neuro testing is considered.; options Absent / Focal tenderness / Diffuse tenderness / Swelling / Compartment firmness / Unable; source MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

### acromegaly_intent_v1: Acromegaly

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Acromegaly (acromegaly_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Acromegaly.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Acromegaly.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Acromegaly needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
2. Any sweating, enlarged hands/feet, ring or shoe-size change, jaw/facial change, joint pain, sleep apnea, headaches, diabetes, hypertension, or colon-polyp history suggesting acromegaly complications? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Acromegaly needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; details Ask specifically about sweating. | Ask specifically about enlarged hands/feet. | Ask specifically about ring. | Ask specifically about shoe-size change. | Ask specifically about jaw/facial change. | Ask specifically about joint pain. | Ask specifically about sleep apnea. | Ask specifically about headaches.
3. Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Acromegaly needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.
4. Any new, worsening, resistant, or treatment-limiting hypertension or diabetes that changes endocrine severity, complication screening, or medication choice? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Acromegaly therapy unsafe.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; details Ask specifically about worsening. | Ask specifically about resistant. | Ask specifically about treatment-limiting hypertension. | Ask specifically about diabetes.
5. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Acromegaly therapy unsafe.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
6. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Acromegaly needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Acromegaly.; options Full / Bitemporal deficit / Other field deficit / Unable to assess; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect hands for acral enlargement (exam_02) - Targets growth hormone excess phenotype with acral soft-tissue or skeletal enlargement; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Acromegaly.; options No acral enlargement / Acral enlargement / Soft-tissue swelling/ring-shoe size clue / Unable to assess; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect tongue size (exam_03) - Targets macroglossia or soft-tissue enlargement affecting acromegaly phenotype, sleep apnea risk, or airway planning; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Acromegaly.; options Normal size / Macroglossia/dental impressions / Airway/sleep-apnea concern / Unable to assess; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Elevated IGF-1 plus inadequate GH suppression changes to pituitary MRI/surgical pathway. Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01_limitation) - Test confrontation visual fields is supportive for Acromegaly, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect hands for acral enlargement (exam_02_limitation) - Inspect hands for acral enlargement is supportive for Acromegaly, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Acromegaly: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Acromegaly.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
27. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Acromegaly.; source PITUITARY_ACROMEGALY_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
28. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Acromegaly.; source ACROMEGALY_DIAGNOSIS_REMISSION_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### addisons_disease_intent_v1: Addison's Disease

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Addison's Disease (addisons_disease_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Addison's Disease.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Addison's Disease.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Addison's Disease.; options No orthostatic drop / Orthostatic hypotension / Symptomatic dizziness/presyncope / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Have you had new salt craving, dizziness on standing, low blood pressure, vomiting, abdominal pain, or unusual skin darkening? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Addison's Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; details Ask specifically about Have you had new salt craving. | Ask specifically about dizziness on standing. | Ask specifically about low blood pressure. | Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about unusual skin darkening.
2. Any abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, poor intake, salt craving, orthostasis, weight loss, or new skin/oral hyperpigmentation suggesting adrenal insufficiency or crisis physiology? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Addison's Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; details Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about nausea/vomiting. | Ask specifically about poor intake. | Ask specifically about salt craving. | Ask specifically about orthostasis. | Ask specifically about weight loss. | Ask specifically about new skin/oral hyperpigmentation.
3. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Addison's Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016
4. Do you feel lightheaded when standing, faint, unusually weak, or better after fluids or salt? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Addison's Disease.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016
5. Any oral, injected, inhaled, topical, eye-drop, joint-injection, or supplement steroid exposure? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Addison's Disease therapy unsafe.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; details Ask specifically about oral. | Ask specifically about injected. | Ask specifically about inhaled. | Ask specifically about topical. | Ask specifically about eye-drop. | Ask specifically about joint-injection. | Ask specifically about supplement steroid exposure.
6. Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Addison's Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
7. Any fever, infection symptoms, recent illness, procedure, hospitalization, wound, urinary symptoms, cough, or sick contacts? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Addison's Disease therapy unsafe.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; details Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about infection symptoms. | Ask specifically about recent illness. | Ask specifically about procedure. | Ask specifically about hospitalization. | Ask specifically about wound. | Ask specifically about urinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about cough.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation (exam_01) - Targets primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Addison's Disease.; options Absent / Oral/palmar hyperpigmentation / Diffuse or scar hyperpigmentation / Unable to assess; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration (exam_02) - Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Addison's Disease.; options Moist / Dry / Very dry / Unable to assess; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test chair-rise strength (exam_03) - Targets clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Addison's Disease.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. High ACTH plus low cortisol confirms primary pattern; mineralocorticoid deficiency changes therapy to fludrocortisone/salt guidance. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Addison's Disease.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Addison's Disease.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Addison's Disease.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Addison's Disease.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Addison's Disease.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation is supportive for Addison's Disease, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

11. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Addison's Disease test or threshold.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
12. Adrenal insufficiency safety labs: check sodium, potassium, glucose, bicarbonate, creatinine, morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin testing when stable, and renin/aldosterone or 17-OHP context for mineralocorticoid/CAH decisions; do not delay stress-dose steroids in shock. (test_06_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Addison's Disease test or threshold.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
13. Morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin for adrenal insufficiency; aldosterone-renin ratio with potassium correction for primary aldosteronism; metanephrines for PPGL. (test_07_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Addison's Disease test or threshold.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
14. Cushing screening with 1-mg DST, late-night salivary cortisol, or 24-hour UFC after excluding exogenous steroids; adrenal imaging follows biochemical framing except emergency/known incidentaloma context. (test_08_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Addison's Disease test or threshold.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Addison's Disease test or threshold.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Addison's Disease test or threshold.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L. (reference_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Addison's Disease test or threshold.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Addison's Disease test or threshold.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

24. Adrenal crisis, severe hyperkalemia, shock, infection. 25. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Addison's Disease.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
26. Adrenal crisis, shock, severe vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyperkalemia/hyponatremia, hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms, psychosis, infection, or VTE. (red_flag_03_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Addison's Disease.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Addison's Disease: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Addison's Disease.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
28. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Addison's Disease.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
29. Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Addison's Disease.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
