(test_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. Cosyntropin stimulation. (test_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. Renin/aldosterone. (test_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. 21-hydroxylase antibodies. (test_04) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Adrenal insufficiency safety labs: check sodium, potassium, glucose, bicarbonate, creatinine, morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin testing when stable, and renin/aldosterone or 17-OHP context for mineralocorticoid/CAH decisions; do not delay stress-dose steroids in shock. (test_06) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin for adrenal insufficiency; aldosterone-renin ratio with potassium correction for primary aldosteronism; metanephrines for PPGL. (test_07) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Cushing screening with 1-mg DST, late-night salivary cortisol, or 24-hour UFC after excluding exogenous steroids; adrenal imaging follows biochemical framing except emergency/known incidentaloma context. (test_08) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. (reference_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (reference_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L. (reference_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Adrenal crisis, severe hyperkalemia, shock, infection. (red_flag_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
28. Adrenal crisis, shock, severe vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyperkalemia/hyponatremia, hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms, psychosis, infection, or VTE. (red_flag_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. High ACTH plus low cortisol confirms primary pattern; mineralocorticoid deficiency changes therapy to fludrocortisone/salt guidance. (management_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Addison's Disease: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (differential_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics. (differential_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### adrenal_insufficiency_intent_v1: Adrenal Insufficiency

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Adrenal Insufficiency (adrenal_insufficiency_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options No orthostatic drop / Orthostatic hypotension / Symptomatic dizziness/presyncope / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, dry skin, hoarse voice, slowed thinking, depression, heavy menses, or weight gain suggesting hypothyroidism? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; details Ask specifically about fatigue. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about dry skin. | Ask specifically about hoarse voice. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about depression. | Ask specifically about heavy menses.
2. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016
3. Any abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, poor intake, salt craving, orthostasis, weight loss, or new skin/oral hyperpigmentation suggesting adrenal insufficiency or crisis physiology? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; details Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about nausea/vomiting. | Ask specifically about poor intake. | Ask specifically about salt craving. | Ask specifically about orthostasis. | Ask specifically about weight loss. | Ask specifically about new skin/oral hyperpigmentation.
4. Any vomiting, severe weakness, abdominal pain, fever, missed steroid doses, recent illness, or inability to keep down stress-dose steroids? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Adrenal Insufficiency therapy unsafe.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; details Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about severe weakness. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about missed steroid doses. | Ask specifically about recent illness. | Ask specifically about inability to keep down stress-dose steroids.
5. Have you had new salt craving, dizziness on standing, low blood pressure, vomiting, abdominal pain, or unusual skin darkening? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; details Ask specifically about Have you had new salt craving. | Ask specifically about dizziness on standing. | Ask specifically about low blood pressure. | Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about unusual skin darkening.
6. Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
7. Do you feel lightheaded when standing, faint, unusually weak, or better after fluids or salt? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Adrenal Insufficiency.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016
8. Any fever, infection symptoms, recent illness, procedure, hospitalization, wound, urinary symptoms, cough, or sick contacts? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Adrenal Insufficiency therapy unsafe.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; details Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about infection symptoms. | Ask specifically about recent illness. | Ask specifically about procedure. | Ask specifically about hospitalization. | Ask specifically about wound. | Ask specifically about urinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about cough.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation (exam_01) - Targets primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options Absent / Oral/palmar hyperpigmentation / Diffuse or scar hyperpigmentation / Unable to assess; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration (exam_02) - Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options Moist / Dry / Very dry / Unable to assess; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test chair-rise strength (exam_03) - Targets clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Suspected adrenal crisis should be treated urgently; do not delay hydrocortisone for testing if unstable. Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

26. Adrenal Insufficiency: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Adrenal Insufficiency.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
27. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Adrenal Insufficiency.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
28. Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Adrenal Insufficiency.; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Adrenal insufficiency safety labs: check sodium, potassium, glucose, bicarbonate, creatinine, morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin testing when stable, and renin/aldosterone or 17-OHP context for mineralocorticoid/CAH decisions; do not delay stress-dose steroids in shock. (test_05) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin for adrenal insufficiency; aldosterone-renin ratio with potassium correction for primary aldosteronism; metanephrines for PPGL. ### amenorrhea_intent_v1: Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) (amenorrhea_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options ___ kg/m2 / Underweight / Overweight/obesity range / Unable/not available; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Did menstrual periods never start by the expected age, or did previously present periods stop? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024
2. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024
3. Is pregnancy possible now, planned soon, or recently postpartum, and would pregnancy change medication or imaging safety? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) therapy unsafe.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024
4. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024
5. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) therapy unsafe.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
6. Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
7. Any new or rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth, acne, scalp hair loss, deepening voice, clitoromegaly, or menstrual irregularity? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; details Ask specifically about rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth. | Ask specifically about acne. | Ask specifically about scalp hair loss. | Ask specifically about deepening voice. | Ask specifically about clitoromegaly. | Ask specifically about menstrual irregularity.
8. Are fertility goals, pregnancy possibility, partner factors, or treatment choices relevant to the timing and safety of this endocrine workup? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) therapy unsafe.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024
9. Any hot flashes, night sweats, sleep disruption, vaginal/genitourinary symptoms, mood change, cycle change, or fertility goal affecting reproductive endocrine management? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) therapy unsafe.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; details Ask specifically about hot flashes. | Ask specifically about night sweats. | Ask specifically about sleep disruption. | Ask specifically about vaginal/genitourinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about mood change. | Ask specifically about cycle change. | Ask specifically about fertility goal.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect acne distribution (exam_01) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options Absent/minimal / Inflammatory acne / Severe/nodulocystic / Unable/deferred; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect terminal hair distribution (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options Expected distribution / Mild excess / Marked excess / Virilization concern / Unable/deferred; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Gonadal axis safety tests: pregnancy test when possible, prolactin/TSH, LH/FSH with estradiol or repeated early-morning testosterone, and targeted androgen, semen, bone, or metabolic testing based on syndrome and fertility goals. (test_06_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) test or threshold.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

17. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) test or threshold.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) test or threshold.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

32. Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
33. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
34. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

(test_05) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Gonadal axis safety tests: pregnancy test when possible, prolactin/TSH, LH/FSH with estradiol or repeated early-morning testosterone, and targeted androgen, semen, bone, or metabolic testing based on syndrome and fertility goals. (test_06) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_07) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_08) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_04) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
30. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_05) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
31. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_06) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
32. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_07) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
33. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, visual loss/headache, rapid virilization, vital sign instability/eating disorder. (red_flag_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
34. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
35. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
36. FSH/E2 pattern separates ovarian failure from central/hypothalamic causes and changes imaging/referral. (management_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
40. Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
41. Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
42. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
43. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia_intent_v1: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) (congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options No orthostatic drop / Orthostatic hypotension / Symptomatic dizziness/presyncope / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any neonatal ambiguous genitalia, salt-wasting episode, early pubarche, rapid growth, virilization, menstrual/fertility issue, or family CAH history? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) therapy unsafe.; source ES_CAH_2018; details Ask specifically about neonatal ambiguous genitalia. | Ask specifically about salt-wasting episode. | Ask specifically about early pubarche. | Ask specifically about rapid growth. | Ask specifically about virilization. | Ask specifically about menstrual/fertility issue. | Ask specifically about family CAH history.
2. How long has conception been attempted, are cycles ovulatory/regular, and have partner semen analysis or prior fertility evaluations found abnormalities? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) therapy unsafe.; source ES_CAH_2018; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
3. Any personal or family autoimmune thyroid disease, type 1 diabetes, celiac disease, pernicious anemia, adrenal disease, pituitary disease, or polyglandular autoimmune syndrome? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; source ES_CAH_2018; details Ask specifically about personal. | Ask specifically about family autoimmune thyroid disease. | Ask specifically about type 1 diabetes. | Ask specifically about celiac disease. | Ask specifically about pernicious anemia. | Ask specifically about adrenal disease. | Ask specifically about pituitary disease. | Ask specifically about polyglandular autoimmune syndrome.
4. Any vomiting, severe weakness, abdominal pain, fever, missed steroid doses, recent illness, or inability to keep down stress-dose steroids? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) therapy unsafe.; source ES_CAH_2018; details Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about severe weakness. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about missed steroid doses. | Ask specifically about recent illness. | Ask specifically about inability to keep down stress-dose steroids.
5. Do you feel lightheaded when standing, faint, unusually weak, or better after fluids or salt? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; source ES_CAH_2018
6. Have you had new salt craving, dizziness on standing, low blood pressure, vomiting, abdominal pain, or unusual skin darkening? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CAH_2018; details Ask specifically about Have you had new salt craving. | Ask specifically about dizziness on standing. | Ask specifically about low blood pressure. | Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about unusual skin darkening.
7. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CAH_2018
8. Any abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, poor intake, salt craving, orthostasis, weight loss, or new skin/oral hyperpigmentation suggesting adrenal insufficiency or crisis physiology? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CAH_2018; details Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about nausea/vomiting. | Ask specifically about poor intake. | Ask specifically about salt craving. | Ask specifically about orthostasis. | Ask specifically about weight loss. | Ask specifically about new skin/oral hyperpigmentation.
9. Any fever, infection symptoms, recent illness, procedure, hospitalization, wound, urinary symptoms, cough, or sick contacts? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) therapy unsafe.; source ES_CAH_2018; details Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about infection symptoms. | Ask specifically about recent illness. | Ask specifically about procedure. | Ask specifically about hospitalization. | Ask specifically about wound. | Ask specifically about urinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about cough.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation (exam_01) - Targets primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options Absent / Oral/palmar hyperpigmentation / Diffuse or scar hyperpigmentation / Unable to assess; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration (exam_02) - Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options Moist / Dry / Very dry / Unable to assess; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test chair-rise strength (exam_03) - Targets clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect terminal hair distribution (exam_04) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options Expected distribution / Mild excess / Marked excess / Virilization concern / Unable/deferred; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Stage pubertal development (exam_05) - Targets pubertal development, delayed/precocious puberty pattern, or hypogonadal phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options Age-appropriate / Delayed/underdeveloped / Advanced/virilized / Unable/deferred; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Electrolyte crisis or vomiting changes to emergency steroid/fluid pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

14. Adrenal insufficiency safety labs: check sodium, potassium, glucose, bicarbonate, creatinine, morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin testing when stable, and renin/aldosterone or 17-OHP context for mineralocorticoid/CAH decisions; do not delay stress-dose steroids in shock. ### cushings_disease_intent_v1: Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's) (cushings_disease_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Have you noticed easy bruising, proximal muscle weakness, wide purple stretch marks, facial rounding, new hypertension, new diabetes, or recurrent infections? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about Have you noticed easy bruising. | Ask specifically about proximal muscle weakness. | Ask specifically about wide purple stretch marks. | Ask specifically about facial rounding. | Ask specifically about new hypertension. | Ask specifically about new diabetes. | Ask specifically about recurrent infections.
2. Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
3. Do symptoms occur in episodes or cycles, and are there symptom-free intervals, repeated abnormal labs, or triggers that suggest cyclic hormone excess? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about Do symptoms occur in episodes. | Ask specifically about cycles. | Ask specifically about are there symptom-free intervals. | Ask specifically about repeated abnormal labs. | Ask specifically about triggers that suggest cyclic hormone excess.
4. Any oral, injected, inhaled, topical, eye-drop, joint-injection, or supplement steroid exposure? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's) therapy unsafe.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about oral. | Ask specifically about injected. | Ask specifically about inhaled. | Ask specifically about topical. | Ask specifically about eye-drop. | Ask specifically about joint-injection. | Ask specifically about supplement steroid exposure.
5. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008
6. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's) therapy unsafe.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
7. How much are you drinking and urinating, including polyuria, polydipsia, overnight urination, thirst intensity, access to water, and any dehydration or confusion? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Clarify urine volume, nocturia, and urinary frequency. | Ask about thirst intensity and access to water. | Ask about dehydration, confusion, and inability to keep up with intake.
8. Have ring size, shoe size, facial features, jaw spacing, sweating, sleep apnea, joint pain, or headaches changed over time? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about Have ring size. | Ask specifically about shoe size. | Ask specifically about facial features. | Ask specifically about jaw spacing. | Ask specifically about sweating. | Ask specifically about sleep apnea. | Ask specifically about joint pain. | Ask specifically about headaches changed over time.
9. Any adrenal symptoms such as orthostasis, salt craving, hyperpigmentation, unexplained weight loss, or thyroid symptoms such as palpitations, tremor, heat/cold intolerance, bowel change, or neck swelling? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about adrenal symptoms such as orthostasis. | Ask specifically about salt craving. | Ask specifically about hyperpigmentation. | Ask specifically about unexplained weight loss. | Ask specifically about thyroid symptoms such as palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about heat/cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about bowel change.

Core physical exam maneuvers

Management-changing findings

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Inspect skin for bruising (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect skin for bruising is supportive for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
26. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (management_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency. (management_04) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Post-1 mg DST cortisol >1.8 ug/dL is a sensitive abnormal screen; late-night salivary and UFC cutoffs are assay-specific. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### cushings_syndrome_intent_v1: Cushing's Syndrome

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Cushing's Syndrome (cushings_syndrome_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Cushing's Syndrome.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Cushing's Syndrome.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Cushing's Syndrome.; options No orthostatic drop / Orthostatic hypotension / Symptomatic dizziness/presyncope / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Do you have trouble rising from a chair, climbing stairs, lifting overhead, or getting up from the floor? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Cushing's Syndrome.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008
2. Any fever, infection symptoms, recent illness, procedure, hospitalization, wound, urinary symptoms, cough, or sick contacts? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Cushing's Syndrome therapy unsafe.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about infection symptoms. | Ask specifically about recent illness. | Ask specifically about procedure. | Ask specifically about hospitalization. | Ask specifically about wound. | Ask specifically about urinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about cough.
3. Any new, worsening, resistant, or treatment-limiting hypertension or diabetes that changes endocrine severity, complication screening, or medication choice? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Cushing's Syndrome therapy unsafe.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about worsening. | Ask specifically about resistant. | Ask specifically about treatment-limiting hypertension. | Ask specifically about diabetes.
4. Do you feel lightheaded when standing, faint, unusually weak, or better after fluids or salt? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Cushing's Syndrome.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008
5. Have you had new salt craving, dizziness on standing, low blood pressure, vomiting, abdominal pain, or unusual skin darkening? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about Have you had new salt craving. | Ask specifically about dizziness on standing. | Ask specifically about low blood pressure. | Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about unusual skin darkening.
6. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008
7. Any abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, poor intake, salt craving, orthostasis, weight loss, or new skin/oral hyperpigmentation suggesting adrenal insufficiency or crisis physiology? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about nausea/vomiting. | Ask specifically about poor intake. | Ask specifically about salt craving. | Ask specifically about orthostasis. | Ask specifically about weight loss. | Ask specifically about new skin/oral hyperpigmentation.
8. Any personal or family autoimmune thyroid disease, type 1 diabetes, celiac disease, pernicious anemia, adrenal disease, pituitary disease, or polyglandular autoimmune syndrome? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Cushing's Syndrome.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; details Ask specifically about personal. | Ask specifically about family autoimmune thyroid disease. | Ask specifically about type 1 diabetes. | Ask specifically about celiac disease. | Ask specifically about pernicious anemia. | Ask specifically about adrenal disease. | Ask specifically about pituitary disease. | Ask specifically about polyglandular autoimmune syndrome.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2.
