22. Cushing safety tests: use late-night salivary cortisol, 1-mg dexamethasone suppression, or 24-hour urinary free cortisol after excluding exogenous steroids, and assess glucose, BP, potassium, infection/VTE, and bone risk to determine severity and treatment urgency. (test_07) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin for adrenal insufficiency; aldosterone-renin ratio with potassium correction for primary aldosteronism; metanephrines for PPGL. (test_08) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Cushing screening with 1-mg DST, late-night salivary cortisol, or 24-hour UFC after excluding exogenous steroids; adrenal imaging follows biochemical framing except emergency/known incidentaloma context. (test_09) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. DST, late-night salivary cortisol, and UFC cutoffs are assay-specific; abnormal screening generally needs confirmation. (reference_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Severe hypokalemia, uncontrolled hypertension/diabetes, infection, psychosis, VTE. (red_flag_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
28. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. Adrenal crisis, shock, severe vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyperkalemia/hyponatremia, hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms, psychosis, infection, or VTE. (red_flag_03) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. Abnormal screening plus convincing phenotype triggers confirmatory/endocrine pathway; ACTH directs adrenal vs ACTH-dependent workup. (management_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Cushing's Syndrome: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: DST, late-night salivary cortisol, and UFC cutoffs are assay-specific; abnormal screening generally needs confirmation. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
37. Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics. (differential_03) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### diabetes_insipidus_intent_v1: Diabetes Insipidus

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Diabetes Insipidus (diabetes_insipidus_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Diabetes Insipidus.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Diabetes Insipidus.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Diabetes Insipidus.; options No orthostatic drop / Orthostatic hypotension / Symptomatic dizziness/presyncope / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Assess mental status (safety_check_04) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Diabetes Insipidus.; options Baseline/alert / Confused / Somnolent / Agitated / Unable to assess; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. What is the approximate 24-hour urine volume, and has urine output changed from baseline? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Diabetes Insipidus.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026
2. How many times do you urinate overnight, and is this new or worsening? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Diabetes Insipidus.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026
3. How much are you drinking and urinating, including polyuria, polydipsia, overnight urination, thirst intensity, access to water, and any dehydration or confusion? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Diabetes Insipidus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; details Clarify urine volume, nocturia, and urinary frequency. | Ask about thirst intensity and access to water. | Ask about dehydration, confusion, and inability to keep up with intake.
4. Do you have reliable access to water, and are thirst, intake, or mental status limiting safe hydration? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Diabetes Insipidus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026
5. Any lithium, demeclocycline, amphotericin, diuretic, SGLT2 inhibitor, glucocorticoid, desmopressin, fluid restriction, or recent medication change affecting polyuria, sodium, thirst, or kidney concentrating ability? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Diabetes Insipidus therapy unsafe.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; details Ask specifically about lithium. | Ask specifically about demeclocycline. | Ask specifically about amphotericin. | Ask specifically about diuretic. | Ask specifically about SGLT2 inhibitor. | Ask specifically about glucocorticoid. | Ask specifically about desmopressin. | Ask specifically about fluid restriction.
6. Any kidney stones, constipation, polyuria, polydipsia, bone pain, confusion, dehydration, or reduced kidney function? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Diabetes Insipidus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; details Ask specifically about kidney stones. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about polyuria. | Ask specifically about polydipsia. | Ask specifically about bone pain. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about reduced kidney function.
7. Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Diabetes Insipidus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.

1. Hypernatremia or impaired water access changes to urgent monitored fluid/desmopressin pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Diabetes Insipidus.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

(management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Diabetes Insipidus.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

(management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Diabetes Insipidus.; source SFE_CDI_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Assess mental status (safety_check_04_limitation) - Global appearance and perfusion are subjective and affected by baseline function, pain, medications, temperature, and examiner experience; pair with objective vitals and reassessment.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Diabetes Insipidus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Check capillary refill (exam_02_limitation) - Check capillary refill is supportive for Diabetes Insipidus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

13. Diabetes insipidus safety tests: check serum sodium/osmolality, urine osmolality/specific gravity, urine volume, glucose/calcium/potassium/renal function, and desmopressin, copeptin, or supervised water-deprivation pathway only when stable. ### erectile_dysfunction_intent_v1: Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related) (erectile_dysfunction_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related).; options ___ kg/m2 / Underweight / Overweight/obesity range / Unable/not available; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any change in libido, morning erections, erectile function, ejaculation, orgasm, fertility goals, or testicular symptoms? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related) therapy unsafe.; source AUA_ED_2018; details Ask specifically about change in libido. | Ask specifically about morning erections. | Ask specifically about erectile function. | Ask specifically about ejaculation. | Ask specifically about orgasm. | Ask specifically about fertility goals. | Ask specifically about testicular symptoms.
2. Any new, worsening, resistant, or treatment-limiting hypertension or diabetes that changes endocrine severity, complication screening, or medication choice? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related) therapy unsafe.; source AUA_ED_2018; details Ask specifically about worsening. | Ask specifically about resistant. | Ask specifically about treatment-limiting hypertension. | Ask specifically about diabetes.
3. Any history of ASCVD, heart failure, stroke/TIA, peripheral arterial disease, claudication, or vascular procedures? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related) therapy unsafe.; source AUA_ED_2018; details Ask specifically about history of ASCVD. | Ask specifically about heart failure. | Ask specifically about stroke/TIA. | Ask specifically about peripheral arterial disease. | Ask specifically about claudication. | Ask specifically about vascular procedures.
4. Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related) needs urgent evaluation today.; source AUA_ED_2018; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.
5. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related) therapy unsafe.; source AUA_ED_2018; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
6. Any hot flashes, night sweats, sleep disruption, vaginal/genitourinary symptoms, mood change, cycle change, or fertility goal affecting reproductive endocrine management? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related) therapy unsafe.; source AUA_ED_2018; details Ask specifically about hot flashes. | Ask specifically about night sweats. | Ask specifically about sleep disruption. | Ask specifically about vaginal/genitourinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about mood change. | Ask specifically about cycle change. | Ask specifically about fertility goal.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Measure testicular volume (exam_01) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related).; options Expected volume / Small bilaterally / Asymmetric/mass concern / Unable/deferred; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect breast tissue (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related).; options No glandular tissue / Tender glandular tissue / Discrete mass / Nipple discharge/skin change / Unable/deferred; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect body hair distribution (exam_03) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related).; options Expected distribution / Reduced androgen-dependent hair / Excess/virilizing pattern / Unable/deferred; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Low testosterone changes to hypogonadism pathway; diabetes/ASCVD risk changes cardiovascular management. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related).; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure testicular volume (exam_01_limitation) - Measure testicular volume is supportive for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect breast tissue (exam_02_limitation) - Inspect breast tissue is supportive for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect body hair distribution (exam_03_limitation) - Assess body hair distribution is supportive for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related).; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
31. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related).; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
32. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related).; source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

(management_03) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
37. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
38. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### gestational_diabetes_intent_v1: Gestational Diabetes

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Gestational Diabetes (gestational_diabetes_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gestational Diabetes.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Gestational Diabetes.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Gestational Diabetes.; options ___ kg/lb / Recent weight gain / Recent weight loss / Unable/not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure waist circumference (safety_check_04) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Gestational Diabetes.; options ___ cm/in / Above risk threshold / Not above risk threshold / Unable/not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. How many weeks pregnant are you, and has pregnancy dating, fetal growth, or obstetric context changed diabetes screening or treatment safety? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gestational Diabetes therapy unsafe.; source ADA_SOC_2026
2. Any history of prior gestational diabetes or macrosomia that changes Gestational Diabetes risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gestational Diabetes.; source ADA_SOC_2026
3. Any pre-pregnancy diabetes, prior A1c/glucose abnormality, PCOS, obesity, steroid exposure, or strong family history that changes gestational diabetes classification or treatment intensity? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gestational Diabetes therapy unsafe.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
4. What is the current home glucose pattern if available, and how has it changed from baseline? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gestational Diabetes.; source ADA_SOC_2026
5. Have fetal growth, estimated fetal weight, amniotic fluid, or obstetric ultrasound findings raised concern for hyperglycemia-related risk? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Gestational Diabetes needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026
6. What is the current obstetric plan, gestational age, fetal surveillance plan, delivery timing, and maternal-fetal medicine involvement? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gestational Diabetes.; source ADA_SOC_2026

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_01) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Gestational Diabetes.; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure waist circumference (safety_check_04_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Gestational Diabetes, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

13. One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Gestational Diabetes test or threshold.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
14. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values. (reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Gestational Diabetes test or threshold.; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

31. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Gestational Diabetes.; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
32. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Gestational Diabetes.; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
33. Physical exam scope for Gestational Diabetes (gestational_diabetes_v1_physical_exam_scope) - This workup is intentionally exam-light: diagnosis and risk stratification are driven by guideline laboratory thresholds, pregnancy/medication context, and anthropometrics rather than a broad physical exam. Check basic bedside data and anthropometrics, document the focused insulin-resistance phenotype exam when relevant, and add foot/vascular, hydration/perfusion, abdominal, cardiopulmonary, or neurologic exams only when symptoms, complications, or a second validated intent trigger them.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to exam-scope cautions; use this row to audit why the workup remains focused rather than comprehensive-by-default.

19. One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. 20. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values. (reference_02) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

37. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values. (differential_02) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
38. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. (differential_03) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### gigantism_intent_v1: Gigantism

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Gigantism (gigantism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gigantism.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Gigantism.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. How has height or growth velocity changed compared with prior measurements, family height pattern, and puberty timing? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gigantism.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014
2. Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Gigantism needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
3. Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Gigantism needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.
4. Is pregnancy possible now, planned soon, or recently postpartum, and would pregnancy change medication or imaging safety? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gigantism therapy unsafe.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014
5. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gigantism therapy unsafe.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
6. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Gigantism needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Gigantism.; options Full / Bitemporal deficit / Other field deficit / Unable to assess; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect hands for acral enlargement (exam_02) - Targets growth hormone excess phenotype with acral soft-tissue or skeletal enlargement; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gigantism.; options No acral enlargement / Acral enlargement / Soft-tissue swelling/ring-shoe size clue / Unable to assess; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect tongue size (exam_03) - Targets macroglossia or soft-tissue enlargement affecting acromegaly phenotype, sleep apnea risk, or airway planning; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gigantism.; options Normal size / Macroglossia/dental impressions / Airway/sleep-apnea concern / Unable to assess; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Stage pubertal development (exam_04) - Targets pubertal development, delayed/precocious puberty pattern, or hypogonadal phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gigantism.; options Age-appropriate / Delayed/underdeveloped / Advanced/virilized / Unable/deferred; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01_limitation) - Test confrontation visual fields is supportive for Gigantism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

### graves_disease_intent_v1: Graves' Disease

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Graves' Disease (graves_disease_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Graves' Disease.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Graves' Disease.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Have you had heat intolerance, palpitations, tremor, anxiety, unintentional weight loss, diarrhea, or increased sweating? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Graves' Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about Have you had heat intolerance. | Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about anxiety. | Ask specifically about unintentional weight loss. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about increased sweating.
2. Any eye pain, redness, light sensitivity, bulging eyes, double vision, reduced vision, or trouble closing the eyelids? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Graves' Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about eye pain. | Ask specifically about redness. | Ask specifically about light sensitivity. | Ask specifically about bulging eyes. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about reduced vision. | Ask specifically about trouble closing the eyelids.
3. Do you currently smoke or recently smoke, especially in the context of Graves orbitopathy or cardiovascular risk? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Graves' Disease.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016
4. Is pregnancy possible now, planned soon, or recently postpartum, and would pregnancy change medication or imaging safety? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Graves' Disease therapy unsafe.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016
5. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Graves' Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016
6. Any fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, dry skin, hoarse voice, slowed thinking, depression, heavy menses, or weight gain suggesting hypothyroidism? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Graves' Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about fatigue. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about dry skin. | Ask specifically about hoarse voice. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about depression. | Ask specifically about heavy menses.
7. Any anxiety, depression, irritability, slowed thinking, memory change, confusion, sleep change, or functional decline? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Graves' Disease.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about anxiety. | Ask specifically about depression. | Ask specifically about irritability. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about memory change. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about sleep change. | Ask specifically about functional decline.
8. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Graves' Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016
9. Any neck swelling, thyroid pain or tenderness, pressure, hoarseness, trouble swallowing, positional shortness of breath, or rapidly enlarging neck mass? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Graves' Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about neck swelling. | Ask specifically about thyroid pain. | Ask specifically about tenderness. | Ask specifically about pressure. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about trouble swallowing. | Ask specifically about positional shortness of breath. | Ask specifically about rapidly enlarging neck mass.
10. Any childhood head/neck radiation, therapeutic radiation, occupational exposure, or family thyroid cancer risk factor? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Graves' Disease.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about childhood head/neck radiation. | Ask specifically about therapeutic radiation. | Ask specifically about occupational exposure. | Ask specifically about family thyroid cancer risk factor.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Graves' Disease.; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate cervical lymph nodes (exam_02) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Graves' Disease.; options No concerning nodes / Tender/mobile nodes / Firm/fixed nodes / Supraclavicular node / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets thyroid-related tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, bradycardia, or heart-failure complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Graves' Disease.; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe outstretched-hands tremor (exam_04) - Targets thyrotoxicosis adrenergic signs, medication effect, or endocrine-related neuromuscular irritability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Graves' Disease.; options No tremor / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Functionally limiting tremor / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. 1. TRAb-positive biochemical hyperthyroidism supports Graves. 4. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Graves' Disease.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01_limitation) - Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Graves' Disease, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

31. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (management_05) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Graves' Disease: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
40. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
41. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### gynecomastia_intent_v1: Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue) (gynecomastia_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options ___ kg/m2 / Underweight / Overweight/obesity range / Unable/not available; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. How long has breast tissue enlargement or tenderness been present, is it unilateral or bilateral, and have rapid growth, discrete mass, skin change, or nipple discharge occurred? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; details Ask specifically about How long has breast tissue enlargement. | Ask specifically about tenderness been present. | Ask specifically about is it unilateral. | Ask specifically about bilateral. | Ask specifically about have rapid growth. | Ask specifically about discrete mass. | Ask specifically about skin change. | Ask specifically about nipple discharge occurred.
2. Any breast tenderness, discrete mass, nipple discharge, testicular pain or mass, fertility change, libido change, medication exposure, liver/kidney disease, alcohol, or cannabis use? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue) therapy unsafe.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; details Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about discrete mass. | Ask specifically about nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about testicular pain. | Ask specifically about mass. | Ask specifically about fertility change. | Ask specifically about libido change. | Ask specifically about medication exposure.
3. Any change in libido, morning erections, erectile function, ejaculation, orgasm, fertility goals, or testicular symptoms? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue) therapy unsafe.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; details Ask specifically about change in libido. | Ask specifically about morning erections. | Ask specifically about erectile function. | Ask specifically about ejaculation. | Ask specifically about orgasm. | Ask specifically about fertility goals. | Ask specifically about testicular symptoms.
4. How has height or growth velocity changed compared with prior measurements, family height pattern, and puberty timing? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Measure testicular volume (exam_01) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options Expected volume / Small bilaterally / Asymmetric/mass concern / Unable/deferred; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect breast tissue (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options No glandular tissue / Tender glandular tissue / Discrete mass / Nipple discharge/skin change / Unable/deferred; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect body hair distribution (exam_03) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options Expected distribution / Reduced androgen-dependent hair / Excess/virilizing pattern / Unable/deferred; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons

Management-changing findings
1. Testicular mass/hCG elevation changes to urgent tumor pathway; hypogonadism pattern changes endocrine workup. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure testicular volume (exam_01_limitation) - Measure testicular volume is supportive for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect breast tissue (exam_02_limitation) - Inspect breast tissue is supportive for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
