14. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_04) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. Gynecomastia safety tests: review medication/substance causes and check testosterone, estradiol, LH/FSH, beta-hCG, prolactin/TSH, liver/kidney function, and testicular evaluation when mass, rapid growth, or malignancy concern exists. (test_05) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_06) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_07) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Hard eccentric breast mass, nipple discharge, skin changes, nodes, testicular mass, rapid progression. (red_flag_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
23. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
24. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
25. Testicular mass/hCG elevation changes to urgent tumor pathway; hypogonadism pattern changes endocrine workup. (management_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
26. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
27. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
28. Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
29. Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
31. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
32. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### hashimotos_thyroiditis_intent_v1: Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism) (hashimotos_thyroiditis_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Have you had cold intolerance, constipation, fatigue, dry skin, slowed thinking, hoarse voice, weight gain, or heavy menses? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about Have you had cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about fatigue. | Ask specifically about dry skin. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about hoarse voice. | Ask specifically about weight gain. | Ask specifically about heavy menses.
2. Any personal or family autoimmune disease, endocrine disease, thyroid cancer/MEN2, adrenal disease, or related inherited condition? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about personal. | Ask specifically about family autoimmune disease. | Ask specifically about endocrine disease. | Ask specifically about thyroid cancer/MEN2. | Ask specifically about adrenal disease. | Ask specifically about related inherited condition.
3. Any neck swelling, rapid growth, pain, hoarseness, trouble swallowing, positional shortness of breath, radiation exposure, or family thyroid cancer/MEN2 history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about neck swelling. | Ask specifically about rapid growth. | Ask specifically about pain. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about trouble swallowing. | Ask specifically about positional shortness of breath. | Ask specifically about radiation exposure. | Ask specifically about family thyroid cancer/MEN2 history.
4. Any palpitations, tremor, heat intolerance, anxiety, insomnia, weight loss, diarrhea, or sweating suggesting thyroid hormone excess or adrenergic physiology? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about heat intolerance. | Ask specifically about anxiety. | Ask specifically about insomnia. | Ask specifically about weight loss. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about sweating.
5. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014
6. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014
7. Any neck swelling, thyroid pain or tenderness, pressure, hoarseness, trouble swallowing, positional shortness of breath, or rapidly enlarging neck mass? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about neck swelling. | Ask specifically about thyroid pain. | Ask specifically about tenderness. | Ask specifically about pressure. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about trouble swallowing. | Ask specifically about positional shortness of breath. | Ask specifically about rapidly enlarging neck mass.
8. Any thyroid hormone, antithyroid drug, amiodarone, lithium, iodine/contrast exposure, biotin, supplement use, missed dose, or recent dose change affecting thyroid interpretation? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism) therapy unsafe.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about thyroid hormone. | Ask specifically about antithyroid drug. | Ask specifically about amiodarone. | Ask specifically about lithium. | Ask specifically about iodine/contrast exposure. | Ask specifically about biotin. | Ask specifically about supplement use. | Ask specifically about missed dose.
9. Any childhood head/neck radiation, therapeutic radiation, occupational exposure, or family thyroid cancer risk factor? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about childhood head/neck radiation. | Ask specifically about therapeutic radiation. | Ask specifically about occupational exposure. | Ask specifically about family thyroid cancer risk factor.
10. Any personal or family history of MEN, VHL, NF1, SDHx-related tumors, medullary thyroid cancer, pheochromocytoma, paraganglioma, or adrenal/pituitary tumors? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about personal. | Ask specifically about family history of MEN. | Ask specifically about SDHx-related tumors. | Ask specifically about medullary thyroid cancer. | Ask specifically about pheochromocytoma. | Ask specifically about paraganglioma. | Ask specifically about adrenal/pituitary tumors.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01_limitation) - Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

### hirsutism_intent_v1: Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) (hirsutism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; options ___ kg/m2 / Underweight / Overweight/obesity range / Unable/not available; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any new or rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth, acne, scalp hair loss, deepening voice, clitoromegaly, or menstrual irregularity? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; details Ask specifically about rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth. | Ask specifically about acne. | Ask specifically about scalp hair loss. | Ask specifically about deepening voice. | Ask specifically about clitoromegaly. | Ask specifically about menstrual irregularity.
2. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018
3. Which medications, supplements, missed doses, biotin, iodine/contrast exposure, hormone therapy, or recent treatment changes could alter Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) interpretation or safety? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) therapy unsafe.; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; details Review missed doses. | Review biotin. | Review iodine/contrast exposure. | Review hormone therapy. | Review recent treatment changes.
4. Are fertility goals, pregnancy possibility, partner factors, or treatment choices relevant to the timing and safety of this endocrine workup? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) therapy unsafe.; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018
5. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) therapy unsafe.; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect acne distribution (exam_01) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; options Absent/minimal / Inflammatory acne / Severe/nodulocystic / Unable/deferred; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect terminal hair distribution (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; options Expected distribution / Mild excess / Marked excess / Virilization concern / Unable/deferred; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_03) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Tumor-range androgens or virilization changes to urgent imaging/endocrine pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Inspect acne distribution (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. 12. Hyperandrogenism/PCOS safety tests: pregnancy test when relevant, total/free testosterone with reliable assay, DHEAS or 17-OHP for mimics, TSH/prolactin as indicated, and A1c/OGTT/lipids for cardiometabolic risk. (test_06_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) test or threshold.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
13. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_07_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) test or threshold.; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
14. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_08_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) test or threshold.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) test or threshold.; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) test or threshold.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

27. Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
28. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
29. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

16. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Hyperandrogenism/PCOS safety tests: pregnancy test when relevant, total/free testosterone with reliable assay, DHEAS or 17-OHP for mimics, TSH/prolactin as indicated, and A1c/OGTT/lipids for cardiometabolic risk. (test_06) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_07) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_08) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Rapid onset, virilization, very high testosterone/DHEAS, pelvic/adrenal mass symptoms. (red_flag_01) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
25. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
26. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Tumor-range androgens or virilization changes to urgent imaging/endocrine pathway. (management_01) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
28. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
29. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
33. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
34. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### hyperaldosteronism_intent_v1: Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) (hyperaldosteronism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; options No orthostatic drop / Orthostatic hypotension / Symptomatic dizziness/presyncope / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any resistant or early-onset hypertension, hypokalemia, cramps or weakness, adrenal incidentaloma, OSA, or medications that could affect the aldosterone-renin ratio (ARR)? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) therapy unsafe.; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; details Ask specifically about resistant. | Ask specifically about early-onset hypertension. | Ask specifically about hypokalemia. | Ask specifically about cramps. | Ask specifically about weakness. | Ask specifically about adrenal incidentaloma.
2. Any muscle cramps, weakness, palpitations, constipation, paresthesias, or paralysis suggesting potassium, calcium, adrenal, or neuromuscular severity? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; details Ask specifically about muscle cramps. | Ask specifically about weakness. | Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about paresthesias. | Ask specifically about paralysis.
3. How much are you drinking and urinating, including polyuria, polydipsia, overnight urination, thirst intensity, access to water, and any dehydration or confusion? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; details Clarify urine volume, nocturia, and urinary frequency. | Ask about thirst intensity and access to water. | Ask about dehydration, confusion, and inability to keep up with intake.
4. Do you feel lightheaded when standing, faint, unusually weak, or better after fluids or salt? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025
5. Have you had new salt craving, dizziness on standing, low blood pressure, vomiting, abdominal pain, or unusual skin darkening? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; details Ask specifically about Have you had new salt craving. | Ask specifically about dizziness on standing. | Ask specifically about low blood pressure. | Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about unusual skin darkening.
6. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025
7. Any abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, poor intake, salt craving, orthostasis, weight loss, or new skin/oral hyperpigmentation suggesting adrenal insufficiency or crisis physiology? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; details Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about nausea/vomiting. | Ask specifically about poor intake. | Ask specifically about salt craving. | Ask specifically about orthostasis. | Ask specifically about weight loss. | Ask specifically about new skin/oral hyperpigmentation.
8. Any oral, injected, inhaled, topical, eye-drop, joint-injection, or supplement steroid exposure? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) therapy unsafe.; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; details Ask specifically about oral. | Ask specifically about injected. | Ask specifically about inhaled. | Ask specifically about topical. | Ask specifically about eye-drop. | Ask specifically about joint-injection. | Ask specifically about supplement steroid exposure.
9. Any personal or family autoimmune thyroid disease, type 1 diabetes, celiac disease, pernicious anemia, adrenal disease, pituitary disease, or polyglandular autoimmune syndrome? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; details Ask specifically about personal. | Ask specifically about family autoimmune thyroid disease. | Ask specifically about type 1 diabetes. | Ask specifically about celiac disease. | Ask specifically about pernicious anemia. | Ask specifically about adrenal disease. | Ask specifically about pituitary disease. | Ask specifically about polyglandular autoimmune syndrome.
10. Any fever, infection symptoms, recent illness, procedure, hospitalization, wound, urinary symptoms, cough, or sick contacts? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) therapy unsafe.; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; details Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about infection symptoms. | Ask specifically about recent illness. | Ask specifically about procedure. | Ask specifically about hospitalization. | Ask specifically about wound. | Ask specifically about urinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about cough.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect and press lower extremity edema (exam_01) - Targets volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; options None / Trace / 1+ / 2+ / 3+ / Asymmetric / Unable to assess; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test chair-rise strength (exam_02) - Targets hypokalemic weakness or functional impairment from mineralocorticoid excess; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets hypokalemia-related arrhythmia, severe hypertension complication, or ECG-relevant cardiac rhythm abnormality; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Suppressed renin with clearly elevated aldosterone changes to PA pathway and targeted therapy/subtyping. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

(management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

### hyperthyroidism_intent_v1: Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) (hyperthyroidism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any palpitations, tremor, heat intolerance, anxiety, insomnia, weight loss, diarrhea, or sweating suggesting thyroid hormone excess or adrenergic physiology? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about heat intolerance. | Ask specifically about anxiety. | Ask specifically about insomnia. | Ask specifically about weight loss. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about sweating.
2. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016
3. Which medications, supplements, missed doses, biotin, iodine/contrast exposure, hormone therapy, or recent treatment changes could alter Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) interpretation or safety? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) therapy unsafe.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Review missed doses. | Review biotin. | Review iodine/contrast exposure. | Review hormone therapy. | Review recent treatment changes.
4. Do you have anterior neck pain or thyroid tenderness, painful swallowing, recent viral illness, fever, or postpartum timing suggesting thyroiditis? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) therapy unsafe.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016
5. Any eye pain, redness, gritty sensation, light sensitivity, bulging eyes, double vision, reduced vision, or trouble closing the eyelids? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about eye pain. | Ask specifically about redness. | Ask specifically about gritty sensation. | Ask specifically about light sensitivity. | Ask specifically about bulging eyes. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about reduced vision. | Ask specifically about trouble closing the eyelids.
6. Any fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, dry skin, hoarse voice, slowed thinking, depression, heavy menses, or weight gain suggesting hypothyroidism? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about fatigue. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about dry skin. | Ask specifically about hoarse voice. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about depression. | Ask specifically about heavy menses.
7. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016
8. Any neck swelling, thyroid pain or tenderness, pressure, hoarseness, trouble swallowing, positional shortness of breath, or rapidly enlarging neck mass? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about neck swelling. | Ask specifically about thyroid pain. | Ask specifically about tenderness. | Ask specifically about pressure. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about trouble swallowing. | Ask specifically about positional shortness of breath. | Ask specifically about rapidly enlarging neck mass.
9. Any childhood head/neck radiation, therapeutic radiation, occupational exposure, or family thyroid cancer risk factor? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about childhood head/neck radiation. | Ask specifically about therapeutic radiation. | Ask specifically about occupational exposure. | Ask specifically about family thyroid cancer risk factor.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01_limitation) - Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. 31. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. 31. Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes. (red_flag_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. Suppressed TSH plus high FT4/T3 confirms overt hyperthyroidism. (management_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. TRAb or uptake pattern changes etiology-specific treatment. (management_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_05) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
39. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
40. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Catalog gaps needing review

### hypogonadism_intent_v1: Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men) (hypogonadism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; options ___ kg/m2 / Underweight / Overweight/obesity range / Unable/not available; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any change in libido, morning erections, erectile function, ejaculation, orgasm, fertility goals, or testicular symptoms? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men) therapy unsafe.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; details Ask specifically about change in libido. | Ask specifically about morning erections. | Ask specifically about erectile function. | Ask specifically about ejaculation. | Ask specifically about orgasm. | Ask specifically about fertility goals. | Ask specifically about testicular symptoms.
2. What was the timing of puberty and sexual development, and were there delayed, incomplete, or abnormal pubertal milestones? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018
3. Any breast tenderness, discrete mass, nipple discharge, testicular pain or mass, fertility change, libido change, medication exposure, liver/kidney disease, alcohol, or cannabis use? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men) therapy unsafe.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; details Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about discrete mass. | Ask specifically about nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about testicular pain. | Ask specifically about mass. | Ask specifically about fertility change. | Ask specifically about libido change. | Ask specifically about medication exposure.
4. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men) therapy unsafe.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
5. Any hot flashes, night sweats, sleep disruption, vaginal/genitourinary symptoms, mood change, cycle change, or fertility goal affecting reproductive endocrine management? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men) therapy unsafe.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; details Ask specifically about hot flashes. | Ask specifically about night sweats. | Ask specifically about sleep disruption. | Ask specifically about vaginal/genitourinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about mood change. | Ask specifically about cycle change. | Ask specifically about fertility goal.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Measure testicular volume (exam_01) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; options Expected volume / Small bilaterally / Asymmetric/mass concern / Unable/deferred; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect breast tissue (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; options No glandular tissue / Tender glandular tissue / Discrete mass / Nipple discharge/skin change / Unable/deferred; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect body hair distribution (exam_03) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; options Expected distribution / Reduced androgen-dependent hair / Excess/virilizing pattern / Unable/deferred; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Fertility plans change away from exogenous testosterone; pituitary signs trigger MRI/endocrine referral. Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure testicular volume (exam_01_limitation) - Measure testicular volume is supportive for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect breast tissue (exam_02_limitation) - Inspect breast tissue is supportive for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect body hair distribution (exam_03_limitation) - Assess body hair distribution is supportive for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Morning total testosterone on two occasions. (test_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men) test or threshold.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

(test_06_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men) test or threshold.; source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

Gonadal axis safety tests: pregnancy test when possible, prolactin/TSH, LH/FSH with estradiol or repeated early-morning testosterone, and targeted androgen, semen, bone, or metabolic testing based on syndrome and fertility goals. (test_06) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_07) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_08) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. New severe headache/vision loss, very low T with low/normal LH/FSH, testicular mass, infertility priority. (red_flag_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
25. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
26. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Fertility plans change away from exogenous testosterone; pituitary signs trigger MRI/endocrine referral.
