Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hypoparathyroidism (hypoparathyroidism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypoparathyroidism.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hypoparathyroidism.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any childhood head/neck radiation, therapeutic radiation, occupational exposure, or family thyroid cancer risk factor? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Hypoparathyroidism.; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; details Ask specifically about childhood head/neck radiation. | Ask specifically about therapeutic radiation. | Ask specifically about occupational exposure. | Ask specifically about family thyroid cancer risk factor.
2. Any muscle cramps, weakness, palpitations, constipation, paresthesias, or paralysis suggesting potassium, calcium, adrenal, or neuromuscular severity? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypoparathyroidism needs urgent evaluation today.; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; details Ask specifically about muscle cramps. | Ask specifically about weakness. | Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about paresthesias. | Ask specifically about paralysis.
3. Any perioral numbness, tingling, cramps, tetany, carpopedal spasm, seizure, laryngospasm, or arrhythmia symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypoparathyroidism needs urgent evaluation today.; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; details Ask specifically about perioral numbness. | Ask specifically about tingling. | Ask specifically about cramps. | Ask specifically about tetany. | Ask specifically about carpopedal spasm. | Ask specifically about seizure. | Ask specifically about laryngospasm. | Ask specifically about arrhythmia symptoms.
4. What is the usual calcium and vitamin D intake, supplement use, diet pattern, and sun exposure? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hypoparathyroidism therapy unsafe.; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022
5. Any kidney stones, nephrocalcinosis, hematuria, flank pain, recurrent UTIs, polyuria, dehydration, or renal-function changes affecting calcium/PTH interpretation? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypoparathyroidism needs urgent evaluation today.; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; details Ask specifically about kidney stones. | Ask specifically about nephrocalcinosis. | Ask specifically about hematuria. | Ask specifically about flank pain. | Ask specifically about recurrent UTIs. | Ask specifically about polyuria. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about renal-function changes.
6. Any antiseizure or enzyme-inducing medication use that could affect vitamin D metabolism, bone density, or calcium interpretation? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hypoparathyroidism therapy unsafe.; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022
7. Any kidney stones, constipation, polyuria, polydipsia, bone pain, confusion, dehydration, or reduced kidney function? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypoparathyroidism needs urgent evaluation today.; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; details Ask specifically about kidney stones. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about polyuria. | Ask specifically about polydipsia. | Ask specifically about bone pain. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about reduced kidney function.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Elicit Trousseau sign (exam_06_limitation) - Elicit Trousseau sign is supportive for Hypoparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

18. DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. 19. 25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults. 31. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hypoparathyroidism.; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
32. Important mimics/exclusions: primary versus secondary or tertiary parathyroid disease, malignancy-associated calcium disorder, renal disease, vitamin D deficiency or excess, medication effects, malabsorption, osteomalacia, osteoporosis, and acute fracture. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hypoparathyroidism.; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### hypopituitarism_intent_v1: Hypopituitarism

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hypopituitarism (hypopituitarism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypopituitarism.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hypopituitarism.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, dry skin, hoarse voice, slowed thinking, depression, heavy menses, or weight gain suggesting hypothyroidism? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; details Ask specifically about fatigue. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about dry skin. | Ask specifically about hoarse voice. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about depression. | Ask specifically about heavy menses.
2. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016
3. How much are you drinking and urinating, including polyuria, polydipsia, overnight urination, thirst intensity, access to water, and any dehydration or confusion? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; details Clarify urine volume, nocturia, and urinary frequency. | Ask about thirst intensity and access to water. | Ask about dehydration, confusion, and inability to keep up with intake.
4. Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
5. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hypopituitarism therapy unsafe.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
6. Have ring size, shoe size, facial features, jaw spacing, sweating, sleep apnea, joint pain, or headaches changed over time? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; details Ask specifically about Have ring size. | Ask specifically about shoe size. | Ask specifically about facial features. | Ask specifically about jaw spacing. | Ask specifically about sweating. | Ask specifically about sleep apnea. | Ask specifically about joint pain. | Ask specifically about headaches changed over time.
7. Any adrenal symptoms such as orthostasis, salt craving, hyperpigmentation, unexplained weight loss, or thyroid symptoms such as palpitations, tremor, heat/cold intolerance, bowel change, or neck swelling? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; details Ask specifically about adrenal symptoms such as orthostasis. | Ask specifically about salt craving. | Ask specifically about hyperpigmentation. | Ask specifically about unexplained weight loss. | Ask specifically about thyroid symptoms such as palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about heat/cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about bowel change.
8. Which medications, supplements, missed doses, biotin, iodine/contrast exposure, hormone therapy, or recent treatment changes could alter Hypopituitarism interpretation or safety? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hypopituitarism therapy unsafe.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; details Review missed doses. | Review biotin. | Review iodine/contrast exposure. | Review hormone therapy. | Review recent treatment changes.
9. Do you have reliable access to water, and are thirst, intake, or mental status limiting safe hydration? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Hypopituitarism.; options Full / Bitemporal deficit / Other field deficit / Unable to assess; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Inspect body hair distribution (exam_03) - Targets hypopituitary phenotype, central adrenal/thyroid/gonadal deficiency clues, or chronic hormone-deficiency severity; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypopituitarism.; options Expected distribution / Reduced androgen-dependent hair / Excess/virilizing pattern / Unable/deferred; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect skin for pallor or dryness (exam_04) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Hypopituitarism.; options No concerning change / Pallor / Dry/coarse skin / Unable to assess; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypopituitarism.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01_limitation) - Test confrontation visual fields is supportive for Hypopituitarism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Pituitary safety tests: check 8 AM cortisol/ACTH before thyroid replacement decisions, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, sodium/osmolality, and MRI/visual-field pathway when mass effect is possible. (test_07_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hypopituitarism test or threshold.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
14. Pituitary-axis panel guided by presentation: AM cortisol/ACTH, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, serum/urine osmolality and sodium when DI is possible. (test_08_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hypopituitarism test or threshold.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

(test_09_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hypopituitarism test or threshold.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hypopituitarism test or threshold.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hypopituitarism test or threshold.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L. (reference_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hypopituitarism test or threshold.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_04_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hypopituitarism test or threshold.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_05_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Hypopituitarism test or threshold.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

22. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. 23. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (red_flag_03_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Hypopituitarism.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
34. Hypopituitarism: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hypopituitarism.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hypopituitarism.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Hypopituitarism.; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

21. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_06) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Pituitary safety tests: check 8 AM cortisol/ACTH before thyroid replacement decisions, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, sodium/osmolality, and MRI/visual-field pathway when mass effect is possible. (test_07) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Pituitary-axis panel guided by presentation: AM cortisol/ACTH, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, serum/urine osmolality and sodium when DI is possible. (test_08) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Pituitary MRI after biochemical evidence or mass-effect symptoms; dynamic testing such as cosyntropin, OGTT/GH suppression, water deprivation/desmopressin or copeptin testing when indicated. (test_09) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. (reference_01) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (reference_02) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L. (reference_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_04) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_05) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
30. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_06) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
31. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_07) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
32. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_08) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
33. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_09) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
34. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_10) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
35. Adrenal crisis, pituitary apoplexy, visual loss, severe hyponatremia. (red_flag_01) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
36. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
37. Pituitary apoplexy, acute severe headache, visual field deficit, cranial nerve palsy, adrenal crisis, severe hypernatremia, inability to drink, or macroadenoma symptoms in pregnancy. (red_flag_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
38. Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation. (management_01) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
40. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
41. Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency. (management_04) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
42. Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
43. Hypopituitarism: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
44. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (differential_02) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
45. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### hypothyroidism_intent_v1: Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid) (hypothyroidism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, dry skin, hoarse voice, slowed thinking, depression, heavy menses, or weight gain suggesting hypothyroidism? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about fatigue. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about dry skin. | Ask specifically about hoarse voice. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about depression. | Ask specifically about heavy menses.
2. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014
3. Any thyroid hormone, antithyroid drug, amiodarone, lithium, iodine/contrast exposure, biotin, supplement use, missed dose, or recent dose change affecting thyroid interpretation? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid) therapy unsafe.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about thyroid hormone. | Ask specifically about antithyroid drug. | Ask specifically about amiodarone. | Ask specifically about lithium. | Ask specifically about iodine/contrast exposure. | Ask specifically about biotin. | Ask specifically about supplement use. | Ask specifically about missed dose.
4. Any palpitations, tremor, heat intolerance, anxiety, insomnia, weight loss, diarrhea, or sweating suggesting thyroid hormone excess or adrenergic physiology? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about heat intolerance. | Ask specifically about anxiety. | Ask specifically about insomnia. | Ask specifically about weight loss. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about sweating.
5. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014
6. Any neck swelling, thyroid pain or tenderness, pressure, hoarseness, trouble swallowing, positional shortness of breath, or rapidly enlarging neck mass? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about neck swelling. | Ask specifically about thyroid pain. | Ask specifically about tenderness. | Ask specifically about pressure. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about trouble swallowing. | Ask specifically about positional shortness of breath. | Ask specifically about rapidly enlarging neck mass.
7. Any childhood head/neck radiation, therapeutic radiation, occupational exposure, or family thyroid cancer risk factor? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about childhood head/neck radiation. | Ask specifically about therapeutic radiation. | Ask specifically about occupational exposure. | Ask specifically about family thyroid cancer risk factor.
8. Any personal or family history of MEN, VHL, NF1, SDHx-related tumors, medullary thyroid cancer, pheochromocytoma, paraganglioma, or adrenal/pituitary tumors? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; details Ask specifically about personal. | Ask specifically about family history of MEN. | Ask specifically about SDHx-related tumors. | Ask specifically about medullary thyroid cancer. | Ask specifically about pheochromocytoma. | Ask specifically about paraganglioma. | Ask specifically about adrenal/pituitary tumors.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate cervical lymph nodes (exam_02) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options No concerning nodes / Tender/mobile nodes / Firm/fixed nodes / Supraclavicular node / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets thyroid-related tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, bradycardia, or heart-failure complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe outstretched-hands tremor (exam_04) - Targets thyrotoxicosis adrenergic signs, medication effect, or endocrine-related neuromuscular irritability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options No tremor / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Functionally limiting tremor / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. High TSH plus low FT4 confirms overt primary hypothyroidism. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism/pituitary pathway. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01_limitation) - Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. ### infertility_intent_v1: Infertility (Hormonal causes)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Infertility (Hormonal causes) (infertility_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options ___ kg/m2 / Underweight / Overweight/obesity range / Unable/not available; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Infertility (Hormonal causes) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024
2. Are cycles predictably ovulatory, with mid-cycle symptoms, LH-kit evidence, luteal symptoms, or cycle tracking suggesting regular ovulation? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Infertility (Hormonal causes) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024
3. Has a semen analysis or partner fertility evaluation been completed, and were any abnormalities found? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Infertility (Hormonal causes) therapy unsafe.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024
4. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Infertility (Hormonal causes) therapy unsafe.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
5. Any palpitations, tremor, heat or cold intolerance, constipation or diarrhea, weight change, fatigue, mood/cognitive change, neck swelling, or eye symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Infertility (Hormonal causes) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; details Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about heat. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about weight change. | Ask specifically about fatigue.
6. Any hirsutism, acne, scalp hair loss, deepening voice, clitoromegaly, rapid virilization, cycle irregularity, or androgenic medication exposure? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Infertility (Hormonal causes) therapy unsafe.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; details Ask specifically about hirsutism. | Ask specifically about acne. | Ask specifically about scalp hair loss. | Ask specifically about deepening voice. | Ask specifically about clitoromegaly. | Ask specifically about rapid virilization. | Ask specifically about cycle irregularity. | Ask specifically about androgenic medication exposure.
7. Any prior pregnancy loss, recurrent loss pattern, infertility evaluation, cycle abnormality, thyroid/prolactin issue, or uterine/ovulatory diagnosis affecting fertility planning? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Infertility (Hormonal causes) therapy unsafe.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; details Ask specifically about prior pregnancy loss. | Ask specifically about recurrent loss pattern. | Ask specifically about infertility evaluation. | Ask specifically about cycle abnormality. | Ask specifically about thyroid/prolactin issue. | Ask specifically about uterine/ovulatory diagnosis.
8. Any new or rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth, acne, scalp hair loss, deepening voice, clitoromegaly, or menstrual irregularity? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; details Ask specifically about rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth. | Ask specifically about acne. | Ask specifically about scalp hair loss. | Ask specifically about deepening voice. | Ask specifically about clitoromegaly. | Ask specifically about menstrual irregularity.
9. Any hot flashes, night sweats, sleep disruption, vaginal/genitourinary symptoms, mood change, cycle change, or fertility goal affecting reproductive endocrine management? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Infertility (Hormonal causes) therapy unsafe.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; details Ask specifically about hot flashes. | Ask specifically about night sweats. | Ask specifically about sleep disruption. | Ask specifically about vaginal/genitourinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about mood change. | Ask specifically about cycle change. | Ask specifically about fertility goal.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect acne distribution (exam_01) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options Absent/minimal / Inflammatory acne / Severe/nodulocystic / Unable/deferred; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect terminal hair distribution (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options Expected distribution / Mild excess / Marked excess / Virilization concern / Unable/deferred; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_03) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect thyroid gland (exam_04) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect for galactorrhea (exam_05) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options Absent / Spontaneous discharge / Expressible discharge / Bloody/unilateral discharge / Unable/deferred; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

1. Anovulation, thyroid/prolactin abnormality, POI pattern, or male factor changes treatment/referral. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

(management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Inspect acne distribution (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Infertility (Hormonal causes), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

15. Infertility hormone safety tests: pregnancy test when relevant, ovulatory timing, TSH/prolactin, LH/FSH with estradiol or testosterone, AMH/ovarian reserve or semen analysis as appropriate, and urgent ectopic/red-flag testing for pain or bleeding. (test_07_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Infertility (Hormonal causes) test or threshold.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_08_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Infertility (Hormonal causes) test or threshold.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_09_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Infertility (Hormonal causes) test or threshold.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Infertility (Hormonal causes) test or threshold.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Infertility (Hormonal causes) test or threshold.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Infertility (Hormonal causes) test or threshold.; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_04_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Infertility (Hormonal causes) test or threshold.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_05_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Infertility (Hormonal causes) test or threshold.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

24. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. 30. Amenorrhea with visual symptoms, POI <40, severe hyperandrogenism, recurrent pregnancy loss needing specialist workup. (red_flag_01_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
33. Infertility (Hormonal causes): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
34. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

24. Infertility hormone safety tests: pregnancy test when relevant, ovulatory timing, TSH/prolactin, LH/FSH with estradiol or testosterone, AMH/ovarian reserve or semen analysis as appropriate, and urgent ectopic/red-flag testing for pain or bleeding. (test_07) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_08) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_09) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
30. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_04) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
31. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_05) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
32. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_06) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
33. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_07) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
34. Amenorrhea with visual symptoms, POI <40, severe hyperandrogenism, recurrent pregnancy loss needing specialist workup. (red_flag_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

(red_flag_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
36. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
37. Anovulation, thyroid/prolactin abnormality, POI pattern, or male factor changes treatment/referral. ### menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency_intent_v1: Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency (menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; source ESHRE_POI_2024
2. Any hot flashes, night sweats, sleep disruption, mood change, vaginal dryness, dyspareunia, urinary symptoms, or sexual-function change? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; source ESHRE_POI_2024; details Ask specifically about hot flashes. | Ask specifically about night sweats. | Ask specifically about sleep disruption. | Ask specifically about mood change. | Ask specifically about vaginal dryness. | Ask specifically about dyspareunia. | Ask specifically about urinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about sexual-function change.
3. Is pregnancy possible now, planned soon, or recently postpartum, and would pregnancy change medication or imaging safety? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency therapy unsafe.; source ESHRE_POI_2024
4. Any chemotherapy, radiation, pelvic/cranial surgery, gonadal surgery, or cancer treatment that could affect endocrine function? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; source ESHRE_POI_2024; details Ask specifically about chemotherapy. | Ask specifically about radiation. | Ask specifically about pelvic/cranial surgery. | Ask specifically about gonadal surgery. | Ask specifically about cancer treatment.
5. Any history of autoimmune or family premature ovarian insufficiency that changes Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; source ESHRE_POI_2024; details Ask specifically about history of autoimmune. | Ask specifically about family premature ovarian insufficiency.
6. Are fertility goals, pregnancy possibility, partner factors, or treatment choices relevant to the timing and safety of this endocrine workup? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency therapy unsafe.; source ESHRE_POI_2024
7. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency therapy unsafe.; source ESHRE_POI_2024; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
