Any new or rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth, acne, scalp hair loss, deepening voice, clitoromegaly, or menstrual irregularity? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; source ESHRE_POI_2024; details Ask specifically about rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth. | Ask specifically about acne. | Ask specifically about scalp hair loss. | Ask specifically about deepening voice. | Ask specifically about clitoromegaly. | Ask specifically about menstrual irregularity.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Inspect acne distribution (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect terminal hair distribution (exam_02_limitation) - Inspect terminal hair distribution is supportive for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect thyroid gland (exam_03_limitation) - Inspect thyroid gland is supportive for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
29. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
30. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; source IMS_MENOPAUSE_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
38. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
39. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source IMS_MENOPAUSE_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### metabolic_syndrome_intent_v1: Metabolic Syndrome

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Metabolic Syndrome (metabolic_syndrome_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Metabolic Syndrome.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Metabolic Syndrome.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Metabolic Syndrome.; options ___ kg/lb / Recent weight gain / Recent weight loss / Unable/not available; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure waist circumference (safety_check_04) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Metabolic Syndrome.; options ___ cm/in / Above risk threshold / Not above risk threshold / Unable/not available; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. How have weight and waist circumference changed over time, and is the change intentional? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Metabolic Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME
2. Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Metabolic Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.
3. What is the usual physical activity level, and has it changed recently because of symptoms, function, or treatment barriers? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Metabolic Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME
4. Any history of ASCVD, heart failure, stroke/TIA, peripheral arterial disease, claudication, or vascular procedures? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Metabolic Syndrome therapy unsafe.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; details Ask specifically about history of ASCVD. | Ask specifically about heart failure. | Ask specifically about stroke/TIA. | Ask specifically about peripheral arterial disease. | Ask specifically about claudication. | Ask specifically about vascular procedures.
5. Any history of PCOS that changes Metabolic Syndrome risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Metabolic Syndrome.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME
6. Any known fatty liver disease, elevated liver enzymes, central adiposity, dyslipidemia, hypertension, insulin resistance, alcohol use, or cardiometabolic treatment history? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Metabolic Syndrome.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; details Ask specifically about known fatty liver disease. | Ask specifically about elevated liver enzymes. | Ask specifically about central adiposity. | Ask specifically about dyslipidemia. | Ask specifically about hypertension. | Ask specifically about insulin resistance. | Ask specifically about alcohol use. | Ask specifically about cardiometabolic treatment history.
7. What blood-pressure, lipid, kidney-protective, or cardiometabolic medications are being used, and are there adherence or adverse-effect concerns? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Metabolic Syndrome therapy unsafe.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME
8. What is the current weight trajectory, and how has it changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Metabolic Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME
9. Any history of prior gestational diabetes that changes Metabolic Syndrome risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Metabolic Syndrome.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_01) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Metabolic Syndrome.; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

1. 3 or more criteria shifts to intensive cardiometabolic risk reduction. Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure waist circumference (safety_check_04_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Metabolic Syndrome, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

25. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Diagnosis commonly requires 3 or more: waist >102 cm men or >88 cm women in ATP III US cut points, TG >=150, HDL <40 men/<50 women, BP >=130/85 or treatment, fasting glucose >=100 or treatment. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. 26. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Metabolic Syndrome.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
27. Physical exam scope for Metabolic Syndrome (metabolic_syndrome_v1_physical_exam_scope) - This workup is intentionally exam-light: diagnosis and risk stratification are driven by guideline laboratory thresholds, pregnancy/medication context, and anthropometrics rather than a broad physical exam. Check basic bedside data and anthropometrics, document the focused insulin-resistance phenotype exam when relevant, and add foot/vascular, hydration/perfusion, abdominal, cardiopulmonary, or neurologic exams only when symptoms, complications, or a second validated intent trigger them.; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to exam-scope cautions; use this row to audit why the workup remains focused rather than comprehensive-by-default.

22. Diagnosis commonly requires 3 or more: waist >102 cm men or >88 cm women in ATP III US cut points, TG >=150, HDL <40 men/<50 women, BP >=130/85 or treatment, fasting glucose >=100 or treatment. Metabolic Syndrome: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Diagnosis commonly requires 3 or more: waist >102 cm men or >88 cm women in ATP III US cut points, TG >=150, HDL <40 men/<50 women, BP >=130/85 or treatment, fasting glucose >=100 or treatment. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. (differential_03) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### osteopenia_intent_v1: Osteopenia

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Osteopenia (osteopenia_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteopenia.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Osteopenia.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any low-trauma fracture, focal bone pain, height loss, falls, gait instability, back pain, glucocorticoid use, or malabsorption risk? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Osteopenia needs urgent evaluation today.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; details Ask specifically about low-trauma fracture. | Ask specifically about focal bone pain. | Ask specifically about height loss. | Ask specifically about falls. | Ask specifically about gait instability. | Ask specifically about back pain. | Ask specifically about glucocorticoid use. | Ask specifically about malabsorption risk.
2. What was the menopause or ovarian-insufficiency timing, and are there fragility fracture, vasomotor, genitourinary, or hormone-therapy considerations? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Osteopenia.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; details Ask specifically about What was the menopause. | Ask specifically about ovarian-insufficiency timing. | Ask specifically about are there fragility fracture. | Ask specifically about vasomotor. | Ask specifically about genitourinary. | Ask specifically about hormone-therapy considerations.
3. Any antiseizure or enzyme-inducing medication use that could affect vitamin D metabolism, bone density, or calcium interpretation? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Osteopenia therapy unsafe.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020
4. Any kidney stones, constipation, polyuria, polydipsia, bone pain, confusion, dehydration, or reduced kidney function? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Osteopenia needs urgent evaluation today.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; details Ask specifically about kidney stones. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about polyuria. | Ask specifically about polydipsia. | Ask specifically about bone pain. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about reduced kidney function.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteopenia.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate focal bone tenderness (exam_02) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteopenia.; options No focal tenderness / Focal tenderness / Diffuse tenderness / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe gait stability if safe (exam_03) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteopenia.; options Stable / Unsteady / Needs assistive device/help / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect spine posture for kyphosis (exam_04) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteopenia.; options No kyphosis/height-loss concern / Kyphosis / Height-loss concern / Unable to assess; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01_limitation) - Assess proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Osteopenia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Palpate focal bone tenderness (exam_02_limitation) - Palpate focal bone tenderness is supportive for Osteopenia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Observe gait stability if safe (exam_03_limitation) - Assess gait stability if safe is supportive for Osteopenia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect spine posture for kyphosis (exam_04_limitation) - Inspect spine posture for kyphosis is supportive for Osteopenia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. DXA. (test_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteopenia test or threshold.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
8. FRAX or validated fracture-risk tool. (test_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteopenia test or threshold.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
10. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_04_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteopenia test or threshold.; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
11. Thyroid safety data: pair TSH/free T4 with syndrome-specific add-ons such as T3, antibodies, ultrasound/FNA, or radionuclide scan, and account for pregnancy, biotin, acute illness, central disease, and medication effects before treatment changes. (test_05_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteopenia test or threshold.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
12. DXA/vertebral imaging or fracture-risk tool when bone density is in scope; calcium/PTH/phosphorus/magnesium/25(OH)D/renal function for calcium-bone disorders. (test_06_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteopenia test or threshold.; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
13. 24-hour urine calcium and renal imaging when primary hyperparathyroidism, kidney stones, or chronic hypoparathyroidism management is relevant. (test_07_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteopenia test or threshold.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
14. Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteopenia test or threshold.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteopenia test or threshold.; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. (reference_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteopenia test or threshold.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. 25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults. (reference_04_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteopenia test or threshold.; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (management_05_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteopenia.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Osteopenia: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Osteopenia.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
28. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Osteopenia.; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
29. Important mimics/exclusions: primary versus secondary or tertiary parathyroid disease, malignancy-associated calcium disorder, renal disease, vitamin D deficiency or excess, medication effects, malabsorption, osteomalacia, osteoporosis, and acute fracture. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Osteopenia.; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

(test_04) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. Thyroid safety data: pair TSH/free T4 with syndrome-specific add-ons such as T3, antibodies, ultrasound/FNA, or radionuclide scan, and account for pregnancy, biotin, acute illness, central disease, and medication effects before treatment changes. (test_05) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. DXA/vertebral imaging or fracture-risk tool when bone density is in scope; calcium/PTH/phosphorus/magnesium/25(OH)D/renal function for calcium-bone disorders. (test_06) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. 24-hour urine calcium and renal imaging when primary hyperparathyroidism, kidney stones, or chronic hypoparathyroidism management is relevant. (test_07) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. (reference_01) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (reference_02) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. (reference_03) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. 25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults. (reference_04) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_05) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Fragility fracture despite osteopenic T-score, rapid bone loss, secondary cause signs. (red_flag_01) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

### osteoporosis_intent_v1: Osteoporosis

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Osteoporosis (osteoporosis_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteoporosis.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Osteoporosis.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any height loss, new back pain, kyphosis, low-trauma fracture, fall, neurologic symptom, or steroid exposure suggesting vertebral compression or bone fragility? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Osteoporosis therapy unsafe.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; details Ask specifically about height loss. | Ask specifically about new back pain. | Ask specifically about kyphosis. | Ask specifically about low-trauma fracture. | Ask specifically about fall. | Ask specifically about neurologic symptom. | Ask specifically about steroid exposure.
2. Any aromatase inhibitor, androgen-deprivation therapy, glucocorticoid, antiseizure medication, PPI, transplant medication, or other bone-active medication exposure? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Osteoporosis therapy unsafe.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; details Ask specifically about aromatase inhibitor. | Ask specifically about androgen-deprivation therapy. | Ask specifically about glucocorticoid. | Ask specifically about antiseizure medication. | Ask specifically about transplant medication. | Ask specifically about other bone-active medication exposure.
3. What was the menopause or ovarian-insufficiency timing, and are there fragility fracture, vasomotor, genitourinary, or hormone-therapy considerations? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Osteoporosis.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; details Ask specifically about What was the menopause. | Ask specifically about ovarian-insufficiency timing. | Ask specifically about are there fragility fracture. | Ask specifically about vasomotor. | Ask specifically about genitourinary. | Ask specifically about hormone-therapy considerations.
4. Any kidney stones, constipation, polyuria, polydipsia, bone pain, confusion, dehydration, or reduced kidney function? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Osteoporosis needs urgent evaluation today.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; details Ask specifically about kidney stones. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about polyuria. | Ask specifically about polydipsia. | Ask specifically about bone pain. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about reduced kidney function.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteoporosis.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate focal bone tenderness (exam_02) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteoporosis.; options No focal tenderness / Focal tenderness / Diffuse tenderness / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe gait stability if safe (exam_03) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteoporosis.; options Stable / Unsteady / Needs assistive device/help / Unable/unsafe to assess; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect spine posture for kyphosis (exam_04) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteoporosis.; options No kyphosis/height-loss concern / Kyphosis / Height-loss concern / Unable to assess; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. T-score <= -2.5 or fragility fracture confirms osteoporosis. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteoporosis.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Hip/vertebral fracture or very low T-score changes to high/very-high-risk pathway. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteoporosis.; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

4. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01_limitation) - Assess proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Palpate focal bone tenderness (exam_02_limitation) - Palpate focal bone tenderness is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Observe gait stability if safe (exam_03_limitation) - Assess gait stability if safe is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Cushing screening with 1-mg DST, late-night salivary cortisol, or 24-hour UFC after excluding exogenous steroids; adrenal imaging follows biochemical framing except emergency/known incidentaloma context. (test_07) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Metanephrine intervals are lab and sampling-position-specific; >3x ULN is highly suggestive. (reference_01) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_02) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, ACS/stroke symptoms, planned surgery without alpha blockade. (red_flag_01) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
28. Adrenal crisis, shock, severe vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyperkalemia/hyponatremia, hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms, psychosis, infection, or VTE. (red_flag_03) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. >3x ULN metanephrines changes to localization/genetic/preoperative alpha-blockade pathway. (management_01) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

35. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Metanephrine intervals are lab and sampling-position-specific; >3x ULN is highly suggestive. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics. (differential_03) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### polycystic_ovary_syndrome_intent_v1: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)

Status: READY

Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) (polycystic_ovary_syndrome_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; options ___ kg/m2 / Underweight / Overweight/obesity range / Unable/not available; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) needs urgent evaluation today.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023
2. Any new or rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth, acne, scalp hair loss, deepening voice, clitoromegaly, or menstrual irregularity? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; details Ask specifically about rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth. | Ask specifically about acne. | Ask specifically about scalp hair loss. | Ask specifically about deepening voice. | Ask specifically about clitoromegaly. | Ask specifically about menstrual irregularity.
3. Any weight change, insulin resistance, acanthosis, dyslipidemia, hypertension, fatty liver, or diabetes risk affecting this endocrine workup? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) needs urgent evaluation today.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; details Ask specifically about weight change. | Ask specifically about insulin resistance. | Ask specifically about acanthosis. | Ask specifically about dyslipidemia. | Ask specifically about hypertension. | Ask specifically about fatty liver. | Ask specifically about diabetes risk.
4. Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) needs urgent evaluation today.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.
5. Are fertility goals, pregnancy possibility, partner factors, or treatment choices relevant to the timing and safety of this endocrine workup? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) therapy unsafe.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023
6. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) therapy unsafe.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
7. Which medications, supplements, missed doses, biotin, iodine/contrast exposure, hormone therapy, or recent treatment changes could alter Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) interpretation or safety? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) therapy unsafe.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; details Review missed doses. | Review biotin. | Review iodine/contrast exposure. | Review hormone therapy. | Review recent treatment changes.

Core physical exam maneuvers

Inspect acne distribution (exam_01) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; options Absent/minimal / Inflammatory acne / Severe/nodulocystic / Unable/deferred; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

26. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Adults: PCOS generally requires 2 of 3 Rotterdam features after excluding mimics: ovulatory dysfunction, hyperandrogenism, PCOM/elevated AMH. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
27. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### prediabetes_intent_v1: Prediabetes

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Prediabetes (prediabetes_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Prediabetes.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Prediabetes.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Prediabetes.; options ___ kg/lb / Recent weight gain / Recent weight loss / Unable/not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure waist circumference (safety_check_04) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Prediabetes.; options ___ cm/in / Above risk threshold / Not above risk threshold / Unable/not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prediabetes needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026
2. What is the usual physical activity level, and has it changed recently because of symptoms, function, or treatment barriers? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prediabetes needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026
3. Any family history of type 2 diabetes, gestational diabetes, premature ASCVD, dyslipidemia, hypertension, or obesity that changes cardiometabolic risk? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Prediabetes.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about family history of type 2 diabetes. | Ask specifically about gestational diabetes. | Ask specifically about premature ASCVD. | Ask specifically about dyslipidemia. | Ask specifically about hypertension. | Ask specifically about obesity.
4. Any history of prior gestational diabetes that changes Prediabetes risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Prediabetes.; source ADA_SOC_2026
5. Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prediabetes needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.
6. Any known fatty liver disease, elevated liver enzymes, central adiposity, dyslipidemia, hypertension, insulin resistance, alcohol use, or cardiometabolic treatment history? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Prediabetes.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about known fatty liver disease. | Ask specifically about elevated liver enzymes. | Ask specifically about central adiposity. | Ask specifically about dyslipidemia. | Ask specifically about hypertension. | Ask specifically about insulin resistance. | Ask specifically about alcohol use. | Ask specifically about cardiometabolic treatment history.
7. Any history of PCOS that changes Prediabetes risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Prediabetes.; source ADA_SOC_2026
8. What is the current weight trajectory, and how has it changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prediabetes needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026
9. Any history of ASCVD, heart failure, stroke/TIA, peripheral arterial disease, claudication, or vascular procedures? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Prediabetes therapy unsafe.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about history of ASCVD. | Ask specifically about heart failure. | Ask specifically about stroke/TIA. | Ask specifically about peripheral arterial disease. | Ask specifically about claudication. | Ask specifically about vascular procedures.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_01) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Prediabetes.; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. A1c 5.7-6.4, FPG 100-125, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 triggers prevention and surveillance. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Prediabetes.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure waist circumference (safety_check_04_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Prediabetes, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. A1c, fasting plasma glucose, or 2-hour 75-g OGTT. (test_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Prediabetes test or threshold.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
7. Lipids, ALT if fatty liver risk, BP/BMI/waist. (test_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Prediabetes test or threshold.; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
8. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Prediabetes test or threshold.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
9. Cardiometabolic safety data: confirm A1c/FPG/OGTT classification, document BP/waist/BMI, and check lipids, kidney function, albuminuria when diabetes-range, and liver/fatty-liver context when treatment selection or risk reduction is in scope. Check basic bedside data and anthropometrics, document the focused insulin-resistance phenotype exam when relevant, and add foot/vascular, hydration/perfusion, abdominal, cardiopulmonary, or neurologic exams only when symptoms, complications, or a second validated intent trigger them.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to exam-scope cautions; use this row to audit why the workup remains focused rather than comprehensive-by-default.
