### primary_hyperparathyroidism_intent_v1: Primary Hyperparathyroidism

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Primary Hyperparathyroidism (primary_hyperparathyroidism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any kidney stones, constipation, polyuria, polydipsia, bone pain, confusion, dehydration, or reduced kidney function? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Primary Hyperparathyroidism needs urgent evaluation today.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; details Ask specifically about kidney stones. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about polyuria. | Ask specifically about polydipsia. | Ask specifically about bone pain. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about reduced kidney function.
2. Which medications, supplements, missed doses, biotin, iodine/contrast exposure, hormone therapy, or recent treatment changes could alter Primary Hyperparathyroidism interpretation or safety? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Primary Hyperparathyroidism therapy unsafe.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; details Review missed doses. | Review biotin. | Review iodine/contrast exposure. | Review hormone therapy. | Review recent treatment changes.
3. Any family history of MEN, familial hypocalciuric hypercalcemia, parathyroid disease, kidney stones, pituitary tumors, pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors, or jaw tumors? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; details Ask specifically about family history of MEN. | Ask specifically about familial hypocalciuric hypercalcemia. | Ask specifically about parathyroid disease. | Ask specifically about kidney stones. | Ask specifically about pituitary tumors. | Ask specifically about pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors. | Ask specifically about jaw tumors.
4. Any antiseizure or enzyme-inducing medication use that could affect vitamin D metabolism, bone density, or calcium interpretation? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Primary Hyperparathyroidism therapy unsafe.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate focal bone tenderness (exam_02) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options No focal tenderness / Focal tenderness / Diffuse tenderness / Unable/unsafe to assess; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe gait stability if safe (exam_03) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options Stable / Unsteady / Needs assistive device/help / Unable/unsafe to assess; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Ca >1 mg/dL above ULN, osteoporosis/fracture, stones, reduced renal function, or age criteria can change to surgical pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Fragility fracture, very low T-score, high fracture risk, or secondary cause changes therapy intensity and fall-prevention urgency. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Calcium-PTH pattern, renal involvement, stones, osteoporosis, or severe calcium symptoms changes endocrine/surgical pathway. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01_limitation) - Assess proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Primary Hyperparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

(reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Primary Hyperparathyroidism test or threshold.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. (reference_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Primary Hyperparathyroidism test or threshold.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. 25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults. (reference_04_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Primary Hyperparathyroidism test or threshold.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. PHPT: high calcium with elevated or inappropriately normal PTH; FHH often Ca/Cr clearance ratio <0.01 but overlap exists. (reference_05_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Primary Hyperparathyroidism test or threshold.; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

Primary Hyperparathyroidism: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
34. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (differential_02) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Important mimics/exclusions: primary versus secondary or tertiary parathyroid disease, malignancy-associated calcium disorder, renal disease, vitamin D deficiency or excess, medication effects, malabsorption, osteomalacia, osteoporosis, and acute fracture. (differential_03) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### prolactinoma_intent_v1: Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) (prolactinoma_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) therapy unsafe.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
2. When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) needs urgent evaluation today.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023
3. How long has conception been attempted, are cycles ovulatory/regular, and have partner semen analysis or prior fertility evaluations found abnormalities? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) therapy unsafe.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
4. Any change in libido, morning erections, erectile function, ejaculation, orgasm, fertility goals, or testicular symptoms? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) therapy unsafe.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; details Ask specifically about change in libido. | Ask specifically about morning erections. | Ask specifically about erectile function. | Ask specifically about ejaculation. | Ask specifically about orgasm. | Ask specifically about fertility goals. | Ask specifically about testicular symptoms.
5. Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) needs urgent evaluation today.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
6. Any dopamine antagonist, antipsychotic, metoclopramide, opioid, estrogen, verapamil, renal disease, hypothyroidism, or supplement exposure that could raise prolactin? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) therapy unsafe.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; details Ask specifically about dopamine antagonist. | Ask specifically about antipsychotic. | Ask specifically about metoclopramide. | Ask specifically about opioid. | Ask specifically about estrogen. | Ask specifically about verapamil. | Ask specifically about renal disease. | Ask specifically about hypothyroidism.
7. How much are you drinking and urinating, including polyuria, polydipsia, overnight urination, thirst intensity, access to water, and any dehydration or confusion? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) needs urgent evaluation today.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; details Clarify urine volume, nocturia, and urinary frequency. | Ask about thirst intensity and access to water. | Ask about dehydration, confusion, and inability to keep up with intake.
8. Have ring size, shoe size, facial features, jaw spacing, sweating, sleep apnea, joint pain, or headaches changed over time? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) needs urgent evaluation today.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; details Ask specifically about Have ring size. | Ask specifically about shoe size. | Ask specifically about facial features. | Ask specifically about jaw spacing. | Ask specifically about sweating. | Ask specifically about sleep apnea. | Ask specifically about joint pain. | Ask specifically about headaches changed over time.
9. Any adrenal symptoms such as orthostasis, salt craving, hyperpigmentation, unexplained weight loss, or thyroid symptoms such as palpitations, tremor, heat/cold intolerance, bowel change, or neck swelling? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) needs urgent evaluation today.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; details Ask specifically about adrenal symptoms such as orthostasis. | Ask specifically about salt craving. | Ask specifically about hyperpigmentation. | Ask specifically about unexplained weight loss. | Ask specifically about thyroid symptoms such as palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about heat/cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about bowel change.
10. Do you have reliable access to water, and are thirst, intake, or mental status limiting safe hydration? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) needs urgent evaluation today.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023

Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).; options Full / Bitemporal deficit / Other field deficit / Unable to assess; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01_limitation) - Test confrontation visual fields is supportive for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

25. Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
26. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).; source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
27. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).; source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

19. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_04) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Prolactin/pituitary mass safety tests: repeat fasting prolactin with macroprolactin, medication, pregnancy, TSH, and renal context, obtain pituitary MRI for persistent or mass-effect concern, and check gonadal/adrenal/thyroid axes for macroadenoma. (test_05) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Pituitary-axis panel guided by presentation: AM cortisol/ACTH, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, serum/urine osmolality and sodium when DI is possible. (test_06) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Pituitary MRI after biochemical evidence or mass-effect symptoms; dynamic testing such as cosyntropin, OGTT/GH suppression, water deprivation/desmopressin or copeptin testing when indicated. (test_07) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Visual field deficit, severe headache/apoplexy, cranial nerve palsy, pregnancy with macroadenoma symptoms. (red_flag_01) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
28. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. Pituitary apoplexy, acute severe headache, visual field deficit, cranial nerve palsy, adrenal crisis, severe hypernatremia, inability to drink, or macroadenoma symptoms in pregnancy. (red_flag_03) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. Mass effect or macroadenoma changes urgency and ophthalmology/neurosurgery involvement. 31. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency. (management_04) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
37. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Basic bedside data / safety checks

Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Any personal or family history of MEN, VHL, NF1, SDHx-related tumors, medullary thyroid cancer, pheochromocytoma, paraganglioma, or adrenal/pituitary tumors? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; details Ask specifically about personal. | Ask specifically about family history of MEN. | Ask specifically about SDHx-related tumors. | Ask specifically about medullary thyroid cancer. | Ask specifically about pheochromocytoma. | Ask specifically about paraganglioma. | Ask specifically about adrenal/pituitary tumors.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

15. Structural thyroid safety workup: use TSH to route radionuclide scan versus FNA pathway, thyroid ultrasound with cervical-node mapping for suspicious nodules/cancer, calcitonin/RET context when medullary cancer or MEN2 is possible, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine decisions. (test_07_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary) test or threshold.; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_08_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary) test or threshold.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. (test_09_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary) test or threshold.; source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. 19. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. 23. Bethesda V/VI, suspicious nodes, calcitonin/CEA elevation, or RET positivity changes surgical/genetic pathway. (management_01_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
24. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
25. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
26. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_04_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
27. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_05_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
28. Airway compromise, vocal cord paralysis, bulky nodes, rapidly enlarging mass, suspected anaplastic transformation. (red_flag_01_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes. (red_flag_03_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
32. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
33. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

(reference_05) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
33. Airway compromise, vocal cord paralysis, bulky nodes, rapidly enlarging mass, suspected anaplastic transformation. (red_flag_01) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
34. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
35. Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes. (red_flag_03) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
36. Bethesda V/VI, suspicious nodes, calcitonin/CEA elevation, or RET positivity changes surgical/genetic pathway. (management_01) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_04) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
40. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_05) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
41. Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
42. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
43. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### thyroid_nodules_intent_v1: Thyroid Nodules (Benign)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Thyroid Nodules (Benign) (thyroid_nodules_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Has the thyroid nodule or neck mass grown rapidly, become painful, fixed, or associated with hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Thyroid Nodules (Benign) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; details Ask specifically about Has the thyroid nodule. | Ask specifically about neck mass grown rapidly. | Ask specifically about become painful. | Ask specifically about fixed. | Ask specifically about associated with hoarseness. | Ask specifically about dysphagia. | Ask specifically about dyspnea. | Ask specifically about suspicious nodes.
2. Any neck swelling, rapid growth, pain, hoarseness, trouble swallowing, positional shortness of breath, radiation exposure, or family thyroid cancer/MEN2 history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Thyroid Nodules (Benign) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; details Ask specifically about neck swelling. | Ask specifically about rapid growth. | Ask specifically about pain. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about trouble swallowing. | Ask specifically about positional shortness of breath. | Ask specifically about radiation exposure. | Ask specifically about family thyroid cancer/MEN2 history.
3. Any childhood head/neck radiation, therapeutic radiation, occupational exposure, or family thyroid cancer risk factor? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; details Ask specifically about childhood head/neck radiation. | Ask specifically about therapeutic radiation. | Ask specifically about occupational exposure. | Ask specifically about family thyroid cancer risk factor.
4. Any personal or family history of MEN, VHL, NF1, SDHx-related tumors, medullary thyroid cancer, pheochromocytoma, paraganglioma, or adrenal/pituitary tumors? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; details Ask specifically about personal. | Ask specifically about family history of MEN. | Ask specifically about SDHx-related tumors. | Ask specifically about medullary thyroid cancer. | Ask specifically about pheochromocytoma. | Ask specifically about paraganglioma. | Ask specifically about adrenal/pituitary tumors.
5. Have you had heat intolerance, palpitations, tremor, anxiety, unintentional weight loss, diarrhea, or increased sweating? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Thyroid Nodules (Benign) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; details Ask specifically about Have you had heat intolerance. | Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about anxiety. | Ask specifically about unintentional weight loss. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about increased sweating.
6. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Thyroid Nodules (Benign) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015
7. Any fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, dry skin, hoarse voice, slowed thinking, depression, heavy menses, or weight gain suggesting hypothyroidism? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Thyroid Nodules (Benign) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; details Ask specifically about fatigue. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about dry skin. | Ask specifically about hoarse voice. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about depression. | Ask specifically about heavy menses.

9. Any neck swelling, thyroid pain or tenderness, pressure, hoarseness, trouble swallowing, positional shortness of breath, or rapidly enlarging neck mass? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Thyroid Nodules (Benign) needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; details Ask specifically about neck swelling. | Ask specifically about thyroid pain. | Ask specifically about tenderness. | Ask specifically about pressure. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about trouble swallowing. | Ask specifically about positional shortness of breath. | Ask specifically about rapidly enlarging neck mass.

Any thyroid hormone, antithyroid drug, amiodarone, lithium, iodine/contrast exposure, biotin, supplement use, missed dose, or recent dose change affecting thyroid interpretation? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Thyroid Nodules (Benign) therapy unsafe.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; details Ask specifically about thyroid hormone. | Ask specifically about antithyroid drug. | Ask specifically about amiodarone. | Ask specifically about lithium. | Ask specifically about iodine/contrast exposure. | Ask specifically about biotin. | Ask specifically about supplement use. | Ask specifically about missed dose.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01_limitation) - Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Thyroid Nodules (Benign), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

10. High-quality thyroid ultrasound with risk stratification. (test_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Nodules (Benign) test or threshold.; source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
11. FNA when indicated by size/risk system; low TSH prompts radionuclide scan. (test_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Nodules (Benign) test or threshold.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
12. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_04_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Nodules (Benign) test or threshold.; source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
13. Structural thyroid safety workup: use TSH to route radionuclide scan versus FNA pathway, thyroid ultrasound with cervical-node mapping for suspicious nodules/cancer, calcitonin/RET context when medullary cancer or MEN2 is possible, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine decisions. (test_05_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Nodules (Benign) test or threshold.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
14. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_06_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Nodules (Benign) test or threshold.; source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. (test_07_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Nodules (Benign) test or threshold.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Nodules (Benign) test or threshold.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

(reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Nodules (Benign) test or threshold.; source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyroid Nodules (Benign) test or threshold.; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. FNA size thresholds are risk-system-specific; high-risk nodules are sampled at smaller sizes than low-risk nodules. 23. Structural thyroid safety workup: use TSH to route radionuclide scan versus FNA pathway, thyroid ultrasound with cervical-node mapping for suspicious nodules/cancer, calcitonin/RET context when medullary cancer or MEN2 is possible, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine decisions. (test_05) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_06) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. (test_07) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

### thyrotoxicosis_intent_v1: Thyrotoxicosis

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Thyrotoxicosis (thyrotoxicosis_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyrotoxicosis.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Thyrotoxicosis.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Do you have anterior neck pain or thyroid tenderness, painful swallowing, recent viral illness, fever, or postpartum timing suggesting thyroiditis? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Thyrotoxicosis therapy unsafe.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016
2. Any thyroid hormone, antithyroid drug, amiodarone, lithium, iodine/contrast exposure, biotin, supplement use, missed dose, or recent dose change affecting thyroid interpretation? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Thyrotoxicosis therapy unsafe.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about thyroid hormone. | Ask specifically about antithyroid drug. | Ask specifically about amiodarone. | Ask specifically about lithium. | Ask specifically about iodine/contrast exposure. | Ask specifically about biotin. | Ask specifically about supplement use. | Ask specifically about missed dose.
3. Any palpitations, tremor, heat intolerance, anxiety, insomnia, weight loss, diarrhea, or sweating suggesting thyroid hormone excess or adrenergic physiology? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Thyrotoxicosis needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about heat intolerance. | Ask specifically about anxiety. | Ask specifically about insomnia. | Ask specifically about weight loss. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about sweating.
4. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Thyrotoxicosis needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016
5. Any fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, dry skin, hoarse voice, slowed thinking, depression, heavy menses, or weight gain suggesting hypothyroidism? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Thyrotoxicosis needs urgent evaluation today.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; details Ask specifically about fatigue. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about dry skin. | Ask specifically about hoarse voice. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about depression. | Ask specifically about heavy menses.
6. Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyrotoxicosis.; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate cervical lymph nodes (exam_02) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyrotoxicosis.; options No concerning nodes / Tender/mobile nodes / Firm/fixed nodes / Supraclavicular node / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets thyroid-related tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, bradycardia, or heart-failure complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyrotoxicosis.; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe outstretched-hands tremor (exam_04) - Targets thyrotoxicosis adrenergic signs, medication effect, or endocrine-related neuromuscular irritability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyrotoxicosis.; options No tremor / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Functionally limiting tremor / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect eyes for proptosis (exam_05) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Thyrotoxicosis.; options Absent / Mild / Marked / Vision/exposure concern / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Test extraocular movements (exam_06) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Thyrotoxicosis.; options Full / Restricted / Diplopia / Painful / Unable to assess; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Low uptake changes management away from antithyroid drugs toward thyroiditis/supportive pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyrotoxicosis.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyrotoxicosis.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyrotoxicosis.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyrotoxicosis.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyrotoxicosis.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01_limitation) - Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Thyrotoxicosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

11. RAIU: high uptake suggests overproduction; low uptake suggests thyroiditis/exogenous/iodine-related in many cases. 12. ESR/CRP if painful thyroid. 13. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyrotoxicosis test or threshold.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
14. Thyrotoxicosis safety data: check TSH, free T4, total T3, TRAb/TSI or RAIU as indicated, ECG for tachyarrhythmia, CBC/liver baseline before antithyroid drugs, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine or teratogenic treatment decisions. (test_06_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyrotoxicosis test or threshold.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_07_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyrotoxicosis test or threshold.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. (test_08_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Thyrotoxicosis test or threshold.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
