30. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Thyrotoxicosis.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
31. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Thyrotoxicosis.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Test extraocular movements (exam_06) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
19. TSH, free T4, T3. (test_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. TRAb/TSI. (test_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. RAIU: high uptake suggests overproduction; low uptake suggests thyroiditis/exogenous/iodine-related in many cases. (test_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. ESR/CRP if painful thyroid. (test_04) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Thyrotoxicosis safety data: check TSH, free T4, total T3, TRAb/TSI or RAIU as indicated, ECG for tachyarrhythmia, CBC/liver baseline before antithyroid drugs, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine or teratogenic treatment decisions. (test_06) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_07) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. (test_08) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
30. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
31. Storm physiology, AF, heart failure, severe weight loss, altered mental status. (red_flag_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
33. Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes. (red_flag_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
34. Low uptake changes management away from antithyroid drugs toward thyroiditis/supportive pathway. (management_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_04) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_05) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Thyrotoxicosis: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
40. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
41. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### type_1_diabetes_mellitus_intent_v1: Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus (type_1_diabetes_mellitus_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___ kg/lb / Recent weight gain / Recent weight loss / Unable/not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. How much are you drinking and urinating, including polyuria, polydipsia, overnight urination, thirst intensity, access to water, and any dehydration or confusion? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Clarify urine volume, nocturia, and urinary frequency. | Ask about thirst intensity and access to water. | Ask about dehydration, confusion, and inability to keep up with intake.
2. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026
3. Any vomiting, abdominal pain, deep or labored breathing, confusion, dehydration, infection symptoms, missed insulin, pump failure, steroid use, or very high glucose/ketones suggesting hyperglycemic crisis? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus therapy unsafe.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about deep. | Ask specifically about labored breathing. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about infection symptoms. | Ask specifically about missed insulin.
4. Any personal or family autoimmune disease, endocrine disease, thyroid cancer/MEN2, adrenal disease, or related inherited condition? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about personal. | Ask specifically about family autoimmune disease. | Ask specifically about endocrine disease. | Ask specifically about thyroid cancer/MEN2. | Ask specifically about adrenal disease. | Ask specifically about related inherited condition.
5. Any fever, infection symptoms, recent illness, procedure, hospitalization, wound, urinary symptoms, cough, or sick contacts? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus therapy unsafe.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about infection symptoms. | Ask specifically about recent illness. | Ask specifically about procedure. | Ask specifically about hospitalization. | Ask specifically about wound. | Ask specifically about urinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about cough.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect feet for ulcers (exam_01) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options No ulcer / Ulcer/wound / Callus/deformity / Infection concern / Unable to assess; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate pedal pulses (exam_02) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options Symmetric/normal / Diminished / Absent / Asymmetric / Unable to assess; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test monofilament sensation (exam_03) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options Protective sensation intact / Reduced / Absent / Unable to assess; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Ketosis plus acidosis requires urgent DKA pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Positive antibodies or low C-peptide supports autoimmune insulin-deficient diabetes. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

(management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Ketosis/acidosis, HHS osmolality, or severe hypoglycemia triggers urgent protocolized treatment rather than routine outpatient adjustment. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Inspect feet for ulcers (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect feet for ulcers is supportive for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Palpate pedal pulses (exam_02_limitation) - Palpate pedal pulses is supportive for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Test monofilament sensation (exam_03_limitation) - Test monofilament sensation is supportive for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. A1c/glucose criteria plus ketones/acid-base if symptomatic. 8. Beta-hydroxybutyrate, BMP, venous/arterial pH, bicarbonate, anion gap. 9. Islet autoantibodies: GAD65, IA-2, ZnT8, insulin Ab if needed; C-peptide with concurrent glucose. (test_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
10. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_04_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
11. Diabetes safety tests: review POC/plasma glucose trend, BMP with potassium/bicarbonate/anion gap and creatinine, beta-hydroxybutyrate or urine ketones when acutely ill, and UACR/lipids for complication-directed therapy. (test_05_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

(test_06_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

(test_07_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
14. A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. (reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. 2-hour 75-g OGTT: normal <140 mg/dL; prediabetes 140-199; diabetes >=200. 18. DKA: diabetes/hyperglycemia plus ketosis plus metabolic acidosis; glucose may be lower in euglycemic DKA. 33. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. ### type_2_diabetes_mellitus_intent_v1: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (type_2_diabetes_mellitus_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___ kg/lb / Recent weight gain / Recent weight loss / Unable/not available; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. How much are you drinking and urinating, including polyuria, polydipsia, overnight urination, thirst intensity, access to water, and any dehydration or confusion? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Clarify urine volume, nocturia, and urinary frequency. | Ask about thirst intensity and access to water. | Ask about dehydration, confusion, and inability to keep up with intake.
2. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026
3. Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
4. Any fever, infection symptoms, recent illness, procedure, hospitalization, wound, urinary symptoms, cough, or sick contacts? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus therapy unsafe.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about infection symptoms. | Ask specifically about recent illness. | Ask specifically about procedure. | Ask specifically about hospitalization. | Ask specifically about wound. | Ask specifically about urinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about cough.
5. Any history of ASCVD, heart failure, stroke/TIA, peripheral arterial disease, claudication, or vascular procedures? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus therapy unsafe.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about history of ASCVD. | Ask specifically about heart failure. | Ask specifically about stroke/TIA. | Ask specifically about peripheral arterial disease. | Ask specifically about claudication. | Ask specifically about vascular procedures.
6. Any numbness, tingling, burning pain, loss of protective sensation, falls, ulcers, or neuropathy diagnosis? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about numbness. | Ask specifically about tingling. | Ask specifically about burning pain. | Ask specifically about loss of protective sensation. | Ask specifically about falls. | Ask specifically about ulcers. | Ask specifically about neuropathy diagnosis.
7. Any known retinopathy, vision changes, eye injections/laser, or overdue dilated eye examination? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about known retinopathy. | Ask specifically about vision changes. | Ask specifically about eye injections/laser. | Ask specifically about overdue dilated eye examination.
8. Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use? - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.
9. Any oral, injected, inhaled, topical, eye-drop, joint-injection, or supplement steroid exposure? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus therapy unsafe.; source ADA_SOC_2026; details Ask specifically about oral. | Ask specifically about injected. | Ask specifically about inhaled. | Ask specifically about topical. | Ask specifically about eye-drop. | Ask specifically about joint-injection. | Ask specifically about supplement steroid exposure.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect feet for ulcers (exam_01) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options No ulcer / Ulcer/wound / Callus/deformity / Infection concern / Unable to assess; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Test monofilament sensation (exam_03) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options Protective sensation intact / Reduced / Absent / Unable to assess; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

Management-changing findings
1. Diabetes-range A1c/FPG/OGTT changes diagnosis and treatment intensity. Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Inspect feet for ulcers (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect feet for ulcers is supportive for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Palpate pedal pulses (exam_02_limitation) - Palpate pedal pulses is supportive for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Test monofilament sensation (exam_03_limitation) - Test monofilament sensation is supportive for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

31. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. 32. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. (differential_03_limitation) - This diagnostic frame is a safety check against premature closure; it does not confirm or exclude disease without the linked history, exam, tests, and longitudinal context for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
40. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. (differential_02) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
41. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. (differential_03) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia_intent_v1: Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia (vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Do you have trouble rising from a chair, climbing stairs, lifting overhead, or getting up from the floor? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; source ENDO_VITD_2024
2. Any malabsorption, bariatric surgery, inflammatory bowel disease, celiac disease, pancreatic disease, or chronic diarrhea affecting nutrient or medication absorption? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia therapy unsafe.; source ENDO_VITD_2024; details Ask specifically about malabsorption. | Ask specifically about bariatric surgery. | Ask specifically about inflammatory bowel disease. | Ask specifically about celiac disease. | Ask specifically about pancreatic disease. | Ask specifically about chronic diarrhea.
3. Any chronic kidney disease, liver disease, malabsorption, bariatric surgery, or other condition that could alter vitamin D, calcium, phosphorus, or PTH interpretation? - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; source ENDO_VITD_2024; details Ask specifically about chronic kidney disease. | Ask specifically about liver disease. | Ask specifically about malabsorption. | Ask specifically about bariatric surgery. | Ask specifically about other condition.
4. Any antiseizure or enzyme-inducing medication use that could affect vitamin D metabolism, bone density, or calcium interpretation? - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia therapy unsafe.; source ENDO_VITD_2024

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

16. Thyroid safety data: pair TSH/free T4 with syndrome-specific add-ons such as T3, antibodies, ultrasound/FNA, or radionuclide scan, and account for pregnancy, biotin, acute illness, central disease, and medication effects before treatment changes. (test_04) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. DXA/vertebral imaging or fracture-risk tool when bone density is in scope; calcium/PTH/phosphorus/magnesium/25(OH)D/renal function for calcium-bone disorders. (test_05) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. 24-hour urine calcium and renal imaging when primary hyperparathyroidism, kidney stones, or chronic hypoparathyroidism management is relevant. (test_06) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. (reference_01) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (reference_02) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. (reference_03) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. 25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults. (reference_04) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Osteomalacia pattern can include low/normal calcium, low phosphorus, high alkaline phosphatase, and secondary high PTH. (reference_05) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_06) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Severe hypocalcemia symptoms, fracture, profound weakness, malabsorption/high-risk state. (red_flag_01) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

27. Hip/vertebral fracture, cord/neurologic symptoms with back pain, severe hypercalcemia with dehydration/AKI/confusion, or symptomatic hypocalcemia with seizure/laryngospasm/arrhythmia. (red_flag_03) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

(management_03) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

(management_04) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Calcium-PTH pattern, renal involvement, stones, osteoporosis, or severe calcium symptoms changes endocrine/surgical pathway. (management_05) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
34. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (differential_02) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Important mimics/exclusions: primary versus secondary or tertiary parathyroid disease, malignancy-associated calcium disorder, renal disease, vitamin D deficiency or excess, medication effects, malabsorption, osteomalacia, osteoporosis, and acute fracture. (differential_03) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
